groups.context_description: Term describes the inward disposition of dread or terror before a threatening force — a fear that collapses resolve and drives flight or paralysis; the commanded opposite of courage

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 20:3 and shall say to them, ‘ Hear , O Israel , today you are drawing near for battle against your enemies : let not your heart faint . Do not fear or panic or be in dread of them ,
Job 31:34 because I stood in great fear of the multitude , and the contempt of families terrified me, so that I kept silence , and did not go out of doors —

groups.context_description: Term describes the inner orientation of holy reverence and awe before God — the fear that is the proper response to divine majesty and holiness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 8:13 But the Lord of hosts , him you shall honor as holy . Let him be your fear , and let him be your dread .

groups.context_description: Term sets a temporal boundary on an inner-being state — marking fury, anger, guilt, endurance, or spiritual renewal as time-bounded, changeable, and having a foreseeable turning point

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 27:44 and stay with him a while , until your brother’s fury turns away —
Job 14:13 Oh that you would hide me in Sheol , that you would conceal me until your wrath be past , that you would appoint me a set time , and remember me !

groups.context_description: Inner moral condition characteristic of humanity — evil inclination of the heart, capacity for wickedness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 6:5 The Lord saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth , and that every intention of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually .
Jer 17:5 Thus says the Lord : “ Cursed is the man who trusts in man and makes flesh his strength , whose heart turns away from the Lord .

groups.context_description: Human frailty and dependence before God — inner limitation and creaturely orientation defining adam

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 2:11 The haughty looks of man shall be brought low , and the lofty pride of men shall be humbled , and the Lord alone will be exalted in that day .
Jer 10:23 I know , O Lord , that the way of man is not in himself, that it is not in man who walks to direct his steps .

groups.context_description: Humanity as object of God's purposeful action — created in God's image, known, called to reflect divine character

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 1:26 Then God said , “Let us make man in our image , after our likeness . And let them have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the heavens and over the livestock and over all the earth and over every creeping thing that creeps on the earth .”
Mic 6:8 He has told you , O man , what is good ; and what does the Lord require of you but to do justice , and to love kindness , and to walk humbly with your God ?

groups.context_description: Specific inner dispositions of individual persons as representative human qualities — meekness, wisdom, contrition, humility

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 12:3 Now the man Moses was very meek , more than all people who were on the face of the earth .
Isa 29:19 The meek shall obtain fresh joy in the Lord , and the poor among mankind shall exult in the Holy One of Israel .

groups.context_description: Term names the acute inner distress of the person facing suffering and death — the troubled, anguished state of the inner being at the limit of what it can bear

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 26:37 And taking with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee , he began to be sorrowful and troubled .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner distress arising from relational concern — the person troubled in themselves because of the impact of their condition on those they love

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 2:26 for he has been longing for you all and has been distressed because you heard that he was ill .

groups.context_description: Unrighteousness as inner moral condition — the state of suppressing truth and yielding to wrongdoing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:18 For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men , who by their unrighteousness suppress the truth .

groups.context_description: Wrongdoing and suffering wrong — the inner moral act and its bearing on the one wronged and the wrongdoer

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:7 To have lawsuits at all with one another is already a defeat for you . Why not rather suffer wrong ? Why not rather be defrauded ?

groups.context_description: Term names the delicacy or delight as what gives the inner person deep satisfaction — the luxurious good that is held as precious and whose loss constitutes inner deprivation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 36:8 They feast on the abundance of your house , and you give them drink from the river of your delights .

groups.context_description: Voluptuous pleasure-loving as an inner orientation of self-sufficient pride

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 47:8 Now therefore hear this , you lover of pleasures , who sit securely , who say in your heart , “ I am, and there is no one besides me; I shall not sit as a widow or know the loss of children ”:

groups.context_description: Delighting in God's goodness — inner joy in divine provision

anchor_verses.verse_text: Neh 9:25 And they captured fortified cities and a rich land , and took possession of houses full of all good things , cisterns already hewn , vineyards , olive orchards and fruit trees in abundance . So they ate and were filled and became fat and delighted themselves in your great goodness .

groups.context_description: Term names delight or pleasure as an expected or lost inner experience — the satisfaction of the body and inner person, whether anticipated with longing or mourned in its absence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 36:8 They feast on the abundance of your house , and you give them drink from the river of your delights .

groups.context_description: Delight and its loss — where delicacies represent the satisfaction of the inner person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 29:17 Discipline your son , and he will give you rest ; he will give delight to your heart .

groups.context_description: Term names the deliberate inner act of leaving — forsaking possessions, family, or prior obligations for the sake of discipleship — as the outward expression of an inner reordering of priorities

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 10:29 Jesus said , “ Truly , I say to you , there is no one who has left house or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or lands , for my sake and for the gospel ,

groups.context_description: Term names the abandonment of what is owed — leaving a spouse, neglecting the weightier matters of the law, or forsaking first love — as an inner failure of loyalty and relational commitment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 2:4 But I have this against you , that you have abandoned the love you had at first .

groups.context_description: Term names the relational priority inherent in the leave/stay act — leaving a gift to seek reconciliation first, leaving the many for the one, the Father's not leaving the Son alone

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 5:24 leave your gift there before the altar and go . First be reconciled to your brother , and then come and offer your gift .

groups.context_description: Term names God's act of releasing a person from the guilt and penalty of sin — the vertical forgiveness that restores the inner standing of the person before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 7:47 Therefore I tell you , her sins , which are many , are forgiven — for she loved much . But he who is forgiven little , loves little .”

groups.context_description: Term names the human act of forgiving another — the horizontal release of an offender from the inner claim of the offended, commanded as the pattern for those who have received divine forgiveness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 18:35 So also my heavenly Father will do to every one of you , if you do not forgive your brother from your heart .”

groups.context_description: Term names the act of permitting or granting access as an inner-being posture — the receptivity, welcome, or restraint that reflects an inner orientation of priority, reverence, or obedience

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 10:14 But when Jesus saw it, he was indignant and said to them , “ Let the children come to me ; do not hinder them , for to such belongs the kingdom of God .

groups.context_description: Term names the withholding of permission as an act reflecting the inner-being condition of the actor — whether as spiritual authority, obstruction of others, or the inner abandonment of proper orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 23:13 “ But woe to you , scribes and Pharisees , hypocrites ! For you shut the kingdom of heaven in people’s faces . For you neither enter yourselves nor allow those who would enter to go in .

groups.context_description: Term names forgiveness of sins as the spiritual transaction through which the inner person is released from guilt and restored to right standing before God — received through repentance, faith, and Christ's atoning blood

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 1:7 In him we have redemption through his blood , the forgiveness of our trespasses , according to the riches of his grace ,

groups.context_description: Term names the absolute limit of forgiveness — the unforgivable sin against the Holy Spirit, and the conditions under which forgiveness is withheld or inaccessible

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 3:29 but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness , but is guilty of an eternal sin ” —

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of foolishness as failure to perceive and attend to spiritual realities — blindness to what truly matters before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 12:20 But God said to him , ‘ Fool ! This night your soul is required of you , and the things you have prepared , whose will they be ?’

groups.context_description: Term names the inner posture of self-boasting folly — speaking and presenting oneself outside the orientation of God's wisdom

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 12:11 I have been a fool ! You forced me to it, for I ought to have been commended by you . For I was not at all inferior to these super - apostles , even though I am nothing .

groups.context_description: Term names foolishness as a disposition of the inner person — a mode of self-presentation and reasoning that departs from the wisdom and orientation of God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 7:22 coveting , wickedness , deceit , sensuality , envy , slander , pride , foolishness .

groups.context_description: Term names the eschatological transformation of the person — the putting on of incorruptibility and immortality at resurrection

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 15:42 So is it with the resurrection of the dead . What is sown is perishable ; what is raised is imperishable .
1Cor 15:53 For this perishable body must put on the imperishable , and this mortal body must put on immortality .

groups.context_description: Term names incorruptibility as the quality of inner love and the inner orientation of those who seek God's glory

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 2:7 to those who by patience in well-doing seek for glory and honor and immortality , he will give eternal life ;
Eph 6:24 Grace be with all who love our Lord Jesus Christ with love incorruptible .

groups.context_description: Term names muteness — the silence of the Servant in suffering, the emptiness of idols that cannot speak, and the irony of an animal rebuking human madness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 8:32 Now the passage of the Scripture that he was reading was this : “ Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent , so he opens not his mouth .
1Cor 12:2 You know that when you were pagans you were led astray to mute idols , however you were led .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner state of exultant joy — the surpassing gladness that arises in the presence of or in response to God's salvation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 1:44 For behold , when the sound of your greeting came to my ears , the baby in my womb leaped for joy .
Heb 1:9 You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness ; therefore God , your God , has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond your companions .”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of exultant rejoicing — the expressive overflow of deep inner joy, especially before God or in response to his work

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 1:47 and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior ,
1Pe 1:8 Though you have not seen him , you love him. Though you do not now see him, you believe in him and rejoice with joy that is inexpressible and filled with glory ,

groups.context_description: Term names indignation as an inner response to wrong — the rising of righteous anger produced by godly grief and repentance

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 7:11 For see what earnestness this godly grief has produced in you , but also what eagerness to clear yourselves, what indignation , what fear , what longing , what zeal , what punishment ! At every point you have proved yourselves innocent in the matter .

groups.context_description: Term names Christ as the Father's beloved — the unique inner-relationship of love between Father and Son expressed in the divine declaration at baptism and transfiguration

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 3:17 and behold , a voice from heaven said , “ This is my beloved Son , with whom I am well pleased .”
Mar 12:6 He had still one other, a beloved son . Finally he sent him to them , saying , ‘They will respect my son .’

groups.context_description: Term names the believer as beloved — addressed as the cherished object of God's and the community's love, called to live and act from that identity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:7 To all those in Rome who are loved by God and called to be saints : Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ .
1Th 2:8 So , being affectionately desirous of you , we were ready to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own selves , because you had become very dear to us .

groups.context_description: Term names God's love as the inner source and ground of all love — poured into hearts, demonstrated in Christ's death, inseparable from the believer

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 15:13 Greater love has no one than this , that someone lay down his life for his friends .
Rom 5:5 and hope does not put us to shame , because God’s love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit who has been given to us .

groups.context_description: Term names love as the defining inner virtue of the believer — the fruit of the Spirit, the greatest commandment, the fulfilment of the law, that which never ends

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 13:4 Love is patient and kind ; love does not envy or boast ; it is not arrogant
Gal 5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love , joy , peace , patience , kindness , goodness , faithfulness ,

groups.context_description: Term names mutual love among believers as the communal inner orientation — the mark of discipleship, the bond of the community

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 13:35 By this all people will know that you are my disciples , if you have love for one another .”
1Th 3:12 and may the Lord make you increase and abound in love for one another and for all , as we do for you ,

groups.context_description: Term names love of God or Christ as the fundamental inner orientation of the person — the love commanded of the whole heart, soul, mind, and strength

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 22:37 And he said to him , “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind .
Joh 14:15 “ If you love me , you will keep my commandments .

groups.context_description: Term names God's or Christ's love toward the person — the divine inner act of love that precedes and enables all human love

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 3:16 “ For God so loved the world , that he gave his only Son , that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life .

Gal 2:20 I have been crucified with Christ . It is no longer I who live , but Christ who lives in me . And the life I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God , who loved me and gave himself for me .

groups.context_description: Term names love of neighbour and enemy — the inner-being orientation outward that fulfils the law

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 5:44 But I say to you , Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you ,

Luk 6:35 But love your enemies , and do good , and lend , expecting nothing in return , and your reward will be great , and you will be sons of the Most High , for he is kind to the ungrateful and the evil .

groups.context_description: Term names mutual love among believers and within marriage — the inner relational orientation that marks discipleship and covenant

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 13:34 A new commandment I give to you , that you love one another : just as I have loved you , you also are to love one another .
1Jo 4:19 We love because he first loved us .

groups.context_description: Term names disordered love — love directed toward the world, darkness, money, or self-glory as the defining inner orientation of the fallen or departing person

2Ti 4:10 For Demas , in love with this present world , has deserted me and gone to Thessalonica . Crescens has gone to Galatia , Titus to Dalmatia .

groups.context_description: Term names the doing of good as the inner-being disposition and practice that characterises those who belong to God — expressed in love for enemies, endurance through unjust suffering, and righteous conduct before others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 6:35 But love your enemies , and do good , and lend , expecting nothing in return , and your reward will be great , and you will be sons of the Most High , for he is kind to the ungrateful and the evil .
3Jo 11 Beloved , do not imitate evil but imitate good . Whoever does good is from God ; whoever does evil has not seen God .

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 6:18 They are to do good , to be rich in good works , to be generous and ready to share ,

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Pe 4:19 Therefore let those who suffer according to God’s will entrust their souls to a faithful Creator while doing good .

groups.context_description: Term names the person characterised by doing good — a moral-character designation that reflects an inner orientation visible in conduct and recognised by others

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Pe 2:14 or to governors as sent by him to punish those who do evil and to praise those who do good .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of not loving good — an inner-being orientation that characterises the person given over to vice, as part of a catalogue of moral darkness

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ti 3:3 heartless , unappeasable , slanderous , without self-control , brutal , not loving good ,

groups.context_description: Term names goodness as the exclusive and defining attribute of God's inner being — the standard by which all human goodness is measured and found derivative

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 10:18 And Jesus said to him , “ Why do you call me good ? No one is good except God alone .

groups.context_description: Term names goodness as the inner character quality of the person — the good heart, good conscience, good treasure — from which good action necessarily flows

groups.context_description: Term names goodness as an inner-being disposition and Spirit-produced fruit — a quality that fills the person and is completed by God, expressed in righteous conduct and resolve

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gal 5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love , joy , peace , patience , kindness , goodness , faithfulness ,

groups.context_description: Term names lust or erotic desire as an inner disposition — the inner appetite that shapes speech and outward conduct while the heart remains elsewhere

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 33:31 And they come to you as people come , and they sit before you as my people , and they hear what you say but they will not do it; for with lustful talk in their mouths they act ; their heart is set on their gain .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of lust — the corrupted desire that drives spiritual adultery and moral ruin

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 23:11 “Her sister Oholibah saw this, and she became more corrupt than her sister in her lust and in her whoring , which was worse than that of her sister .

groups.context_description: Term names the anguish of supreme inner crisis driving intensified prayer and somatic manifestation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 22:44 And being in agony he prayed more earnestly ; and his sweat became like great drops of blood falling down to the ground .

groups.context_description: Term names the anguish of supreme inner crisis driving intensified prayer and somatic manifestation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 22:44 And being in agony he prayed more earnestly ; and his sweat became like great drops of blood falling down to the ground .

groups.context_description: Term names God's love for his people — the covenantal inner disposition of steadfast love that persists through and exceeds all human response

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 31:3 the Lord appeared to him from far away . I have loved you with an everlasting love ; therefore I have continued my faithfulness to you.
Zep 3:17 The Lord your God is in your midst , a mighty one who will save ; he will rejoice over you with gladness ; he will quiet you by his love ; he will exult over you with loud singing .

groups.context_description: Term names the intense mutual love between persons — including Jonathan's love for David and the burning love of the Song

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 1:26 I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan ; very pleasant have you been to me; your love to me was extraordinary , surpassing the love of women .
Song 8:6 Set me as a seal upon your heart , as a seal upon your arm , for love is strong as death , jealousy is fierce as the grave . Its flashes are flashes of fire , the very flame of the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names love as moral loyalty and kindness — the inner orientation of faithfulness between persons that mirrors divine chesed

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 10:12 Hatred stirs up strife , but love covers all offenses .

Jer 2:2 “ Go and proclaim in the hearing of Jerusalem , Thus says the Lord , “I remember the devotion of your youth , your love as a bride , how you followed me in the wilderness , in a land not sown .

groups.context_description: Term names the lover — either as the rightful object of marital delight, or the hired substitute marking spiritual infidelity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 5:19 a lovely deer , a graceful doe . Let her breasts fill you at all times with delight; be intoxicated always in her love .
Hos 8:9 For they have gone up to Assyria , a wild donkey wandering alone ; Ephraim has hired lovers .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-sourced praise that comes from God — the true commendation that reaches the hidden life of the heart, not external human approval

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 2:29 But a Jew is one inwardly, and circumcision is a matter of the heart , by the Spirit , not by the letter . His praise is not from man but from God .

1Cor 4:5 Therefore do not pronounce judgment before the time , before the Lord comes , who will bring to light the things now hidden in darkness and will disclose the purposes of the heart . Then each one will receive his commendation from God .

groups.context_description: Term names the doxological purpose orientation — all of life directed toward the praise of God's glory

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 1:12 so that we who were the first to hope in Christ might be to the praise of his glory .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner orientation of the mind toward what is praiseworthy — thinking on praiseworthy things as a discipline of the inner life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 4:8 Finally , brothers , whatever is true , whatever is honorable , whatever is just , whatever is pure , whatever is lovely , whatever is commendable , if there is any excellence , if there is anything worthy of praise , think about these things .

groups.context_description: Term names praise as the universal inner-being response to God — the call to all peoples to orient themselves toward God in vocal exaltation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 19:5 And from the throne came a voice saying , “ Praise our God , all you his servants , you who fear him , small and great .”

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 21:16 and they said to him , “Do you hear what these are saying ?” And Jesus said to them , “ Yes ; have you never read , “‘Out of the mouth of infants and nursing babies you have prepared praise ’?”

groups.context_description: Term names praise as a sacrificial inner-being act — the offering of the inner life to God expressed through deliberate vocal acknowledgement

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 13:15 Through him then let us continually offer up a sacrifice of praise to God , that is , the fruit of lips that acknowledge his name .

groups.context_description: Term names the call to extol God — the inner-being orientation of all peoples toward God in praise

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 15:11 And again , “ Praise the Lord , all you Gentiles , and let all the peoples extol him .”

groups.context_description: Term names shame as an inner state that either inhibits action or is overcome by inner confidence and hope — the person choosing not to be ashamed as an act of inner identity confidence

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Pe 4:16 Yet if anyone suffers as a Christian , let him not be ashamed , but let him glorify God in that name .

groups.context_description: Term names shame as an inner state of disgrace and vulnerability — experienced in social reversal, chosen through suffering, perverted as grounds for boasting, or avoided through moral integrity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 12:2 looking to Jesus , the founder and perfecter of our faith , who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross , despising the shame , and is seated at the right hand of the throne of God .

groups.context_description: Term names shameful conduct and motivation — where the inner moral standard of shame governs what is done (shameful deeds) and what drives it (shameful gain)

anchor_verses.verse_text: Tit 1:11 They must be silenced , since they are upsetting whole families by teaching for shameful gain what they ought not to teach.

groups.context_description: Term names being put to shame or vindicated as an inner relational state — hope does not shame the one who holds it; faith brings vindication; boastful conduct brings shame

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 5:5 and hope does not put us to shame , because God’s love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit who has been given to us .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner capacity of perception and discernment — the faculty by which love and knowledge translate into practical moral discrimination

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 1:9 And it is my prayer that your love may abound more and more , with knowledge and all discernment ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner faculty of moral discernment — the trained and exercised capacity to distinguish good from evil; discernment as a developed inner capability

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 5:14 But solid food is for the mature , for those who have their powers of discernment trained by constant practice to distinguish good from evil .

groups.context_description: Asking Jesus — the inner-being posture of those who approach Jesus with need or faith

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 12:21 So these came to Philip , who was from Bethsaida in Galilee , and asked him , “ Sir , we wish to see Jesus .”

groups.context_description: Jesus asking the Father — his inner-being orientation of prayer and intercession

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 17:9 I am praying for them . I am not praying for the world but for those whom you have given me , for they are yours .

groups.context_description: Apostolic asking — pastoral concern expressed as appeal

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Th 4:1 Finally , then , brothers , we ask and urge you in the Lord Jesus , that as you received from us how you ought to walk and to please God , just as you are doing, that you do so more and more .

groups.context_description: Forceful demanding desire — the aggressive inner claim of hostile acquisition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 22:31 “ Simon , Simon , behold , Satan demanded to have you , that he might sift you like wheat ,

groups.context_description: Term names the moral ground of a person's standing — the charge, reason, or basis for guilt or innocence, including the declared innocence of Christ and the inner conviction that grounds Paul's suffering without shame

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 19:4 Pilate went out again and said to them , “ See , I am bringing him out to you that you may know that I find no guilt in him .”

groups.context_description: Term names the causal ground of guilt or salvation — the found absence of guilt in Christ and his being the source of eternal salvation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 5:9 And being made perfect , he became the source of eternal salvation to all who obey him ,

groups.context_description: Faith reception — akoē as the channel through which faith, understanding and inner response to the word arrive

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Th 2:13 And we also thank God constantly for this , that when you received the word of God , which you heard from us , you accepted it not as the word of men but as what it really is , the word of God , which is at work in you believers .

groups.context_description: Term names lack of self-control as an inner vulnerability — the absence of inner restraint that creates exposure to temptation

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 7:5 Do not deprive one another , except perhaps by agreement for a limited time , that you may devote yourselves to prayer ; but then come together again , so that Satan may not tempt you because of your lack of self-control .

groups.context_description: Term names intemperance as an inner character vice — the absence of inner governance listed among qualities of moral deterioration

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ti 3:3 heartless , unappeasable , slanderous , without self-control , brutal , not loving good ,

groups.context_description: Negating fear, dread, and dismay — divine oracles and human petitions that qualify the inner state of fear as the term's direct object

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 9:34 Let him take his rod away from me, and let not dread of him terrify me .
Isa 41:10 fear not , for I am with you; be not dismayed , for I am your God ; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous right hand .

groups.context_description: Negating anger and displeasure — petition or command that the inner state of anger be withheld or restrained, directed at God or another person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 45:5 And now do not be distressed or angry with yourselves because you sold me here , for God sent me before you to preserve life .

groups.context_description: Grief and inner pain — being grieved, distressed, or pained at heart

groups.context_description: Term names toil or pain as an inner condition — the experience of laborious distress, whether as a consequence of the Fall, anxious striving, or the moral-spiritual fruit of one's choices

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 3:16 To the woman he said , “I will surely multiply your pain in childbearing ; in pain you shall bring forth children . Your desire shall be for your husband , and he shall rule over you .”
Psa 127:2 It is in vain that you rise up early and go late to rest, eating the bread of anxious toil; for he gives to his beloved sleep .

groups.context_description: Term names pain or grievousness — the inner condition of distress arising from suffering, moral-spiritual weight, or the consequences of life under the curse

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 139:24 And see if there be any grievous way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting !

groups.context_description: Term names the painful toil of cursed existence — the inner-experiential condition of laboring under the Fall, marked by pain, sweat, and the longing for relief

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 3:17 And to Adam he said , “ Because you have listened to the voice of your wife and have eaten of the tree of which I commanded you, ‘You shall not eat of it,’ cursed is the ground because of you; in pain you shall eat of it all the days of your life ;

groups.context_description: Term names slothfulness as an inner disposition that produces stupor and material ruin — inaction consuming the person from within

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 19:15 Slothfulness casts into a deep sleep , and an idle person will suffer hunger .

groups.context_description: Term names the sluggard as a person whose inner desire craves but whose will refuses to act — passivity, inertia, and self-defeating inner contradiction

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 13:4 The soul of the sluggard craves and gets nothing , while the soul of the diligent is richly supplied .

groups.context_description: Term names the sluggard's inner self-deception — rationalisation and inflated self-regard enabling continued avoidance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 26:16 The sluggard is wiser in his own eyes than seven men who can answer sensibly .

groups.context_description: Term names sluggishness as the inner disposition of hesitation that must be overcome in pursuing God's purpose

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 18:9 They said , “ Arise , and let us go up against them, for we have seen the land , and behold, it is very good . And will you do nothing ? Do not be slow to go , to enter in and possess the land .

groups.context_description: Term names idleness as an inner disposition that the person of virtue refuses — its absence marking inner diligence and character

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 31:27 She looks well to the ways of her household and does not eat the bread of idleness .

groups.context_description: Term names contentment as a learned inner disposition — the acquired capacity of the inner person to remain settled and sufficient regardless of external circumstances

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 4:11 Not that I am speaking of being in need , for I have learned , in whatever situation I am , to be content .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner state of contentment and sufficiency — the condition of the person whose inner life is settled and satisfied, neither grasping nor anxious, freed for generous action

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 6:6 But godliness with contentment is great gain ,

groups.context_description: Self-will as inner-being vice — arrogant self-assertion and willful presumption

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Pe 2:10 and especially those who indulge in the lust of defiling passion and despise authority . Bold and willful , they do not tremble as they blaspheme the glorious ones ,

groups.context_description: Term names the exercise of domineering authority in relational/ecclesial context — engaging the inner being through disposition toward authority and submission

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 2:12 I do not permit a woman to teach or to exercise authority over a man ; rather , she is to remain quiet .

groups.context_description: Term names service of God as the directed inner orientation of the person — the wholehearted devotion that God requires and defines covenantal faithfulness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 10:12 “And now , Israel , what does the Lord your God require of you, but to fear the Lord your God , to walk in all his ways , to love him, to serve the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul ,
1Sa 12:20 And Samuel said to the people , “Do not be afraid ; you have done all this evil . Yet do not turn aside from following the Lord , but serve the Lord with all your heart .

groups.context_description: Term names service of idols and false gods as the inner-being act of devotion misdirected — Israel's persistent defection from the Lord

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 3:8 Therefore the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel , and he sold them into the hand of Cushan-rishathaim king of Mesopotamia . And the people of Israel served Cushan-rishathaim eight years .

groups.context_description: Term names the service of God as the central obligation of Israel's life — the commanded inner orientation of devoted service to the Lord as opposed to other gods

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 10:3 So Moses and Aaron went in to Pharaoh and said to him, “ Thus says the Lord , the God of the Hebrews , ‘ How long will you refuse to humble yourself before me? Let my people go , that they may serve me .

1Ch 28:9 “And you , Solomon my son , know the God of your father and serve him with a whole heart and with a willing mind , for the Lord searches all hearts and understands every plan and thought . If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will cast you off forever .

groups.context_description: Term names Levitical priestly service as an enacted inner-being vocation — ministry in the tabernacle/temple as a calling expressing devotion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 8:19 And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the people of Israel , to do the service for the people of Israel at the tent of meeting and to make atonement for the people of Israel , that there may be no plague among the people of Israel when the people of Israel come near the sanctuary .”

groups.context_description: Term names diligent labour as an expression of inner wisdom — the wise person's orientation toward productive work contrasted with the fool's pursuit of worthless things

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 12:11 Whoever works his land will have plenty of bread , but he who follows worthless pursuits lacks sense .

groups.context_description: Term names the burden of service — worship obligations that weigh the worshiper, or human sin that weighs God, revealing the inner weight of the covenantal relationship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 43:24 You have not bought me sweet cane with money , or satisfied me with the fat of your sacrifices . But you have burdened me with your sins ; you have wearied me with your iniquities .

groups.context_description: Term names temple/tabernacle ministry as a vocation expressing the community's inner orientation toward God — ordered service requiring wholehearted devotion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 16:9 is it too small a thing for you that the God of Israel has separated you from the congregation of Israel , to bring you near to himself, to do service in the tabernacle of the Lord and to stand before the congregation to minister to them ,
2Ch 31:21 And every work that he undertook in the service of the house of God and in accordance with the law and the commandments , seeking his God , he did with all his heart , and prospered .

groups.context_description: Term names work as a human vocation and moral measure — the ordinary labour of life, and the fruit of righteous inner orientation producing inner peace

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 32:17 And the effect of righteousness will be peace , and the result of righteousness , quietness and trust forever .

groups.context_description: Term names purposeful action — especially God's acts of wonder and Daniel's act of prayer — naming inner devotion through prayer and divine inner attributes

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 6:10 When Daniel knew that the document had been signed , he went to his house where he had windows in his upper chamber open toward Jerusalem . He got down on his knees three times a day and prayed and gave thanks before his God , as he had done previously .

groups.context_description: Term names service as an inner-being experience — both oppressive bondage producing inward groaning, and devoted service originating in a stirred heart and spirit

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 2:23 During those many days the king of Egypt died , and the people of Israel groaned because of their slavery and cried out for help. Their cry for rescue from slavery came up to God .

Exo 35:21 And they came , everyone whose heart stirred him , and everyone whose spirit moved him , and brought the Lord’s contribution to be used for the tent of meeting , and for all its service , and for the holy garments .

groups.context_description: Term names slavery as an inner-being condition of bondage — experienced as degradation but also as the context for divine faithfulness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ezr 9:9 For we are slaves . Yet our God has not forsaken us in our slavery , but has extended to us his steadfast love before the kings of Persia , to grant us some reviving to set up the house of our God , to repair its ruins , and to give us protection in Judea and Jerusalem .

groups.context_description: Term names human works as objects of divine moral scrutiny — acts whose inner origins are known to God and subject to judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 34:25 Thus , knowing their works , he overturns them in the night , and they are crushed .

groups.context_description: Term names human works as resting in God's hand — linking inner character to outward acts under divine oversight

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:1 But all this I laid to heart , examining it all , how the righteous and the wise and their deeds are in the hand of God . Whether it is love or hate , man does not know ; both are before him.

groups.context_description: Term names the destructive craving of the wicked — desire as an inner force that boasts, grasps, goes unsatisfied, and ultimately brings ruin

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 10:3 For the wicked boasts of the desires of his soul , and the one greedy for gain curses and renounces the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the righteous person's desire — the inner longing that orients toward God or what is genuinely good, and that God hears and fulfils

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 38:9 O Lord , all my longing is before you; my sighing is not hidden from you.

groups.context_description: Term names desire as a primal inner state — the original experience of the inner being drawn toward what appears good or what the appetite craves

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 3:6 So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food , and that it was a delight to the eyes , and that the tree was to be desired to make one wise , she took of its fruit and ate , and she also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate .

groups.context_description: Term names craving or appetite as a disruptive inner force — desire for physical gratification that overrides proper orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 106:14 But they had a wanton craving in the wilderness , and put God to the test in the desert ;

groups.context_description: Term names desire as the governing orientation of the soul — what the inner being inclines toward, craves, or chooses as its object

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 13:4 The soul of the sluggard craves and gets nothing , while the soul of the diligent is richly supplied .

groups.context_description: Term names intense personal longing — the soul expressing deep yearning toward a specific person or object

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 26:9 My soul yearns for you in the night ; my spirit within me earnestly seeks you. For when your judgments are in the earth , the inhabitants of the world learn righteousness .

groups.context_description: Term names the sovereign desire of God — his inner purposive longing or choice expressed as divine will toward its object

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 132:13 For the Lord has chosen Zion ; he has desired it for his dwelling place :

groups.context_description: Term names the inner twisting or perversion — the bowing down of the person under suffering, or the moral warping of the way away from God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 12:8 A man is commended according to his good sense , but one of twisted mind is despised .

groups.context_description: Term names the exclamatory expression of woe and pain — the inner experience of anguish finding voice in the cry woe!

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 23:29 Who has woe ? Who has sorrow ? Who has strife ? Who has complaining ? Who has wounds without cause ? Who has redness of eyes ?

groups.context_description: Term names mourning as an inner-relational response to loss — the condition of grief expressed in ritual practice, personal weeping, and communal lamentation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:2 It is better to go to the house of mourning than to go to the house of feasting , for this is the end of all mankind , and the living will lay it to heart .

Isa 61:3 to grant to those who mourn in Zion — to give them a beautiful headdress instead of ashes , the oil of gladness instead of mourning , the garment of praise instead of a faint spirit ; that they may be called oaks of righteousness , the planting of the Lord , that he may be glorified .

groups.context_description: Iniquity and wickedness as inner moral quality — conceived, spoken, enacted

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 22:8 Whoever sows injustice will reap calamity , and the rod of his fury will fail .

groups.context_description: Trouble and toil as an experienced inner condition of life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 90:10 The years of our life are seventy , or even by reason of strength eighty ; yet their span is but toil and trouble ; they are soon gone , and we fly away .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-moral condition of wickedness or iniquity — the orientation toward evil that characterizes those who reject God's ways

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 66:18 If I had cherished iniquity in my heart , the Lord would not have listened .

Isa 32:6 For the fool speaks folly , and his heart is busy with iniquity , to practice ungodliness , to utter error concerning the Lord , to leave the craving of the hungry unsatisfied , and to deprive the thirsty of drink .

groups.context_description: Term names the concentrated inner wish — desire expressed as an exclamation of intense longing or strong wish

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 34:36 Would that Job were tried to the end , because he answers like wicked men .

groups.context_description: Term names injustice as a moral condition — the pattern of wrong done by or to persons, which issues from the inner self and has inner consequences

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 64:6 They search out injustice , saying, “We have accomplished a diligent search .” For the inward mind and heart of a man are deep .

groups.context_description: Term names the unjust person — one whose inner character is oriented away from God and toward wickedness, with corresponding consequences

anchor_verses.verse_text: Zep 3:5 The Lord within her is righteous ; he does no injustice ; every morning he shows forth his justice ; each dawn he does not fail ; but the unjust knows no shame .

groups.context_description: Term names acting unjustly — the inner orientation that produces perverse conduct, unable to perceive righteousness or divine majesty

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 26:10 If favor is shown to the wicked , he does not learn righteousness ; in the land of uprightness he deals corruptly and does not see the majesty of the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the unjust person — one whose inner character is defined by moral wickedness, into whose hands the innocent may be cast

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 16:11 God gives me up to the ungodly and casts me into the hands of the wicked .

groups.context_description: Term names iniquity as a continuing inner pattern — the persistent orientation toward wrong that has characterised Israel since its early history

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 10:9 From the days of Gibeah , you have sinned , O Israel ; there they have continued . Shall not the war against the unjust overtake them in Gibeah ?

groups.context_description: Term names injustice as an inner moral condition of the person — the twisting away from what is right, producing harm to others and alienation from God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 3:20 Again, if a righteous person turns from his righteousness and commits injustice , and I lay a stumbling block before him , he shall die . Because you have not warned him , he shall die for his sin , and his righteous deeds that he has done shall not be remembered , but his blood I will require at your hand .

groups.context_description: Term names the absence of injustice in God — divine righteousness as the standard against which human injustice is measured

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 34:10 “ Therefore , hear me, you men of understanding : far be it from God that he should do wickedness , and from the Almighty that he should do wrong .

groups.context_description: Term names mourning as the inner-being state of deep grief over loss, sin, exile, or judgment — the authentic inner response of persons and communities to covenantal rupture

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 16:1 The Lord said to Samuel , “ How long will you grieve over Saul , since I have rejected him from being king over Israel ? Fill your horn with oil , and go . I will send you to Jesse the Bethlehemite , for I have provided for myself a king among his sons .”

groups.context_description: Term names the mourning person or mourning state — the inner-being condition of grief that is recognised, comforted, and transformed by God, whose eschatological reversal is the gift of joy in place of ashes

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 61:3 to grant to those who mourn in Zion — to give them a beautiful headdress instead of ashes , the oil of gladness instead of mourning , the garment of praise instead of a faint spirit ; that they may be called oaks of righteousness , the planting of the Lord , that he may be glorified .

groups.context_description: Term names iniquity as the inner-being condition of moral debt before God — the accumulated perversion of the human heart that God marks, bears, forgives, and will ultimately remember no more

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:6 All we like sheep have gone astray ; we have turned —every one —to his own way ; and the Lord has laid on him the iniquity of us all .

Jer 31:34 And no longer shall each one teach his neighbor and each his brother , saying , ‘ Know the Lord ,’ for they shall all know me, from the least of them to the greatest , declares the Lord . For I will forgive their iniquity , and I will remember their sin no more .”

groups.context_description: Inner valuation and estimation — the act of deeming worthy or unworthy, including inner self-assessment, desire, inner determination of fitness, and the valuation of honour and calling

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Th 1:11 To this end we always pray for you , that our God may make you worthy of his calling and may fulfill every resolve for good and every work of faith by his power ,

Heb 10:29 How much worse punishment , do you think , will be deserved by the one who has trampled underfoot the Son of God , and has profaned the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified , and has outraged the Spirit of grace ?

groups.context_description: Term names worthiness as a divine attribute — the intrinsic moral and ontological deservingness of God and the Lamb to receive honour, glory, and to act with authority

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 4:11 “ Worthy are you, our Lord and God , to receive glory and honor and power , for you created all things , and by your will they existed and were created .”

Rev 5:9 And they sang a new song , saying , “ Worthy are you to take the scroll and to open its seals , for you were slain , and by your blood you ransomed people for God from every tribe and language and people and nation ,

groups.context_description: Term names worthiness or desert as a moral quality of persons — whether a person's inner character qualifies them for honour, consequence, or divine presence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 11:38 of whom the world was not worthy — wandering about in deserts and mountains , and in dens and caves of the earth .
Rev 3:4 Yet you have still a few names in Sardis , people who have not soiled their garments , and they will walk with me in white , for they are worthy .

groups.context_description: Term names the absence of inner-being qualities — no fear of God, no understanding, no comforter, no shepherd, no hope — where negation discloses an inner-being deficit or moral absence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 20:11 Abraham said , “I did it because I thought , ‘ There is no fear of God at all in this place , and they will kill me because of my wife .’

groups.context_description: Spiritual receptivity and its refusal — o.zen as the inner faculty through which divine word is received, the heart opened or hardened

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 29:4 But to this day the Lord has not given you a heart to understand or eyes to see or ears to hear .

groups.context_description: The discerning ear — o.zen as the inner organ that tests, evaluates and seeks wisdom

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 12:11 Does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food ?

groups.context_description: Prayer addressing God's ear — o.zen as the inner cry of the soul urgently seeking divine attention and response

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 19:16 Incline your ear , O Lord , and hear ; open your eyes , O Lord , and see ; and hear the words of Sennacherib , which he has sent to mock the living God .

groups.context_description: Wilful closing of the ear — o.zen as the inner organ deliberately shut against evil or against God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 33:15 He who walks righteously and speaks uprightly , who despises the gain of oppressions , who shakes his hands , lest they hold a bribe , who stops his ears from hearing of bloodshed and shuts his eyes from looking on evil ,

groups.context_description: Ears tingling — o.zen as the somatic-inner receptor of divine judgment producing inner-physical response of shock

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 3:11 Then the Lord said to Samuel , “ Behold , I am about to do a thing in Israel at which the two ears of everyone who hears it will tingle .

groups.context_description: Hearing that opens into inner transformation — o.zen as the event of communal hearing that triggers inner covenant commitment, learning and lament

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 24:7 Then he took the Book of the Covenant and read it in the hearing of the people . And they said , “ All that the Lord has spoken we will do , and we will be obedient .”

groups.context_description: Revelation received and inner courage kindled — o.zen as the channel of divine disclosure that produces inner courage, prayer and intercession

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 7:27 For you , O Lord of hosts , the God of Israel , have made this revelation to your servant , saying , ‘I will build you a house .’ Therefore your servant has found courage to pray this prayer to you.

groups.context_description: Inner attentiveness called to God's word — a.zan as the summons to the receptive inner posture that constitutes covenantal attentiveness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 15:26 saying , “ If you will diligently listen to the voice of the Lord your God , and do that which is right in his eyes , and give ear to his commandments and keep all his statutes , I will put none of the diseases on you that I put on the Egyptians , for I am the Lord , your healer .”

groups.context_description: Inner hardness refusing God's ear — a.zan as the inner act of attentiveness withheld, expressing resistance to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 24:19 Yet he sent prophets among them to bring them back to the Lord . These testified against them, but they would not pay attention .

groups.context_description: Inner attentiveness oriented toward evil — a.zan as the disposition of the character that gives ear to wickedness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 17:4 An evildoer listens to wicked lips , and a liar gives ear to a mischievous tongue .

groups.context_description: Prayer as inner cry seeking God's ear — a.zan as the act of the soul urgently addressing God for mercy and deliverance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 140:6 I say to the Lord , You are my God ; give ear to the voice of my pleas for mercy , O Lord !

groups.context_description: Term names the institutional memorial — an act, object, or day established to keep an event or person before God and the community across generations

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 12:14 “ This day shall be for you a memorial day, and you shall keep it as a feast to the Lord ; throughout your generations , as a statute forever , you shall keep it as a feast .

Mal 3:16 Then those who feared the Lord spoke with one another . The Lord paid attention and heard them, and a book of remembrance was written before him of those who feared the Lord and esteemed his name .

groups.context_description: Term names the priestly/liturgical keeping of persons before God — the act of bearing names or offerings into God's presence for ongoing remembrance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 28:29 So Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment on his heart , when he goes into the Holy Place , to bring them to regular remembrance before the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of remembrance — the capacity to hold persons and events in living consciousness, and the absence of such memory as loss

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 1:11 There is no remembrance of former things, nor will there be any remembrance of later things yet to be among those who come after .

groups.context_description: Term names the memorial portion of an offering — the part burned before God as an act of bringing the offerer to divine remembrance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 2:2 and bring it to Aaron’s sons the priests . And he shall take from it a handful of the fine flour and oil , with all of its frankincense , and the priest shall burn this as its memorial portion on the altar , a food offering with a pleasing aroma to the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the rule of other lords over the people — the domination experienced by those whose inner allegiance is drawn away from God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 26:13 O Lord our God , other lords besides you have ruled over us, but your name alone we bring to remembrance .

groups.context_description: Term names God as husband of his people — the defining relational bond expressing belonging, delight, and faithful claim

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 54:5 For your Maker is your husband , the Lord of hosts is his name ; and the Holy One of Israel is your Redeemer , the God of the whole earth he is called .

Isa 62:4 You shall no more be termed Forsaken , and your land shall no more be termed Desolate , but you shall be called My Delight Is in Her , and your land Married ; for the Lord delights in you, and your land shall be married .

groups.context_description: Term names the marriage bond as the expression of spiritual allegiance — its faithful or unfaithful orientation toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mal 2:11 Judah has been faithless , and abomination has been committed in Israel and in Jerusalem . For Judah has profaned the sanctuary of the Lord , which he loves , and has married the daughter of a foreign god .

groups.context_description: Term names the owner's relationship to possessions — the inner futility and self-harm of holding wealth as master over it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 1:3 The ox knows its owner , and the donkey its master’s crib , but Israel does not know , my people do not understand .”

groups.context_description: Term names the marital bond as the arena of inner honour, trust, and grief — the husband-relationship as constitutive of inner dignity, faithfulness, and loss

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 12:4 An excellent wife is the crown of her husband , but she who brings shame is like rottenness in his bones .
Pro 31:11 The heart of her husband trusts in her, and he will have no lack of gain .

groups.context_description: Term names a person as "master" of an inner quality or capacity — defined by anger, appetite, or ability as their characteristic inner possession

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 22:24 Make no friendship with a man given to anger , nor go with a wrathful man ,
Pro 23:2 and put a knife to your throat if you are given to appetite .

groups.context_description: Term names a person as the master/possessor of a defining inner quality — vision, wrath, malice, or destruction — as their characteristic disposition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 37:19 They said to one another , “ Here comes this dreamer .
Pro 24:8 Whoever plans to do evil will be called a schemer .

groups.context_description: Term names a person as the possessor of an inner quality or capacity — wisdom, wickedness, a sworn commitment — that defines and shapes their inner life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 16:22 Good sense is a fountain of life to him who has it, but the instruction of fools is folly .
Ecc 7:12 For the protection of wisdom is like the protection of money , and the advantage of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of him who has it.

groups.context_description: Term names a person as the possessor of spiritual power — a medium or sorceress defined by her mastery of occult capacities

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 28:7 Then Saul said to his servants , “Seek out for me a woman who is a medium , that I may go to her and inquire of her.” And his servants said to him, “ Behold , there is a medium at En-dor .”

groups.context_description: Term names Baal-worship as the outward expression of inward spiritual allegiance misoriented away from God — serving, following, forgetting, and whoring after false gods

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 18:21 And Elijah came near to all the people and said , “How long will you go limping between two different opinions ? If the Lord is God , follow him; but if Baal , then follow him.” And the people did not answer him a word .
Jer 9:14 but have stubbornly followed their own hearts and have gone after the Baals , as their fathers taught them.

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of brutish insensibility — the person who is beast-like in understanding, unable or unwilling to perceive what is true

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 73:22 I was brutish and ignorant ; I was like a beast toward you .

groups.context_description: Distress and displeasure as inner affective state

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 6:14 Then the king , when he heard these words , was much distressed and set his mind to deliver Daniel . And he labored till the sun went down to rescue him .

groups.context_description: Term names God's elective choosing of a person or people — the expression of divine inner will in covenant election and appointment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 7:6 “For you are a people holy to the Lord your God . The Lord your God has chosen you to be a people for his treasured possession , out of all the peoples who are on the face of the earth .

groups.context_description: Term names God's choosing of a place — divine designation of a location as the dwelling of his name and the site of covenant worship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 12:5 But you shall seek the place that the Lord your God will choose out of all your tribes to put his name and make his habitation there . There you shall go ,

groups.context_description: Term names the human moral or volitional choosing — the inner will of the person directed toward good or evil, wisdom or folly, God or idols

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 30:19 I call heaven and earth to witness against you today , that I have set before you life and death , blessing and curse . Therefore choose life , that you and your offspring may live ,

groups.context_description: Term names practical human choosing — selecting persons or options in leadership, strategy, or administration; the act of directed human preference

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 17:9 So Moses said to Joshua , “ Choose for us men , and go out and fight with Amalek . Tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the staff of God in my hand .”

groups.context_description: Term names treachery as an inner moral condition — the act and disposition of betrayal as a defining moral characteristic

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 24:16 From the ends of the earth we hear songs of praise , of glory to the Righteous One . But I say , “I waste away , I waste away . Woe is me! For the traitors have betrayed , with betrayal the traitors have betrayed .”

groups.context_description: Term names the treacherous as those whose outward conduct conceals an inward failure of wholehearted devotion — treachery as the gap between inner state and outward profession

Psa 139:2 You know when I sit down and when I rise up ; you discern my thoughts from afar .

groups.context_description: Term names the failure, hardening, or absence of inner understanding — spiritual blindness, incapacity to perceive God or moral reality, and the rhetorical limits of human comprehension before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 6:10 Make the heart of this people dull , and their ears heavy , and blind their eyes ; lest they see with their eyes , and hear with their ears , and understand with their hearts , and turn and be healed .”
Jer 4:22 “ For my people are foolish ; they know me not ; they are stupid children ; they have no understanding . They are ‘ wise ’—in doing evil ! But how to do good they know not .”

groups.context_description: Understanding as the inner receptive capacity that God recognises and enriches — those who have understanding receive wisdom and knowledge from God (Dan 2:21); the cognitive-spiritual capacity for discernment.

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 2:21 He changes times and seasons ; he removes kings and sets up kings ; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding ;

groups.context_description: Term names Israel's wisdom and understanding as demonstrated through faithful obedience to God's statutes — the inner capacity for discernment made visible in the life of the covenant people (Deu 4:6).

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 4:6 Keep them and do them, for that will be your wisdom and your understanding in the sight of the peoples , who , when they hear all these statutes , will say , ‘ Surely this great nation is a wise and understanding people .’

groups.context_description: Term names understanding as a human inner-being capacity — the gift from God enabling discernment of his ways and wise conduct

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ch 12:32 Of Issachar , men who had understanding of the times , to know what Israel ought to do , 200 chiefs , and all their kinsmen under their command .

groups.context_description: Term names understanding as a divine attribute — the infinite discernment by which God creates, orders, and governs all things

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 147:5 Great is our Lord , and abundant in power ; his understanding is beyond measure .

groups.context_description: Term names understanding as a human inner-being capacity — enabling wisdom, right conduct, and discernment, received from God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 2:11 discretion will watch over you, understanding will guard you,
Pro 14:29 Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding , but he who has a hasty temper exalts folly .

groups.context_description: Blasphemy as inner contempt and rebellion — the hardened heart that curses God and will not repent

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 16:11 and cursed the God of heaven for their pain and sores . They did not repent of their deeds .

groups.context_description: Slander and speaking evil — inner hostile speech against others or the gospel

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 4:13 when slandered , we entreat . We have become , and are still , like the scum of the world , the refuse of all things .

groups.context_description: Term names the coming or approach that engages the inner being through encounter with God — God coming to humans in theophany, warning, judgment and salvation; humans coming before God in prayer, worship, confession, and sacred entry; the day of the Lord approaching with dread or hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 4:20 but they shall not go in to look on the holy things even for a moment , lest they die .”

Num 7:89 And when Moses went into the tent of meeting to speak with the Lord , he heard the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat that was on the ark of the testimony , from between the two cherubim ; and it spoke to him .
Num 14:24 But my servant Caleb , because he has a different spirit and has followed me fully , I will bring into the land into which he went , and his descendants shall possess it .

Num 21:7 And the people came to Moses and said , “We have sinned , for we have spoken against the Lord and against you. Pray to the Lord , that he take away the serpents from us.” So Moses prayed for the people .
Job 23:3 Oh , that I knew where I might find him, that I might come even to his seat !
Isa 1:12 “When you come to appear before me, who has required of you this trampling of my courts ?

Isa 19:1 An oracle concerning Egypt . Behold , the Lord is riding on a swift cloud and comes to Egypt ; and the idols of Egypt will tremble at his presence , and the heart of the Egyptians will melt within them .

Isa 30:29 You shall have a song as in the night when a holy feast is kept, and gladness of heart , as when one sets out to the sound of the flute to go to the mountain of the Lord , to the Rock of Israel .
Isa 35:4 Say to those who have an anxious heart , “Be strong ; fear not ! Behold , your God will come with vengeance , with the recompense of God . He will come and save you .”
Hos 10:12 Sow for yourselves righteousness ; reap steadfast love ; break up your fallow ground , for it is the time to seek the Lord , that he may come and rain righteousness upon you .

groups.context_description: Term names the arrival of calamity, distress, dread, and mortality upon the inner being — the moment when feared things come, sighing replaces bread, evil comes instead of hoped-for good, and trouble arrives without rest

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 3:25 For the thing that I fear comes upon me, and what I dread befalls me .
Job 4:5 But now it has come to you, and you are impatient ; it touches you, and you are dismayed .
Job 30:26 But when I hoped for good , evil came , and when I waited for light , darkness came .

Hab 3:16 I hear , and my body trembles ; my lips quiver at the sound ; rottenness enters into my bones ; my legs tremble beneath me. Yet I will quietly wait for the day of trouble to come upon people who invade us .

groups.context_description: Term in relational and covenantal entry with inner-being moral dimension — entering another person with longing, deception, desperation, or transgression; entering covenant commitment; and the purity implications of entering sacred or defiled space

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 6:4 The Nephilim were on the earth in those days , and also afterward , when the sons of God came in to the daughters of man and they bore children to them . These were the mighty men who were of old , the men of renown .

Hos 9:10 Like grapes in the wilderness , I found Israel . Like the first fruit on the fig tree in its first season , I saw your fathers . But they came to Baal-peor and consecrated themselves to the thing of shame , and became detestable like the thing they loved .

groups.context_description: Term names the covenantal casting away of a people and its inner-relational consequence — the loss carries redemptive significance for the world's reconciliation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 11:15 For if their rejection means the reconciliation of the world , what will their acceptance mean but life from the dead ?

groups.context_description: Term names the casting aside of what inhibits (expressing inner urgency toward Christ) or the abandonment of inner confidence — the throwing away of obstacles or inner dispositions

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 10:35 Therefore do not throw away your confidence , which has a great reward .

groups.context_description: Term names shame as the inner experience of disgrace and disappointment — the person overwhelmed by shame from moral failure, social reversal, or spiritual abandonment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 2:25 And the man and his wife were both naked and were not ashamed .

groups.context_description: Term names the promised absence of shame for those who trust in God — hope in God is the antidote to inner shame; shame will not be the final word for the faithful

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 22:5 To you they cried and were rescued ; in you they trusted and were not put to shame .

groups.context_description: Term names the shaming of enemies and evildoers as divine judgment — God bringing inner shame upon those who oppose him or his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 6:10 All my enemies shall be ashamed and greatly troubled ; they shall turn back and be put to shame in a moment .

groups.context_description: Term names the experience of shame as an inward condition of mortification — arising from sin, failure, or covenant breach before God or community

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ezr 9:7 From the days of our fathers to this day we have been in great guilt . And for our iniquities we , our kings , and our priests have been given into the hand of the kings of the lands , to the sword , to captivity , to plundering , and to utter shame , as it is today .

Jer 3:25 Let us lie down in our shame , and let our dishonor cover us. For we have sinned against the Lord our God , we and our fathers , from our youth even to this day , and we have not obeyed the voice of the Lord our God .”

groups.context_description: Term names shame as a social condition imposed upon an enemy or adversary — functioning as the opposite of honor and as the visible marker of defeat or rejection

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 8:22 Those who hate you will be clothed with shame , and the tent of the wicked will be no more .”
Psa 132:18 His enemies I will clothe with shame , but on him his crown will shine .”

groups.context_description: Term names shame as the inner consequence of idolatry — the people put to shame through their misplaced devotion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 10:6 The thing itself shall be carried to Assyria as tribute to the great king . Ephraim shall be put to shame , and Israel shall be ashamed of his idol .

groups.context_description: Term names shame as a covering experience — shame that covers the face or envelops the person as the experience of disgrace and judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 7:18 They put on sackcloth , and horror covers them. Shame is on all faces , and baldness on all their heads .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner experience of enduring hostile plots — suffering, tears, and faithful perseverance of the person who serves God while subject to covert opposition.

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 20:19 serving the Lord with all humility and with tears and with trials that happened to me through the plots of the Jews ;

groups.context_description: Term names the counsellor as a category that the human person cannot fill for God — the recognition of the incomparable inner wisdom of God against which human wisdom is nothing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 11:34 “ For who has known the mind of the Lord , or who has been his counselor ?”

groups.context_description: Term names the taking of counsel as an expression of inner moral intention — the deliberation that reveals the orientation of the heart toward a moral end

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 27:1 When morning came , all the chief priests and the elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner deliberative purpose or resolved intention of a person, located within the heart as the seat of purposive will

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 27:42 The soldiers ’ plan was to kill the prisoners , lest any should swim away and escape .

1Cor 4:5 Therefore do not pronounce judgment before the time , before the Lord comes , who will bring to light the things now hidden in darkness and will disclose the purposes of the heart . Then each one will receive his commendation from God .

groups.context_description: Term names God's sovereign purposive counsel that directs human events and vocation, intersecting with the inner life of those subject to it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 7:30 but the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the purpose of God for themselves , not having been baptized by him .)
Eph 1:11 In him we have obtained an inheritance , having been predestined according to the purpose of him who works all things according to the counsel of his will ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of deliberate decision — the mind resolving upon a course of action through weighing and choosing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 14:31 Or what king , going out to encounter another king in war , will not sit down first and deliberate whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand ?

groups.context_description: Term names deliberate planning directed toward harm — the inner will forming an intention against another

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 11:53 So from that day on they made plans to put him to death .

groups.context_description: Term names a determined purposive will — human deliberate intention or divine sovereign resolve — as the inner origin of directed action

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 27:43 But the centurion , wishing to save Paul , kept them from carrying out their plan . He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and make for the land ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of human willing, intending, or desiring — the volitional faculty in motion toward an object or action

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 1:19 And her husband Joseph , being a just man and unwilling to put her to shame , resolved to divorce her quietly .
1Ti 6:9 But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation , into a snare , into many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction .

groups.context_description: Term names God's or Christ's inner purposive will — sovereign divine willing that directs revelation, creation, and redemption

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 22:42 saying , “ Father , if you are willing , remove this cup from me . Nevertheless , not my will , but yours , be done .”
2Pe 3:9 The Lord is not slow to fulfill his promise as some count slowness , but is patient toward you , not wishing that any should perish , but that all should reach repentance .

groups.context_description: Term names contempt as an inner attitude of disregard — held toward others from pride or comfortable indifference, or experienced as relational humiliation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 12:5 In the thought of one who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune ; it is ready for those whose feet slip .
