groups.context_description: Term names trembling before divine theophanic presence — the total inner agitation produced by the overwhelming encounter with God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 19:16 On the morning of the third day there were thunders and lightnings and a thick cloud on the mountain and a very loud trumpet blast , so that all the people in the camp trembled .
Job 37:1 “At this also my heart trembles and leaps out of its place .

groups.context_description: Term names trembling in fear before threatening powers or circumstances — the inner agitation of panic and dread

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 28:5 When Saul saw the army of the Philistines , he was afraid , and his heart trembled greatly .
Isa 19:16 In that day the Egyptians will be like women , and tremble with fear before the hand that the Lord of hosts shakes over them .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of promised security — dwelling without trembling; the eschatological rest from fear as the divine gift

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 26:6 I will give peace in the land , and you shall lie down , and none shall make you afraid . And I will remove harmful beasts from the land , and the sword shall not go through your land .

Jer 30:10 “Then fear not , O Jacob my servant , declares the Lord , nor be dismayed , O Israel ; for behold , I will save you from far away , and your offspring from the land of their captivity . Jacob shall return and have quiet and ease , and none shall make him afraid .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of overwhelming trembling before the sacred or the divine — the total inner agitation that seizes those at the boundary of the holy

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 27:33 Then Isaac trembled very violently and said , “ Who was it then that hunted game and brought it to me, and I ate it all before you came , and I have blessed him? Yes, and he shall be blessed .”
Dan 10:7 And I , Daniel , alone saw the vision , for the men who were with me did not see the vision , but a great trembling fell upon them, and they fled to hide themselves.

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of panic and dread — the acute inner terror before threatening circumstances, battle, or judgement

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 14:15 And there was a panic in the camp , in the field , and among all the people . The garrison and even the raiders trembled , the earth quaked , and it became a very great panic .
Isa 21:4 My heart staggers ; horror has appalled me; the twilight I longed for has been turned for me into trembling .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of trembling at the word of God — the responsive, reverent trembling that characterises the person God looks upon with favour

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ezr 9:4 Then all who trembled at the words of the God of Israel , because of the faithlessness of the returned exiles , gathered around me while I sat appalled until the evening sacrifice .

Isa 66:2 All these things my hand has made , and so all these things came to be, declares the Lord . But this is the one to whom I will look : he who is humble and contrite in spirit and trembles at my word .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of fearful trembling before threatening circumstances or the sacred — the inner agitation of fear or reverent anxiety

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 7:3 Now therefore proclaim in the ears of the people , saying , ‘ Whoever is fearful and trembling , let him return home and hurry away from Mount Gilead .’” Then 22,000 of the people returned , and 10,000 remained .

1Sa 4:13 When he arrived , Eli was sitting on his seat by the road watching , for his heart trembled for the ark of God . And when the man came into the city and told the news, all the city cried out .

groups.context_description: Term names joy as the abiding inner state — the deep gladness that characterises the life of faith and the community of believers

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 15:11 These things I have spoken to you , that my joy may be in you , and that your joy may be full .
Gal 5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love , joy , peace , patience , kindness , goodness , faithfulness ,

groups.context_description: Term names the fullness and completion of joy as the goal of the inner life — the joy that is made complete in God and in his purposes

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 16:24 Until now you have asked nothing in my name . Ask , and you will receive , that your joy may be full .
1Jo 1:4 And we are writing these things so that our joy may be complete .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of rejoicing in God, his work, and his people — the abiding orientation of the inner person toward gladness in the Lord

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say, rejoice.
1Th 5:16 Rejoice always ,

groups.context_description: Term names the paradoxical inner joy that persists through and is intensified by suffering, sorrow, and tribulation

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 6:10 as sorrowful , yet always rejoicing ; as poor , yet making many rich ; as having nothing , yet possessing everything .
1Pe 4:13 But rejoice insofar as you share Christ’s sufferings , that you may also rejoice and be glad when his glory is revealed .

groups.context_description: Term names the communal sharing of inner joy — the act of rejoicing together in another's good, and the inner orientation of love that makes such shared joy possible

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 15:6 And when he comes home , he calls together his friends and his neighbors , saying to them , ‘ Rejoice with me , for I have found my sheep that was lost .’
1Cor 13:6 it does not rejoice at wrongdoing , but rejoices with the truth .

groups.context_description: Term designates wisdom as an inner character quality — the wise person's disposition toward knowledge, instruction, and God contrasted with the fool

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 10:8 The wise of heart will receive commandments , but a babbling fool will come to ruin .
Pro 29:11 A fool gives full vent to his spirit , but a wise man quietly holds it back .

groups.context_description: Term identifies wisdom as a quality that God gives to rulers and leaders — a divine endowment of inner discernment for governance, judgment, and leadership

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:12 behold , I now do according to your word . Behold , I give you a wise and discerning mind , so that none like you has been before you and none like you shall arise after you .

groups.context_description: Term names wisdom as the inner orientation from which the fear of the Lord flows — contrasted with self-attributed wisdom as an inner posture opposed to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 37:24 Therefore men fear him; he does not regard any who are wise in their own conceit .”
Pro 3:7 Be not wise in your own eyes ; fear the Lord , and turn away from evil .

groups.context_description: Term names disease as a condition in which the inner person encounters divine restoration — illness as the context of forgiveness and healing of the whole person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 103:3 who forgives all your iniquity , who heals all your diseases ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-being condition of grievous weakness and wound — the pain that afflicts and weakens the person or community, which God inflicts purposively and heals

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:10 Yet it was the will of the Lord to crush him; he has put him to grief ; when his soul makes an offering for guilt , he shall see his offspring ; he shall prolong his days ; the will of the Lord shall prosper in his hand .

groups.context_description: Term names the absence of grief over sin as moral failure — the inner-being indifference to national and communal catastrophe that characterises those who are at ease

anchor_verses.verse_text: Amo 6:6 who drink wine in bowls and anoint themselves with the finest oils , but are not grieved over the ruin of Joseph !

groups.context_description: Term names sickness and grief as an inner-being condition — the suffering, grief, and moral sickness borne by persons, nations, and supremely by the Servant who vicariously carries what others cannot bear

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:4 Surely he has borne our griefs and carried our sorrows ; yet we esteemed him stricken , smitten by God , and afflicted .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of profaning what is sacred — the inner-moral act of treating holy things, God's name, or the Sabbath as common, constituting a violation of the inner-spiritual relationship with God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 18:21 You shall not give any of your children to offer them to Molech , and so profane the name of your God : I am the Lord .
Lev 19:12 You shall not swear by my name falsely , and so profane the name of your God : I am the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term describes the inner renewal of strength and vitality that comes through waiting on or turning to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 40:31 but they who wait for the Lord shall renew their strength ; they shall mount up with wings like eagles ; they shall run and not be weary ; they shall walk and not faint .

groups.context_description: Term expresses the transience of human life and creation — the rapid passing that characterises human existence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 90:5 You sweep them away as with a flood ; they are like a dream , like grass that is renewed in the morning :

groups.context_description: Term describes the wilful overriding of divine covenant — an inner act of defiance against binding obligation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 24:5 The earth lies defiled under its inhabitants ; for they have transgressed the laws , violated the statutes , broken the everlasting covenant .

groups.context_description: Term describes the passing of God or divine spirit near the person, engaging the person's inner capacity for perception or producing inner dread

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 9:11 Behold, he passes by me, and I see him not; he moves on , but I do not perceive him .

groups.context_description: "The Lord is my portion" — God as the ultimate inner orientation of trust and belonging

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 16:5 The Lord is my chosen portion and my cup ; you hold my lot .

groups.context_description: Portion as lot and inner acceptance — finding contentment in one's assigned place

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 5:19 Everyone also to whom God has given wealth and possessions and power to enjoy them , and to accept his lot and rejoice in his toil — this is the gift of God .

groups.context_description: Portion as moral destiny and spiritual belonging — receiving or losing one's share in God's community

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 20:29 This is the wicked man’s portion from God , the heritage decreed for him by God .”

groups.context_description: Division as inner conflict and relational fracture — people divided, desire to dominate

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 15:9 The enemy said , ‘I will pursue , I will overtake , I will divide the spoil , my desire shall have its fill of them . I will draw my sword ; my hand shall destroy them.’

groups.context_description: Portion allotted by God — the divine distribution of understanding, pain, and eschatological reward

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 39:17 because God has made her forget wisdom and given her no share in understanding .

groups.context_description: A pleasant portion despoiled — God's inner grief over the loss of what is precious

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 12:10 Many shepherds have destroyed my vineyard ; they have trampled down my portion ; they have made my pleasant portion a desolate wilderness .

groups.context_description: Flattering lips and double heart — the inner moral quality of deceptive smoothness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 12:2 Everyone utters lies to his neighbor ; with flattering lips and a double heart they speak .

groups.context_description: Slippery footing — the inner instability of those who appear to prosper

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 73:18 Truly you set them in slippery places ; you make them fall to ruin .

groups.context_description: Willing inner disposition in service — every willing person offering skill to God's work

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ch 28:21 And behold the divisions of the priests and the Levites for all the service of the house of God ; and with you in all the work will be every willing man who has skill for any kind of service ; also the officers and all the people will be wholly at your command .”

groups.context_description: Term names the divinely assigned portion as the condition imposed on human pride — what God apportions to the person who exalts himself

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 4:15 But leave the stump of its roots in the earth , bound with a band of iron and bronze , amid the tender grass of the field . Let him be wet with the dew of heaven . Let his portion be with the beasts in the grass of the earth .

groups.context_description: Term names change as inner transformation — the hoped-for renewal of one's condition through patient waiting, or the failure to change as evidence of persistent inner hardness toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 14:14 If a man dies , shall he live again? All the days of my service I would wait , till my renewal should come .

groups.context_description: Term names the Nazirite locks of hair as the physical expression of an inner covenantal reality — the consecration whose physical removal represents the departure of inner strength and divine empowerment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 16:13 Then Delilah said to Samson , “ Until now you have mocked me and told me lies . Tell me how you might be bound .” And he said to her, “ If you weave the seven locks of my head with the web and fasten it tight with the pin ^ , then I shall become weak and be like any other man .”

groups.context_description: Term names the perishing as those whose vulnerable condition calls forth advocacy from the inner conscience of others — the destitute whose cause requires the advocate's will

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 31:8 Open your mouth for the mute , for the rights of all who are destitute .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner warming process of developing intensity — anger or passionate feeling as a thermal process building from within through meditation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 39:3 My heart became hot within me. As I mused , the fire burned ; then I spoke with my tongue :

groups.context_description: Term names the quality of desirability in an object — what makes it a focus of inner longing, lust, or delight; the magnetic draw the object exerts on the inner being

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 23:12 She lusted after the Assyrians , governors and commanders , warriors clothed in full armor , horsemen riding on horses , all of them desirable young men .

groups.context_description: Term names the cherished object of deepest desire — what the inner being holds most dear, and whose loss or destruction evokes profound grief

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 24:21 ‘ Say to the house of Israel , Thus says the Lord God : Behold , I will profane my sanctuary , the pride of your power , the delight of your eyes , and the yearning of your soul , and your sons and your daughters whom you left behind shall fall by the sword .

groups.context_description: Term names desirability or preciousness as the quality that draws the inner being — what is cherished, longed for, or grieved when lost

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 11:37 He shall pay no attention to the gods of his fathers , or to the one beloved by women . He shall not pay attention to any other god , for he shall magnify himself above all .

groups.context_description: Term names coveting — the prohibited inner desire for what belongs to another; desire as acquisitive grasping that violates boundaries

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 20:17 “You shall not covet your neighbor’s house ; you shall not covet your neighbor’s wife , or his male servant , or his female servant , or his ox , or his donkey , or anything that is your neighbor’s .”

groups.context_description: Term names desire as positive inner orientation — delight in what is genuinely good, beautiful, or worthy

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 19:10 More to be desired are they than gold , even much fine gold ; sweeter also than honey and drippings of the honeycomb .

groups.context_description: Term names idolatrous or misdirected desire — inner longing directed toward what does not profit or toward spiritual falsehood

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 44:9 All who fashion idols are nothing , and the things they delight in do not profit . Their witnesses neither see nor know , that they may be put to shame .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine inner desire — God's own longing for a place or his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 68:16 Why do you look with hatred , O many-peaked mountain , at the mount that God desired for his abode , yes , where the Lord will dwell forever ?

groups.context_description: Term names human compassion or its withholding — the inner disposition of pity or mercy toward another, and its refusal

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 2:6 When she opened it, she saw the child , and behold , the baby was crying . She took pity on him and said , “ This is one of the Hebrews ’ children .”

groups.context_description: Term names divine compassion or its withholding — God's inner disposition of pity or mercy toward his people, and its withdrawal in judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 36:15 The Lord , the God of their fathers , sent persistently to them by his messengers , because he had compassion on his people and on his dwelling place .

groups.context_description: Term names the precious/desirable thing as the object of inner-being longing — the valued possession or quality that the inner person holds dear, desires, or is drawn toward

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 3:19 “‘ I said , How I would set you among my sons , and give you a pleasant land , a heritage most beautiful of all nations . And I thought you would call me, My Father , and would not turn from following me .

groups.context_description: The loss or desecration of what is precious — inner-being grief

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 21:20 He was thirty-two years old when he began to reign , and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem . And he departed with no one’s regret . They buried him in the city of David , but not in the tombs of the kings .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of being gracious or showing favour — God's inner disposition of grace toward the supplicant, and the human cry that turns to God in petition and dependence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 6:25 the Lord make his face to shine upon you and be gracious to you ;

To the choirmaster . A Psalm of David , when Nathan the prophet went to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba . Psa 51:1 Have mercy on me, O God , according to your steadfast love ; according to your abundant mercy blot out my transgressions .

groups.context_description: Term names supplication to God — the inner act of pleading for mercy, rooted in recognition of one's own need and God's disposition to hear and forgive

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 8:38 whatever prayer , whatever plea is made by any man or by all your people Israel , each knowing the affliction of his own heart and stretching out his hands toward this house ,
Dan 9:20 While I was speaking and praying , confessing my sin and the sin of my people Israel , and presenting my plea before the Lord my God for the holy hill of my God ,

groups.context_description: Term names supplication to human authority — the humble inner posture of plea before one with power over the supplicant's life or freedom

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 37:20 Now hear , please , O my lord the king : let my humble plea come before you and do not send me back to the house of Jonathan the secretary , lest I die there .”

groups.context_description: Term names mercy shown in moral action and the plea of prayer — the practice of mercy toward the oppressed and the inner posture of petition before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 4:27 Therefore , O king , let my counsel be acceptable to you : break off your sins by practicing righteousness , and your iniquities by showing mercy to the oppressed , that there may perhaps be a lengthening of your prosperity .”
Dan 6:11 Then these men came by agreement and found Daniel making petition and plea before his God .

groups.context_description: Term names the inward experience of being ashamed — the felt condition of shame, confoundedness, or disgrace arising from failure, defeat, or exposure

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 34:5 Those who look to him are radiant , and their faces shall never be ashamed .
Pro 13:5 The righteous hates falsehood , but the wicked brings shame and disgrace .

groups.context_description: Term names delight as the orientation of the inner being toward its object — the inner disposition of willing, delighting in, or taking pleasure in what one loves

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mic 7:18 Who is a God like you, pardoning iniquity and passing over transgression for the remnant of his inheritance ? He does not retain his anger forever , because he delights in steadfast love .

groups.context_description: Term names divine pleasure, purpose, or delight — what God takes pleasure in, purposes, or wills

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:10 Yet it was the will of the Lord to crush him; he has put him to grief ; when his soul makes an offering for guilt , he shall see his offspring ; he shall prolong his days ; the will of the Lord shall prosper in his hand .

groups.context_description: Term names human delight or pleasure as inner orientation — what the person takes pleasure in, delights in, or values most

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 1:2 but his delight is in the law of the Lord , and on his law he meditates day and night .

groups.context_description: Term names desire as practical want or request — what someone asks for or desires in a relational or practical context

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 5:8 And Hiram sent to Solomon , saying , “I have heard the message that you have sent to me . I am ready to do all you desire in the matter of cedar and cypress timber .

groups.context_description: Term names the absence of desire or care — the inner state of indifference, being unmourned, or valued by no one

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 48:38 On all the housetops of Moab and in the squares there is nothing but lamentation , for I have broken Moab like a vessel for which no one cares , declares the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the kindling of inner anger as the first act of moral rebellion — Cain's anger at divine rejection as the origin-point of the anger characteristic in Scripture

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 4:5 but for Cain and his offering he had no regard . So Cain was very angry , and his face fell .

groups.context_description: Term names divine anger as the inner response to Israel's repeated covenant violation — the burning of God's wrath in the moment of particular transgression

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 32:10 Now therefore let me alone , that my wrath may burn hot against them and I may consume them, in order that I may make a great nation of you.”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner injunction against anger as spiritual counsel — do not let anger kindle; cease from it; it leads only to harm

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 37:8 Refrain from anger , and forsake wrath ! Fret not yourself; it tends only to evil .

groups.context_description: Term names the burning intensity of divine wrath at its maximum — fierce anger as the pinnacle of God's response to covenant violation, carrying cosmic consequences

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 4:8 For this put on sackcloth , lament and wail , for the fierce anger of the Lord has not turned back from us .”

groups.context_description: Term names human burning anger as the inner response to perceived injustice or affront — the fierce heat of personal wrath that drives consequential action

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 28:11 Now hear me, and send back the captives from your relatives whom you have taken , for the fierce wrath of the Lord is upon you .”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner capacity for endurance under increasing pressure — the ability of the inner person to sustain more difficult circumstances without collapsing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 12:5 “ If you have raced with men on foot , and they have wearied you, how will you compete with horses ? And if in a safe land you are so trusting , what will you do in the thicket of the Jordan ?

groups.context_description: Term names competition as an exposure of inner character — striving for display reveals the poverty of the inner life compared with the practice of justice

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 22:15 Do you think you are a king because you compete in cedar ? Did not your father eat and drink and do justice and righteousness ? Then it was well with him .

groups.context_description: Term names God's gracious giving and forgiving — the divine act of freely granting, releasing, and bestowing on the basis of grace alone, supremely in the gift of the Son and forgiveness of sins

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 8:32 He who did not spare his own Son but gave him up for us all , how will he not also with him graciously give us all things?

groups.context_description: Term names the human act of forgiving — releasing another from debt, offence, or resentment, patterned on and grounded in the divine forgiveness received, as an inner-relational act of grace extended to another

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 4:32 Be kind to one another , tenderhearted , forgiving one another , as God in Christ forgave you .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of bestowing grace — the divine favour poured upon a person or people as a relational act that marks and transforms the one so favoured

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 1:28 And he came to her and said , “ Greetings , O favored one , the Lord is with you !”

groups.context_description: Term names grace as the sovereign, unmerited divine disposition toward humanity — the freely given, transforming gift through which God justifies, saves, calls, and sustains, supremely displayed in Christ

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 12:9 But he said to me , “ My grace is sufficient for you , for my power is made perfect in weakness .” Therefore I will boast all the more gladly of my weaknesses , so that the power of Christ may rest upon me .
Eph 2:8 For by grace you have been saved through faith . And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God ,

groups.context_description: Term names grace as the relational sphere and power in which the believer stands and lives — the ongoing inner-relational condition of being under grace, accessed by faith, sustained by God, and expressed in the transformed inner being

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 5:2 Through him we have also obtained access by faith into this grace in which we stand , and we rejoice in hope of the glory of God .

1Cor 15:10 But by the grace of God I am what I am , and his grace toward me was not in vain . On the contrary , I worked harder than any of them , though it was not I , but the grace of God that is with me .

groups.context_description: Term names grace as relational favour — the disposition of goodwill between persons or between a person and God, which opens access, creates loyalty, and reflects the character of the one granting it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 1:30 And the angel said to her , “Do not be afraid , Mary , for you have found favor with God .

groups.context_description: Term names grace as the animating power and sphere of apostolic mission — the word of grace, the commendation to grace, the extending of grace to more people, and the inner source of ministry identity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 20:32 And now I commend you to God and to the word of his grace , which is able to build you up and to give you the inheritance among all those who are sanctified .

groups.context_description: Term names reproach as an inner-social condition experienced by individuals — the felt shame of being despised, scorned, or dishonoured before others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 69:20 Reproaches have broken my heart , so that I am in despair . I looked for pity , but there was none , and for comforters , but I found none .
Jer 15:15 O Lord , you know ; remember me and visit me, , and take vengeance for me on my persecutors . In your forbearance take me not away ; know that for your sake I bear reproach .

groups.context_description: Term names reproach as a corporate-communal condition imposed upon God's people as divine judgment or national disgrace — the community bears the inner-social burden of collective shame

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jos 5:9 And the Lord said to Joshua , “ Today I have rolled away the reproach of Egypt from you.” And so the name of that place is called Gilgal to this day .
Eze 5:14 Moreover, I will make you a desolation and an object of reproach among the nations all around you and in the sight of all who pass by .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of taunting or reviling as an expression of contempt, defiance, or hostility directed at persons — the verbal act originates in an inner orientation of contempt and produces inner-being impact on the recipient

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 42:10 As with a deadly wound in my bones , my adversaries taunt me, while they say to me all the day long, “ Where is your God ?”

groups.context_description: Term names the act of taunting or defying God — expressing an inner orientation of contempt and arrogance toward the divine, and functioning as the ground of divine judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 19:22 “Whom have you mocked and reviled ? Against whom have you raised your voice and lifted your eyes to the heights ? Against the Holy One of Israel !
Psa 79:12 Return sevenfold into the lap of our neighbors the taunts with which they have taunted you, O Lord !

groups.context_description: Term names the charisma as God's free gift of grace — the spiritual capacity given to each person for the building up of the community, and supremely the gift of eternal life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 6:23 For the wages of sin is death , but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord .
Rom 12:6 Having gifts that differ according to the grace given to us , let us use them: if prophecy , in proportion to our faith ;

groups.context_description: Term names the posture of inner restraint — holding silence in response to emotional provocation, injustice, or the desire to speak

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 13:20 And her brother Absalom said to her, “Has Amnon your brother been with you? Now hold your peace , my sister . He is your brother ; do not take this to heart .” So Tamar lived , a desolate woman , in her brother Absalom’s house .
Psa 32:3 For when I kept silent , my bones wasted away through my groaning all day long.

groups.context_description: Term names the receptive posture of silence — an inner orientation of attentive submission, readiness to hear or receive

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 14:14 The Lord will fight for you, and you have only to be silent .”
Job 6:24 “ Teach me , and I will be silent ; make me understand how I have gone astray .

groups.context_description: Term names the posture of wisdom — silence as an expression of inner discernment, understanding, or moral restraint

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 11:12 Whoever belittles his neighbor lacks sense , but a man of understanding remains silent .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition when an appeal is made for God not to be silent — the psalmist's existential need for divine responsiveness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Of David . Psa 28:1 To you, O Lord , I call ; my rock , be not deaf to me, lest, if you be silent to me, I become like those who go down to the pit .
Psa 39:12 “ Hear my prayer , O Lord , and give ear to my cry ; hold not your peace at my tears ! For I am a sojourner with you, a guest , like all my fathers .

groups.context_description: Term names God's own posture of silence or its breaking — divine inner restraint, patience, or deliberate withholding

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 50:21 These things you have done , and I have been silent ; you thought that I was one like yourself. But now I rebuke you and lay the charge before you.
Isa 42:14 For a long time I have held my peace ; I have kept still and restrained myself; now I will cry out like a woman in labor ; I will gasp and pant .

groups.context_description: Term applied to ethical-existential confrontation — the moral weight of remaining silent at a critical moment of injustice or crisis

anchor_verses.verse_text: Est 4:14 For if you keep silent at this time , relief and deliverance will rise for the Jews from another place , but you and your father’s house will perish . And who knows whether you have not come to the kingdom for such a time as this ?”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner devising of evil — the heart plotting harm or crafting schemes against another

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 6:14 with perverted heart devises evil , continually sowing discord ;

groups.context_description: Term names the metaphorical plowing of iniquity — inner sin etched deeply into the heart or expressed as a pattern of moral cultivation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 17:1 “The sin of Judah is written with a pen of iron ; with a point of diamond it is engraved on the tablet of their heart , and on the horns of their altars ,

groups.context_description: Term names God's steadfast love as the defining inner character of his covenantal disposition toward his people — the loyal, enduring, freely given love that grounds all relationship with him

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 34:6 The Lord passed before him and proclaimed , “The Lord , the Lord , a God merciful and gracious , slow to anger , and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness ,
Lam 3:22 The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases ; his mercies never come to an end ;

groups.context_description: Term names the human inner disposition of covenantal loyalty and loving-kindness — the settled character quality of faithfulness in relationship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 6:6 For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice , the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings .
Mic 6:8 He has told you , O man , what is good ; and what does the Lord require of you but to do justice , and to love kindness , and to walk humbly with your God ?

groups.context_description: Term describes the human appeal to and meditation on God's steadfast love as a sustaining inner orientation — the soul's trust and hope grounded in God's chesed

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 25:7 Remember not the sins of my youth or my transgressions ; according to your steadfast love remember me, for the sake of your goodness , O Lord !
Psa 48:9 We have thought on your steadfast love , O God , in the midst of your temple .

groups.context_description: Term names the responsive quality of inner covenantal mercy — the faithfulness of character that draws out corresponding faithfulness from God

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 22:26 “ With the merciful you show yourself merciful ; with the blameless man you show yourself blameless ;

groups.context_description: Term names the act of shaming another — the imposition of social-inner disgrace as a consequence of exposed wrongdoing

anchor_verses.verse_text: lest he who hears you bring shame upon you, and your ill repute have no end.

groups.context_description: Term names God as the inner person's refuge — the place of safety and strength to which the whole person turns in distress, fear, and trouble

anchor_verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster . Of the Sons of Korah . According to Alamoth . A Song . Psa 46:1 God is our refuge and strength , a very present help in trouble .
Psa 142:5 I cry to you , O Lord ; I say , “ You are my refuge , my portion in the land of the living .”

groups.context_description: Term names a misplaced inner refuge — false security found in lies or inadequate protection, which God will sweep away

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 28:15 Because you have said , “We have made a covenant with death , and with Sheol we have an agreement , when the overwhelming whip passes through it will not come to us, for we have made lies our refuge , and in falsehood we have taken shelter ”;

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of taking refuge in God — the whole-person orientation of fleeing to God as the source of protection, safety, and deliverance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 34:8 Oh, taste and see that the Lord is good ! Blessed is the man who takes refuge in him !

To the choirmaster : according to Do Not Destroy . A Miktam of David , when he fled from Saul , in the cave . Psa 57:1 Be merciful to me, O God , be merciful to me, for in you my soul takes refuge ; in the shadow of your wings I will take refuge , till the storms of destruction pass by .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of taking refuge in inadequate objects — misplaced refuge in Egypt, human power, or idols, contrasted with refuge in God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 30:2 who set out to go down to Egypt , without asking for my direction , to take refuge in the protection of Pharaoh and to seek shelter in the shadow of Egypt !

groups.context_description: Term names the eschatological possession of the kingdom by the saints — the inner being engaged through hope and the orientation toward God's ultimate vindication

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 7:18 But the saints of the Most High shall receive the kingdom and possess the kingdom forever , forever and ever .’

groups.context_description: Term names moral deficiency revealed by divine assessment — the inner being found morally wanting before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 5:27 Tekel , you have been weighed in the balances and found wanting ;

groups.context_description: Term names the inward suppression of speech under emotional or spiritual pressure, with deteriorating inner consequence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 39:2 I was mute and silent ; I held my peace to no avail , and my distress grew worse .

groups.context_description: Term names the moral dimension of silence — the choice to be silent or not at a moment of ethical weight

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 7:9 Then they said to one another , “We are not doing right . This day is a day of good news . If we are silent and wait until the morning light , punishment will overtake us. Now therefore come ; let us go and tell the king’s household .”

groups.context_description: Term names God's posture of silence and its breaking — divine restraint, patience, or resolve

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 57:11 Whom did you dread and fear , so that you lied , and did not remember me, did not lay it to heart ? Have I not held my peace , even for a long time , and you do not fear me?
Isa 64:12 Will you restrain yourself at these things , O Lord ? Will you keep silent , and afflict us so terribly ?

groups.context_description: Term names prophetic inner resolve — refusing silence in advocacy for God's purposes

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 62:1 For Zion’s sake I will not keep silent , and for Jerusalem’s sake I will not be quiet , until her righteousness goes forth as brightness , and her salvation as a burning torch .

groups.context_description: Term names the object of inner desire — what the person specifically longs for or purposes; desire as directed toward a concrete end

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 21:4 My heart staggers ; horror has appalled me; the twilight I longed for has been turned for me into trembling .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner longing or attachment of a person — the soul's desire for another person, or purposive desire directed toward what one wills to accomplish

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 34:8 But Hamor spoke with them, saying , “The soul of my son Shechem longs for your daughter . Please give her to him to be his wife .

groups.context_description: Term names divine inner attachment — God's love set on a person or people as an act of inner binding

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 7:7 It was not because you were more in number than any other people that the Lord set his love on you and chose you , for you were the fewest of all peoples ,

groups.context_description: Term names the deliberate devising of harm — the inner formation of schemes, plots, or evil plans against another

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mic 2:1 Woe to those who devise wickedness and work evil on their beds ! When the morning dawns , they perform it, because it is in the power of their hand .

groups.context_description: Term names purposive planning — the inner formation of a deliberate purpose or plan, whether by God or human agents

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 50:20 As for you , you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good , to bring it about that many people should be kept alive , as they are today .
Jer 29:11 For I know the plans I have for you, declares the Lord , plans for welfare and not for evil , to give you a future and a hope .

groups.context_description: Term names the moral crediting or imputing of righteousness/guilt — the inner act of reckoning a person's standing before God or human authority

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 15:6 And he believed the Lord , and he counted it to him as righteousness .
Psa 32:2 Blessed is the man against whom the Lord counts no iniquity , and in whose spirit there is no deceit .

groups.context_description: Term names the evaluative regard of persons or things — the inner act of assessing worth, status, or character in relational and social contexts

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 19:19 and said to the king , “Let not my lord hold me guilty or remember how your servant did wrong on the day my lord the king left Jerusalem . Do not let the king take it to heart .
Isa 53:3 He was despised and rejected by men , a man of sorrows , and acquainted with grief ; and as one from whom men hide their faces he was despised , and we esteemed him not .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of thinking and considering — the mind forming judgments, reconsidering paths, or holding deliberate thoughts

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:59 When I think on my ways , I turn my feet to your testimonies ;

groups.context_description: Term names the inner evaluative act of counting or regarding — assessing a person's or thing's worth, status, or standing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:4 Surely he has borne our griefs and carried our sorrows ; yet we esteemed him stricken , smitten by God , and afflicted .

groups.context_description: Term names the product of human ingenuity — devices or schemes formed by the inner inventive capacity, whether for construction or moral harm

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:29 See , this alone I found , that God made man upright , but they have sought out many schemes .

groups.context_description: Term names human inner thought and planning — the purposive deliberation of the heart, whether toward good or evil, faithful or rebellious ends

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 6:5 The Lord saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth , and that every intention of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually .
Pro 19:21 Many are the plans in the mind of a man , but it is the purpose of the Lord that will stand .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine thoughts and purposes — God's inner deliberative will that exceeds human understanding and stands forever

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 33:11 The counsel of the Lord stands forever , the plans of his heart to all generations .
Isa 55:8 For my thoughts are not your thoughts , neither are your ways my ways , declares the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the creative inner faculty of design — the skilled devising of form, pattern, or means, whether in craftsmanship or providential mercy

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 31:4 to devise artistic designs , to work in gold , silver , and bronze ,

2Sa 14:14 We must all die ; we are like water spilled on the ground , which cannot be gathered up again. But God will not take away life , and he devises means so that the banished one will not remain an outcast .

groups.context_description: Term names inner scheming — the deliberate devising of plans directed against another

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 35:20 For they do not speak peace , but against those who are quiet in the land they devise words of deceit .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner capacity for explanation and calculation — the rational faculty seeking to understand the scheme of things

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:25 I turned my heart to know and to search out and to seek wisdom and the scheme of things, and to know the wickedness of folly and the foolishness that is madness .

groups.context_description: Term names the personal confession of sin before God — the inner turning point at which the sinner names their sin, takes ownership, and turns toward God in acknowledgement

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:4 Against you, you only , have I sinned and done what is evil in your sight , so that you may be justified in your words and blameless in your judgment .
Psa 119:11 I have stored up your word in my heart , that I might not sin against you .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as the sustained inner-being orientation away from God — the pattern of covenantal unfaithfulness characterising individuals and generations who walk in idolatry

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 17:7 And this occurred because the people of Israel had sinned against the Lord their God , who had brought them up out of the land of Egypt from under the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt , and had feared other gods

groups.context_description: Term names the moral wisdom about sin — the universal reality that no one is without sin, the consequences sin brings, and the inner posture that safeguards against it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:20 Surely there is not a righteous man on earth who does good and never sins .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as the offence and burden carried by persons and peoples before God — the sin confessed, forgiven, covered, borne, and purged through divine grace

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:5 Behold , I was brought forth in iniquity , and in sin did my mother conceive me.
Isa 1:18 “ Come now , let us reason together, says the Lord : though your sins are like scarlet , they shall be as white as snow ; though they are red like crimson , they shall become like wool .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as the named offence confessed to and before God — the great sin acknowledged and the prayer for covering and forgiveness that follows

anchor_verses.verse_text: A Maskil of David . Psa 32:1 Blessed is the one whose transgression is forgiven , whose sin is covered .

groups.context_description: Inner state of dismay — inner person overwhelmed, destabilised, and paralysed by threat, shame, or collapse of what was trusted

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 1:17 But you , dress yourself for work; arise , and say to them everything that I command you. Do not be dismayed by them , lest I dismay you before them.

groups.context_description: Commanded absence of dismay — inner steadiness called for in the face of threat, opposition, or cosmic instability

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 51:7 “ Listen to me, you who know righteousness , the people in whose heart is my law ; fear not the reproach of man , nor be dismayed at their revilings .

groups.context_description: Inner terror induced by divine action — overwhelming of the inner person by God's power or judgment, including awe of covenantal reverence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 30:31 The Assyrians will be terror-stricken at the voice of the Lord , when he strikes with his rod .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as a moral-inner condition or state — the condition of wrongdoing that stands before God and requires forgiveness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 34:7 keeping steadfast love for thousands , forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin , but who will by no means clear the guilty , visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children and the children’s children, to the third and the fourth generation.”

groups.context_description: Term names the overwhelming pangs of extreme distress — anguish described through the labour-birth metaphor: seized, overtaking, coming upon

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 13:8 They will be dismayed : pangs and agony will seize them; they will be in anguish like a woman in labor . They will look aghast at one another ; their faces will be aflame .

groups.context_description: Term names the overwhelming pangs of extreme distress — anguish described through the labour-birth metaphor: seized, overtaking, coming upon

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 13:8 They will be dismayed : pangs and agony will seize them; they will be in anguish like a woman in labor . They will look aghast at one another ; their faces will be aflame .

groups.context_description: Term names the pangs of anguish or death — the inner-experiential condition of being gripped, bound, or overwhelmed by pain, distress, or the approach of death

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 116:3 The snares of death encompassed me; the pangs of Sheol laid hold on me; I suffered distress and anguish .
They will be dismayed: pangs and agony will seize them; they will be in anguish like a woman in labor. They will look aghast at one another; their faces will be aflame.

groups.context_description: Term names the relational binding through love or sin — cords used as a metaphor for the inner bonds of love, kindness, and faithfulness, or the binding power of sin and wickedness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 11:4 I led them with cords of kindness , with the bands of love , and I became to them as one who eases the yoke on their jaws , and I bent down to them and fed them.

groups.context_description: Life as contingent on inner-being orientation — obedience, fear, seeking, and choosing God as the condition of life; also surviving divine encounter

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 30:19 I call heaven and earth to witness against you today , that I have set before you life and death , blessing and curse . Therefore choose life , that you and your offspring may live ,

groups.context_description: Word of God and the Spirit as source of inner-being life — life received from God's word, promise, or Spirit as sustaining reality of the inner person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 8:3 And he humbled you and let you hunger and fed you with manna , which you did not know , nor did your fathers know , that he might make you know that man does not live by bread alone , but man lives by every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord .

groups.context_description: Divine revival of the inner person — God restoring life to the spirit and heart of those depleted, contrite, or despairing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 57:15 For thus says the One who is high and lifted up, who inhabits eternity , whose name is Holy : “I dwell in the high and holy place, and also with him who is of a contrite and lowly spirit , to revive the spirit of the lowly , and to revive the heart of the contrite .

groups.context_description: Inner-being wrestling with life — weight, value, perplexity, and hope of being alive in the face of death

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 14:14 If a man dies , shall he live again? All the days of my service I would wait , till my renewal should come .

groups.context_description: God's gift of reviving — inner-being renewal of a people or person after affliction, understood as divine favour overturning desolation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ezr 9:8 But now for a brief moment favor has been shown by the Lord our God , to leave us a remnant and to give us a secure hold within his holy place , that our God may brighten our eyes and grant us a little reviving in our slavery .

groups.context_description: Living God (Aramaic) as object of inner-being acknowledgment, worship, and trust — enduring reality as ground for inner person's orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 4:34 At the end of the days I , Nebuchadnezzar , lifted my eyes to heaven , and my reason returned to me, and I blessed the Most High , and praised and honored him who lives forever , for his dominion is an everlasting dominion , and his kingdom endures from generation to generation ;

groups.context_description: The living as those with capacity for knowledge of God and intercession — inner-being capacities distinctive to those alive

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 2:30 But as for me , this mystery has been revealed to me, not because of any wisdom that I have more than all the living , but in order that the interpretation may be made known to the king , and that you may know the thoughts of your mind .

groups.context_description: Living in inner-being suspension — physically alive but existentially and relationally confined, as if dead

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 20:3 And David came to his house at Jerusalem . And the king took the ten concubines whom he had left to care for the house and put them in a house under guard and provided for them, but did not go in to them. So they were shut up until the day of their death , living as if in widowhood .

groups.context_description: Living God as ground and object of the inner person's deepest orientation — soul's thirst, worship, and trust directed to the living God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 42:2 My soul thirsts for God , for the living God . When shall I come and appear before God ?

groups.context_description: Life as the God-given condition within which the inner person exists, fears, and responds — fearing God all one's days

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 33:11 Say to them, As I live , declares the Lord God , I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked , but that the wicked turn from his way and live ; turn back , turn back from your evil ways , for why will you die , O house of Israel ?

groups.context_description: Inner-being quality of the living — awareness of mortality, hope, wisdom, and thanksgiving as distinguishing capacities of those alive

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:5 For the living know that they will die , but the dead know nothing , and they have no more reward , for the memory of them is forgotten .

groups.context_description: Life as fruit of divine redemption and inner-being turning — restoration to life as outcome of repentance, trust, or divine care

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 19:25 For I know that my Redeemer lives , and at the last he will stand upon the earth .

groups.context_description: Term names forceful proclamation — a declaration made with commanding intensity that originates in an inner urgency or authority

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 4:14 He proclaimed aloud and said thus : ‘ Chop down the tree and lop off its branches , strip off its leaves and scatter its fruit . Let the beasts flee from under it and the birds from its branches .

groups.context_description: Hardening of the heart — wilful inner resistance to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 9:12 But the Lord hardened the heart of Pharaoh , and he did not listen to them, as the Lord had spoken to Moses .

groups.context_description: 'Be strong and courageous' — divine or prophetic command to inner courage grounded in divine presence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jos 1:9 Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous . Do not be frightened , and do not be dismayed , for the Lord your God is with you wherever you go .”

groups.context_description: Strengthening the weak — care for the inner being of another

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 35:4 Say to those who have an anxious heart , “Be strong ; fear not ! Behold , your God will come with vengeance , with the recompense of God . He will come and save you .”

groups.context_description: Self-strengthening in God — inner act of drawing on divine strength in crisis or mission

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 30:6 And David was greatly distressed , for the people spoke of stoning him, because all the people were bitter in soul , each for his sons and daughters . But David strengthened himself in the Lord his God .

groups.context_description: Strengthening that enables or reflects a moral orientation — toward good or ill

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 13:22 Because you have disheartened the righteous falsely , although I have not grieved him, and you have encouraged the wicked , that he should not turn from his evil way to save his life ,

groups.context_description: Holding fast to integrity, wisdom, or covenant

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 27:6 I hold fast my righteousness and will not let it go ; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days .

groups.context_description: God holding the person — divine grasp as reassurance and covenant

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 41:13 For I , the Lord your God , hold your right hand ; it is I who say to you, “ Fear not , I am the one who helps you .”

groups.context_description: Being seized by inner anguish, fear, or grief

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 4:27 And when she came to the mountain to the man of God , she caught hold of his feet . And Gehazi came to push her away . But the man of God said , “Leave her alone , for she is in bitter distress , and the Lord has hidden it from me and has not told me .”

groups.context_description: The inner-being act of grasping — idols or false support vs God

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 9:9 Then they will say , ‘ Because they abandoned the Lord their God who brought their fathers out of the land of Egypt and laid hold on other gods and worshiped them and served them. Therefore the Lord has brought all this disaster on them.’”

groups.context_description: Inner resolve expressed as firm insistence or determined compliance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 12:23 Only be sure that you do not eat the blood , for the blood is the life , and you shall not eat the life with the flesh .

groups.context_description: Prevailing through inner strength or resolve

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 17:50 So David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and with a stone , and struck the Philistine and killed him. There was no sword in the hand of David .

groups.context_description: Inner character in contest — resolute or irresolute, audacious or guile-driven

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 13:7 and certain worthless scoundrels gathered about him and defied Rehoboam the son of Solomon , when Rehoboam was young and irresolute and could not withstand them .

groups.context_description: Perseverance as inner-being posture — standing firm despite pressure, grounded in resolve or knowledge of God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 11:32 He shall seduce with flattery those who violate the covenant , but the people who know their God shall stand firm and take action .

groups.context_description: Force as coercive domination — inner disposition of aggression and self-willed domination

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 34:4 The weak you have not strengthened , the sick you have not healed , the injured you have not bound up , the strayed you have not brought back , the lost you have not sought , and with force and harshness you have ruled them.

groups.context_description: Force as urgent intensity in prayer and repentance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jon 3:8 but let man and beast be covered with sackcloth , and let them call out mightily to God . Let everyone turn from his evil way and from the violence that is in his hands .

groups.context_description: Comparative growth of strength — prevailing over opposition

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 3:1 There was a long war between the house of Saul and the house of David . And David grew stronger and stronger, while the house of Saul became weaker and weaker.

groups.context_description: Term names the inner dynamic where personal strength becomes the condition for pride and spiritual unfaithfulness — strength that displaces dependence on God

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 26:16 But when he was strong , he grew proud , to his destruction . For he was unfaithful to the Lord his God and entered the temple of the Lord to burn incense on the altar of incense .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine hand/strength that comes upon the prophet, orienting and commissioning his inner being

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 8:11 For the Lord spoke thus to me with his strong hand upon me, and warned me not to walk in the way of this people , saying :

groups.context_description: Term names God's redemptive power — the strong hand by which he delivered Israel from Egypt — as the basis for covenantal memory and obligation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 13:3 Then Moses said to the people , “ Remember this day in which you came out from Egypt , out of the house of slavery , for by a strong hand the Lord brought you out from this place. No leavened bread shall be eaten .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of self-reliant pride in one's own strength, set against dependence on God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Amo 6:13 you who rejoice in Lo-debar , who say , “Have we not by our own strength captured Karnaim for ourselves?”

groups.context_description: Term names God as the strength and sufficiency of the inner person, the object of love and relational orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster . A Psalm of David , the servant of the Lord , who addressed the words of this song to the Lord on the day when the Lord delivered him from the hand of all his enemies , and from the hand of Saul . He said : Psa 18:1 I love you, O Lord , my strength .

groups.context_description: Term names the joy that characterises life in God's presence and the celebration of his works — joy as the defining quality of divine worship

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ch 16:27 Splendor and majesty are before him; strength and joy are in his place .

Neh 8:10 Then he said to them, “ Go your way. Eat the fat and drink sweet wine and send portions to anyone who has nothing ready , for this day is holy to our Lord . And do not be grieved , for the joy of the Lord is your strength .”

groups.context_description: Term names rage as inner fury producing visible physical transformation and extreme action — the Aramaic term for anger at its most intense somatic expression

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 3:13 Then Nebuchadnezzar in furious rage commanded that Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego be brought . So they brought these men before the king .

groups.context_description: Term names divine wrath as consuming inner fire — the heat of God's anger poured out in judgment, spending itself completely

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 5:13 “Thus shall my anger spend itself , and I will vent my fury upon them and satisfy myself. And they shall know that I am the Lord —that I have spoken in my jealousy — when I spend my fury upon them .

groups.context_description: Term names anger as the inner state that transforms countenance and drives extreme action — human fury at its most visible and disruptive

anchor_verses.verse_text: Est 1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king’s command delivered by the eunuchs . At this the king became enraged , and his anger burned within him .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner heat of habitual anger — the fury characteristic as a settled disposition that requires management and produces destruction when uncontrolled

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 19:19 A man of great wrath will pay the penalty , for if you deliver him, you will only have to do it again .

groups.context_description: Term names divine compassion as the inward movement of mercy that acts on behalf of those in need, despite their unworthiness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 19:16 But he lingered . So the men seized him and his wife and his two daughters by the hand , the Lord being merciful to him, and they brought him out and set him outside the city .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine relational favour bestowed on a person — the inner-relational disposition of God toward those he knows by name, which creates standing, protection, and access, and which is sought, recognised, and treasured

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 33:17 And the Lord said to Moses , “ This very thing that you have spoken I will do , for you have found favor in my sight , and I know you by name .”

Zec 12:10 “And I will pour out on the house of David and the inhabitants of Jerusalem a spirit of grace and pleas for mercy , so that, when they look on me, on him whom they have pierced , they shall mourn for him, as one mourns for an only child , and weep bitterly over him, as one weeps over a firstborn .

groups.context_description: Term names relational favour between persons — the inner-relational disposition of goodwill one person seeks and receives from another, which creates access, loyalty, and life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rut 2:10 Then she fell on her face , bowing to the ground , and said to him, “ Why have I found favor in your eyes , that you should take notice of me , since I am a foreigner ?”

groups.context_description: Term names favour as inner character quality — the gracious disposition expressed in speech, conduct, and wisdom that generates standing and blessing in community

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 31:30 Charm is deceitful , and beauty is vain , but a woman who fears the Lord is to be praised .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of earnest supplication — the plea for mercy that arises from deep need and relational vulnerability before God, offered with the expectation that God hears and responds

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 9:3 Then I turned my face to the Lord God , seeking him by prayer and pleas for mercy with fasting and sackcloth and ashes .

Zec 12:10 “And I will pour out on the house of David and the inhabitants of Jerusalem a spirit of grace and pleas for mercy , so that, when they look on me, on him whom they have pierced , they shall mourn for him, as one mourns for an only child , and weep bitterly over him, as one weeps over a firstborn .

groups.context_description: Term names the bosom as the seat of intimate trusted relationship — the close inner circle of family and friendship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 16:5 And Sarai said to Abram , “May the wrong done to me be on you! I gave my servant to your embrace , and when she saw that she had conceived , she looked on me with contempt . May the Lord judge between you and me !”

groups.context_description: Term names the bosom as the place of cherishing — what the inner person holds closest

anchor_verses.verse_text: And the battle continued that day, and the king was propped up in his chariot facing the Syrians, until at evening he died. And the blood of the wound flowed into the bottom of the chariot.

groups.context_description: Term names the bosom as the seat of hidden inner knowledge — concealing transgression within oneself rather than confessing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 31:33 if I have concealed my transgressions as others do by hiding my iniquity in my heart ,

groups.context_description: Term names the loins as the seat of strength and generative power — the source of descendants and inner fortitude

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 35:11 And God said to him, “ I am God Almighty : be fruitful and multiply . A nation and a company of nations shall come from you, and kings shall come from your own body .

groups.context_description: Term names the loins girded for action — the inner readiness and preparation for challenge or service

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 38:3 Dress for action like a man ; I will question you, and you make it known to me .

groups.context_description: Term names God's steadfast love as his defining inner attribute — the covenantal faithfulness that never ceases, extends to heaven, and is the ground of Israel's hope and petition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 63:3 Because your steadfast love is better than life , my lips will praise you .
Lam 3:22 The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases ; his mercies never come to an end ;

groups.context_description: Term names human expressions of covenantal loyalty and kindness — the inner disposition of faithfulness between persons that mirrors divine chesed

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rut 3:10 And he said , “May you be blessed by the Lord , my daughter . You have made this last kindness greater than the first in that you have not gone after young men , whether poor or rich .
Pro 3:3 Let not steadfast love and faithfulness forsake you; bind them around your neck ; write them on the tablet of your heart .

groups.context_description: Term names the failure or withdrawal of chesed — its absence as the condition of moral and spiritual ruin; also the passionate demand for it as the priority above ritual

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 6:6 For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice , the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings .
Mic 6:8 He has told you , O man , what is good ; and what does the Lord require of you but to do justice , and to love kindness , and to walk humbly with your God ?

groups.context_description: Term names the godly or pious person — one whose inner character is shaped by covenantal faithfulness toward God, set apart for his purposes and preserved by him

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 4:3 But know that the Lord has set apart the godly for himself; the Lord hears when I call to him .
Psa 16:10 For you will not abandon my soul to Sheol , or let your holy one see corruption .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of domineering over others as an exercise of power — the inner being is engaged through the disposition of the one who dominates (absence of the fear of God) and the moral contrast with restrained, God-fearing authority

anchor_verses.verse_text: Neh 5:15 The former governors who were before me laid heavy burdens on the people and took from them for their daily ration forty shekels of silver . Even their servants lorded it over the people . But I did not do so , because of the fear of God .

groups.context_description: Term names authority as a capacity given by God or exercised by a person — engaging the inner being through bestowal, pride, or exercise of sovereign power

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 2:37 You , O king , the king of kings , to whom the God of heaven has given the kingdom , the power , and the might , and the glory ,

groups.context_description: Term names governing authority held by a person by God's appointment — the inner being is engaged through the exercise of that authority in providential circumstances that draw out submission, recognition, and the outworking of prior inner-being history

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 42:6 Now Joseph was governor over the land . He was the one who sold to all the people of the land . And Joseph’s brothers came and bowed themselves before him with their faces to the ground .

groups.context_description: Term names power as the capacity to command or control — the inner being is engaged through the recognition of the limits of human power over spirit and death, and through the prudent posture of subjects under sovereign authority

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:8 No man has power to retain the spirit , or power over the day of death . There is no discharge from war , nor will wickedness deliver those who are given to it.

groups.context_description: Term names the capacity to rule or exercise power over others — the inner being is engaged through the divine granting of rule, the character of the one appointed to authority, and the demonstration that earthly power has limits before divine protection

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 2:38 and into whose hand he has given , wherever they dwell , the children of man , the beasts of the field , and the birds of the heavens , making you rule over them all — you are the head of gold .

groups.context_description: Term names the beginning that discloses an inner-being condition — either the moment when prayer begins and God responds immediately, or the role of leadership in praise

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 9:23 At the beginning of your pleas for mercy a word went out , and I have come to tell it to you, for you are greatly loved . Therefore consider the word and understand the vision .

groups.context_description: Term names God's inner disposition of grace freely expressed toward persons — the divine movement of favour toward the undeserving

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 33:19 And he said , “I will make all my goodness pass before you and will proclaim before you my name ‘The Lord .’ And I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious , and will show mercy on whom I will show mercy .
Num 6:25 the Lord make his face to shine upon you and be gracious to you ;

groups.context_description: Term names the human inner act of seeking grace — the disposition of humble appeal directed toward God or another from a place of need and dependence

anchor_verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster : with stringed instruments . A Psalm of David . Psa 4:1 Answer me when I call , O God of my righteousness ! You have given me relief when I was in distress . Be gracious to me and hear my prayer !
Psa 31:9 Be gracious to me, O Lord , for I am in distress ; my eye is wasted from grief ; my soul and my body also.

groups.context_description: Term designates the inner character of graciousness — the settled disposition of the person (primarily God) to extend favour and mercy freely to those who need it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 34:6 The Lord passed before him and proclaimed , “The Lord , the Lord , a God merciful and gracious , slow to anger , and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness ,
Psa 103:8 The Lord is merciful and gracious , slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love .

groups.context_description: Terror spread in the land of the living — the dread imposed by military powers upon subject populations as an inner-being condition of the dominated

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 32:27 And they do not lie with the mighty , the fallen from among the uncircumcised , who went down to Sheol with their weapons of war , whose swords were laid under their heads , and whose iniquities are upon their bones ; for the terror of the mighty men was in the land of the living .

Eze 32:32 For I spread terror in the land of the living ; and he shall be laid to rest among the uncircumcised , with those who are slain by the sword , Pharaoh and all his multitude , declares the Lord God .”

groups.context_description: Terror as inner experience — the dread felt by the person, whether before God or at the threat of enemies; including terror directed toward and away from the person in relation to divine presence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 54:14 In righteousness you shall be established ; you shall be far from oppression , for you shall not fear ; and from terror , for it shall not come near you .
Jer 17:17 Be not a terror to me; you are my refuge in the day of disaster .

groups.context_description: Divine terror as an inner state fallen upon persons — the God-sent terror that falls on surrounding peoples, producing an inner condition that prevents hostile action

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 35:5 And as they journeyed , a terror from God fell upon the cities that were around them, so that they did not pursue the sons of Jacob .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of waiting with hope — the expectant orientation of the soul toward God's action, help, or timing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 33:20 Our soul waits for the Lord ; he is our help and our shield .
Isa 30:18 Therefore the Lord waits to be gracious to you, and therefore he exalts himself to show mercy to you. For the Lord is a God of justice ; blessed are all those who wait for him .

groups.context_description: Term names the deepest inner yearning of the soul — intense longing for the sanctuary as cherished object

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 24:21 ‘ Say to the house of Israel , Thus says the Lord God : Behold , I will profane my sanctuary , the pride of your power , the delight of your eyes , and the yearning of your soul , and your sons and your daughters whom you left behind shall fall by the sword .

groups.context_description: Term names wealth/treasure as a moral category — where the inner character of the person determines whether wealth becomes blessing or snare

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 15:6 In the house of the righteous there is much treasure , but trouble befalls the income of the wicked .
Isa 33:6 and he will be the stability of your times , abundance of salvation , wisdom , and knowledge ; the fear of the Lord is Zion’s treasure .

groups.context_description: Term names the anointing of inner perception — the invitation to receive what opens the eyes of the heart to see one's true spiritual condition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 3:18 I counsel you to buy from me gold refined by fire , so that you may be rich , and white garments so that you may clothe yourself and the shame of your nakedness may not be seen , and salve to anoint your eyes , so that you may see .

groups.context_description: Term names the anointing of the eyes as the restoration of inner perception — the outer act whose purpose is the return of the capacity to see

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 9:11 He answered , “The man called Jesus made mud and anointed my eyes and said to me , ‘ Go to Siloam and wash .’ So I went and washed and received my sight .”

groups.context_description: Term names the anointing of the Spirit as inner knowledge and protection — the anointing that remains in the person and teaches them from within, guarding against deception

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Jo 2:27 But the anointing that you received from him abides in you , and you have no need that anyone should teach you . But as his anointing teaches you about everything , and is true , and is no lie — just as it has taught you , abide in him .

groups.context_description: Term names the anointing of the Spirit as inner knowledge and protection — the anointing that remains in the person and teaches them from within, guarding against deception

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Jo 2:27 But the anointing that you received from him abides in you , and you have no need that anyone should teach you . But as his anointing teaches you about everything , and is true , and is no lie — just as it has taught you , abide in him .

groups.context_description: Term names the Christ as the anointed one whose identity is the object of inner faith — the person of Jesus as the ground of life through belief

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 20:31 but these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ , the Son of God , and that by believing you may have life in his name .

groups.context_description: Term names union with Christ as the defining inner reality of the person — being in Christ as the location of the transformed inner life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 8:1 There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus .
