groups.context_description: Term names the death and resurrection of Christ as the inner transformative foundation — Christ died for sins; this is received truth on which the inner life rests and on which hope of resurrection stands

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 5:8 but God shows his love for us in that while we were still sinners , Christ died for us .

groups.context_description: Term names the anointing of Jesus with the Holy Spirit as the equipping for inner authority and action — Spirit-reception as the source of inner capacity for ministry

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 10:38 how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power . He went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil , for God was with him .

groups.context_description: Term names the anointing of the believer by God as the ground of inner certainty — divine anointing as the establishing act that makes the person firm in faith

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:21 And it is God who establishes us with you in Christ , and has anointed us ,

groups.context_description: Kindness as inner quality of love — outward expression of love's inner character toward others

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 13:4 Love is patient and kind ; love does not envy or boast ; it is not arrogant

groups.context_description: Deceptive inner disposition expressed as flattering speech — false kindness targeting the naive

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 16:18 For such persons do not serve our Lord Christ , but their own appetites , and by smooth talk and flattery they deceive the hearts of the naive .

groups.context_description: Goodness/kindness as inner quality of God or persons — disposition of gracious favour expressed toward others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 2:4 Or do you presume on the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience , not knowing that God’s kindness is meant to lead you to repentance ?
Eph 4:32 Be kind to one another , tenderhearted , forgiving one another , as God in Christ forgave you .

groups.context_description: Term names kindness as God's inner disposition of generous goodwill toward humanity — the divine attribute that leads to repentance, governs judgment and mercy, and is displayed supremely in Christ

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 11:22 Note then the kindness and the severity of God : severity toward those who have fallen , but God’s kindness to you , provided you continue in his kindness . Otherwise you too will be cut off .

groups.context_description: Term names kindness as a Spirit-produced inner quality of the believer — the disposition to act with goodness toward others, listed as fruit of the Spirit and garment of the renewed person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gal 5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love , joy , peace , patience , kindness , goodness , faithfulness ,

groups.context_description: Term describes pity and compassion as an inner disposition that moves toward or spares those in need — whether in a person or in God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 72:13 He has pity on the weak and the needy , and saves the lives of the needy .
Jon 4:11 And should not I pity Nineveh , that great city , in which there are more than 120,000 persons who do not know their right hand from their left , and also much cattle ?”

groups.context_description: Term names the withholding of pity as a judicial inner disposition — the inner setting aside of compassionate impulse in the service of righteous judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 19:13 Your eye shall not pity him , but you shall purge the guilt of innocent blood from Israel , so that it may be well with you .
Eze 7:4 And my eye will not spare you, nor will I have pity , but I will punish you for your ways , while your abominations are in your midst . Then you will know that I am the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner command to take heart — courage addressed to the inner life, grounded in Christ's presence and victory

anchor_verses.verse_text: And behold, some people brought to him a paralytic, lying on a bed. And when Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Take heart, my son; your sins are forgiven.”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner loss of heart — the fading of inner courage and resolve in the face of suffering or opposition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 18:1 And he told them a parable to the effect that they ought always to pray and not lose heart .

groups.context_description: Term connects and qualifies inner-being content — directing attention to persons or conditions where inner life is expressed

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 142:3 When my spirit faints within me, you know my way ! In the path where I walk they have hidden a trap for me .
Psa 143:8 Let me hear in the morning of your steadfast love , for in you I trust . Make me know the way I should go , for to you I lift up my soul .

groups.context_description: Term frames inner reflection on meaning and worth of human experience — wisdom on joy, futility, divine gifting

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:24 There is nothing better for a person than that he should eat and drink and find enjoyment in his toil . This also , I saw , is from the hand of God ,
Ecc 5:19 Everyone also to whom God has given wealth and possessions and power to enjoy them , and to accept his lot and rejoice in his toil — this is the gift of God .

groups.context_description: Term directs and intensifies expression of inner pride — pointing to object of self-glorification

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 4:30 and the king answered and said , “Is not this great Babylon , which I have built by my mighty power as a royal residence and for the glory of my majesty ?”

groups.context_description: Term directs attention to consequence of inner moral orientation — outcome of covenant faithfulness or spiritual infidelity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 132:12 If your sons keep my covenant and my testimonies that I shall teach them, their sons also forever shall sit on your throne .”

Hos 7:16 They return , but not upward ; they are like a treacherous bow ; their princes shall fall by the sword because of the insolence of their tongue . This shall be their derision in the land of Egypt .

groups.context_description: Term names the disciple's inner experience of being delivered up for Christ's sake — requiring trust in divine provision

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 10:19 When they deliver you over, do not be anxious how you are to speak or what you are to say , for what you are to say will be given to you in that hour .

groups.context_description: Term names the entrustment of stewardship — the master's trust in servants and the servant's calling of faithful management

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 25:14 “ For it will be like a man going on a journey , who called his servants and entrusted to them his property .

groups.context_description: Term names continual inner self-entrustment to God — trusting self-surrender to the one who judges justly under unjust suffering

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Pe 2:23 When he was reviled , he did not revile in return ; when he suffered , he did not threaten , but continued entrusting himself to him who judges justly .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner moral act of giving oneself over to desire — the hardened self-abandonment to sensuality

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 4:19 They have become callous and have given themselves up to sensuality , greedy to practice every kind of impurity .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine delegation of all authority — the Father handing over all things to the Son

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 11:27 All things have been handed over to me by my Father , and no one knows the Son except the Father , and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.

groups.context_description: Term names disciplinary delivering over to Satan — aimed at the inner restoration and salvation of the spirit

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 5:5 you are to deliver this man to Satan for the destruction of the flesh , so that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the heart's obedience to a received pattern of teaching — the inner act of self-surrender to a shaping tradition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 6:17 But thanks be to God , that you who were once slaves of sin have become obedient from the heart to the standard of teaching to which you were committed ,

groups.context_description: Term in the water/Spirit contrast — water as the outward insufficient sign pointing to inner transformation of Spirit baptism

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 3:11 “ I baptize you with water for repentance , but he who is coming after me is mightier than I , whose sandals I am not worthy to carry . He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire .

groups.context_description: Term as living water — metaphor for inner spiritual life permanently satisfying inner thirst, welling up to eternal life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 4:14 but whoever drinks of the water that I will give him will never be thirsty again. The water that I will give him will become in him a spring of water welling up to eternal life .”

groups.context_description: Term in the context of inner cleansing — water as instrument of purification touching conscience and moral state

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 10:22 let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith , with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water .

groups.context_description: Term associated with inner anguish and desperate craving — the tormented soul's thirst for relief

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 16:24 And he called out , ‘ Father Abraham , have mercy on me , and send Lazarus to dip the end of his finger in water and cool my tongue , for I am in anguish in this flame .’

groups.context_description: Term in the act of washing hands as moral self-exculpation — the external act revealing inner conflict over guilt

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 27:24 So when Pilate saw that he was gaining nothing , but rather that a riot was beginning , he took water and washed his hands before the crowd , saying , “I am innocent of this man’s blood ; see to it yourselves .”

groups.context_description: Term as water of life in eschatological promise — eternal inner satisfaction and refreshment of the redeemed

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 22:17 The Spirit and the Bride say , “ Come .” And let the one who hears say , “ Come .” And let the one who is thirsty come ; let the one who desires take the water of life without price .

groups.context_description: Term as element of faith-testing — water on which the person either walks by trust or sinks through doubt

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 14:29 He said , “ Come .” So Peter got out of the boat and walked on the water and came to Jesus .

groups.context_description: Term revealing inner disposition through contrast — water not given (indifference) vs. tears given (love)

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 7:44 Then turning toward the woman he said to Simon , “Do you see this woman ? I entered your house ; you gave me no water for my feet , but she has wet my feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair .

groups.context_description: Term names the gift as an act of worship — offering given as expression of inner devotion toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 2:11 And going into the house , they saw the child with Mary his mother , and they fell down and worshiped him . Then , opening their treasures , they offered him gifts , gold and frankincense and myrrh .

groups.context_description: Term names the moral claim of relational conscience amid worship — the gift interrupted by reconciliation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 5:23 So if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you ,

groups.context_description: Term names misuse of religious dedication as pretext for moral evasion — piety masking filial failure

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 15:5 But you say , ‘If anyone tells his father or his mother , “What you would have gained from me is given to God,”

groups.context_description: Term names inner inadequacy of ritual gifts to perfect the conscience — gap between external offering and inner transformation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 9:9 ( which is symbolic for the present age ). According to this arrangement, gifts and sacrifices are offered that cannot perfect the conscience of the worshiper ,

groups.context_description: Term names salvation as God's free gift — not earned but received, establishing inner dependence and gratitude

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 2:8 For by grace you have been saved through faith . And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God ,

groups.context_description: Term names radical self-giving generosity — total offering from poverty as expression of complete inner trust

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 21:4 For they all contributed out of their abundance , but she out of her poverty put in all she had to live on .”

groups.context_description: Term names the divine free gift received by the person — grace, righteousness, calling — that transforms the inner being and establishes standing before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 4:10 Jesus answered her , “ If you knew the gift of God , and who it is that is saying to you , ‘ Give me a drink ,’ you would have asked him , and he would have given you living water .”
Act 2:38 And Peter said to them , “ Repent and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins , and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit .

groups.context_description: Term names inner disposition of good giving — a father giving to a son who asks; and God's generous orientation toward those who ask

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 15:30 So when they were sent off , they went down to Antioch , and having gathered the congregation together , they delivered the letter .

groups.context_description: Term names the giving that designates the betrayer — the final extension of grace in the moment before betrayal

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 4:17 And the scroll of the prophet Isaiah was given to him . He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written ,

groups.context_description: Term names inner self-restrained generosity — giving to those who cannot repay, oriented toward divine reward

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 14:14 and you will be blessed , because they cannot repay you . For you will be repaid at the resurrection of the just .”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner relinquishing of personal vengeance — entrusting retribution to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 12:19 Beloved , never avenge yourselves , but leave it to the wrath of God, for it is written , “ Vengeance is mine , I will repay , says the Lord .”

groups.context_description: Term expresses impulse to return thanksgiving — gratitude as inner response to grace

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Th 3:9 For what thanksgiving can we return to God for you , for all the joy that we feel for your sake before our God ,

groups.context_description: Term names God's just inner commitment to repay — divine retributive justice; impossibility of placing God in debt

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Th 1:6 since indeed God considers it just to repay with affliction those who afflict you ,

groups.context_description: Term names inner disposition of radical generosity — distributing to those in need from a transformed orientation toward wealth

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 18:22 When Jesus heard this , he said to him , “ One thing you still lack . Sell all that you have and distribute to the poor , and you will have treasure in heaven ; and come , follow me .”

groups.context_description: Term names surrender of power as expression of inner allegiance — handing authority to the object of devotion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 17:13 These are of one mind , and they hand over their power and authority to the beast .

groups.context_description: Term names inner disposition of sharing — giving from oneself to another in need from love and reoriented will; extending even to sharing one's very self

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Th 2:8 So , being affectionately desirous of you , we were ready to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own selves , because you had become very dear to us .

groups.context_description: Term names God's granting of all things needed for inner transformation — life, godliness, participation in the divine nature

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Pe 1:4 by which he has granted to us his precious and very great promises , so that through them you may become partakers of the divine nature , having escaped from the corruption that is in the world because of sinful desire .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine perfect gift — every good gift from above; gift bringing justification where condemnation was due

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 1:17 Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above , coming down from the Father of lights , with whom there is no variation or shadow due to change .

groups.context_description: Term names the impossibility of preceding God in giving — no person can give first so as to put God in debt; establishing pure creaturely dependence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 11:35 “ Or who has given a gift to him that he might be repaid ?”

groups.context_description: Term names terror sent by God as an instrument of divine power — the overwhelming inner dread God places on enemies before Israel

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 15:16 Terror and dread fall upon them; because of the greatness of your arm , they are still as a stone , till your people , O Lord , pass by , till the people pass by whom you have purchased .
Exo 23:27 I will send my terror before you and will throw into confusion all the people against whom you shall come , and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.

groups.context_description: Term names the terror of God experienced by the individual — the inner condition of acute dread before divine presence or judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 9:34 Let him take his rod away from me, and let not dread of him terrify me .
Psa 55:4 My heart is in anguish within me; the terrors of death have fallen upon me .

groups.context_description: Term names terror as the quality of overwhelming power — the dread that powerful persons, creatures, or situations generate in others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 41:14 Who can open the doors of his face ? Around his teeth is terror .
Pro 20:2 The terror of a king is like the growling of a lion ; whoever provokes him to anger forfeits his life .

groups.context_description: Term names the oneness of covenantal union — the 'one flesh' or 'one heart and one way' that defines the depth of relationship between persons, or between God and his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 32:39 I will give them one heart and one way , that they may fear me forever , for their own good and the good of their children after them .

Mal 2:15 Did he not make them one , with a portion of the Spirit in their union? And what was the one God seeking ? Godly offspring . So guard yourselves in your spirit , and let none of you be faithless to the wife of your youth .

groups.context_description: Term names the corporate oneness of inner resolve — the united will of a community described as one man or one people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 20:1 Then all the people of Israel came out , from Dan to Beersheba , including the land of Gilead , and the congregation assembled as one man to the Lord at Mizpah .

groups.context_description: Term names the uniqueness of God or of the irreplaceable — the one that shapes the weight of divine identity or the cost of a singular relationship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 22:2 He said , “ Take your son , your only son Isaac , whom you love , and go to the land of Moriah , and offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains of which I shall tell you .”
Job 23:13 But he is unchangeable , and who can turn him back? What he desires , that he does .

groups.context_description: Term names the quantifier that intensifies inner-being experience — the one that shapes the subjective inner weight

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 29:20 So Jacob served seven years for Rachel , and they seemed to him but a few days because of the love he had for her .

groups.context_description: Patient endurance as inner character — anechō as expression of the inner disposition of forbearance toward others and under trial

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 4:2 with all humility and gentleness , with patience , bearing with one another in love ,

groups.context_description: Inner disposition toward received teaching — anechō as the will's posture of acceptance or rejection of truth

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ti 4:3 For the time is coming when people will not endure sound teaching , but having itching ears they will accumulate for themselves teachers to suit their own passions ,

groups.context_description: Spiritual receptivity — echō as indicator of the inner capacity to hear and respond to divine word

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 11:15 He who has ears to hear , let him hear .

groups.context_description: Possession of spiritual endowments — echō as the condition of having or lacking love, faith and the Spirit as inner realities

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 13:2 And if I have prophetic powers , and understand all mysteries and all knowledge , and if I have all faith , so as to remove mountains , but have not love , I am nothing .

groups.context_description: Emotive inner states — echō as carrier of compassion, fear, joy and zeal as active inner movements

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 9:36 When he saw the crowds , he had compassion for them , because they were harassed and helpless , like sheep without a shepherd .

groups.context_description: Inner deficit and corruption — echō as indicator of rootlessness, debased mind and inner spiritual lack

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 13:21 yet he has no root in himself , but endures for a while , and when tribulation or persecution arises on account of the word , immediately he falls away .

groups.context_description: Peace, hope and access — echō as the condition of holding peace, hope and relational access to God as inner states

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 5:1 Therefore , since we have been justified by faith , we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ .

groups.context_description: Inner cognitive and moral faculties — echō as the condition of possessing or lacking knowledge, conscience and inner discernment

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:1 Now concerning food offered to idols : we know that “ all of us possess knowledge .” This “ knowledge ” puffs up , but love builds up .

groups.context_description: Will, motivation and inner orientation — echō as the condition of the volitional and motivational inner life

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 9:17 For if I do this of my own will , I have a reward , but if not of my own will , I am still entrusted with a stewardship .

groups.context_description: The testimony as the object of inner heart-engagement — the divine testimony to which the heart is inclined, which the soul keeps, which is the ground of whole-hearted covenant renewal

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 23:3 And the king stood by the pillar and made a covenant before the Lord , to walk after the Lord and to keep his commandments and his testimonies and his statutes with all his heart and all his soul , to perform the words of this covenant that were written in this book . And all the people joined in the covenant .
Psa 119:36 Incline my heart to your testimonies , and not to selfish gain !

groups.context_description: Testimony sealed within disciples — the divine testimony committed to an inner circle, with spiritual condition (having or lacking dawn) as the measure of reception

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 8:16 Bind up the testimony ; seal the teaching among my disciples .

groups.context_description: Witnessing as inner moral act and relational accountability — the bearing of witness (true or false) as an expression of inner integrity; God as inner-relational witness; prophetic witness calling to inner repentance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 31:50 If you oppress my daughters , or if you take wives besides my daughters , although no one is with us, see , God is witness between you and me .”
Exo 20:16 “You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor .

groups.context_description: Prophetic testimony as directed at inner orientation — the divine and prophetic warning that calls to inner repentance, obedience, and spiritual receptivity; including testimony that reveals inner hardness or receptivity

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 17:13 Yet the Lord warned Israel and Judah by every prophet and every seer , saying , “ Turn from your evil ways and keep my commandments and my statutes , in accordance with all the Law that I commanded your fathers , and that I sent to you by my servants the prophets .”

Jer 6:10 To whom shall I speak and give warning , that they may hear ? Behold , their ears are uncircumcised , they cannot listen ; behold , the word of the Lord is to them an object of scorn ; they take no pleasure in it .

groups.context_description: Divine testimonies as the object of inner devotion — the testimonies kept with the whole heart, delighted in, clung to, and received as counselors; the inner states of joy, delight, and steadfast love that the testimonies evoke

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:2 Blessed are those who keep his testimonies , who seek him with their whole heart ,
Psa 119:24 Your testimonies are my delight ; they are my counselors .

groups.context_description: Inner steadfastness in the face of external snare — not forgetting the law as an act of inner faithfulness despite constraint

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:61 Though the cords of the wicked ensnare me, I do not forget your law .

groups.context_description: Term names the idol as the object of misplaced inner orientation — the thing toward which the person's allegiance, rejoicing, or worship is wrongly directed

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Th 1:9 For they themselves report concerning us the kind of reception we had among you , and how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God ,

groups.context_description: Term names the idol in relation to inner conviction or conscience — in theological understanding, moral formation, or the incompatibility of idol and God within the person

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 6:16 What agreement has the temple of God with idols ? For we are the temple of the living God ; as God said , “I will make my dwelling among them and walk among them, and I will be their God , and they shall be my people .

groups.context_description: Term names food sacrificed to idols as the occasion of an inner conscience crisis — the act of eating or abstaining governed by the conscience, whose strength or weakness determines the moral outcome

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:7 However , not all possess this knowledge . But some , through former association with idols , eat food as really offered to an idol , and their conscience , being weak , is defiled .

groups.context_description: Term names the idolater as a person whose inner orientation is toward created things rather than God — a defining inner condition with eschatological consequences

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 10:7 Do not be idolaters as some of them were; as it is written , “The people sat down to eat and drink and rose up to play .”

groups.context_description: Term names the idol's temple as the site where a brother's weak conscience can be damaged — the location triggers an inner crisis of conscience in the weak

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:10 For if anyone sees you who have knowledge eating in an idol’s temple , will he not be encouraged , if his conscience is weak , to eat food offered to idols ?

groups.context_description: Term names a city full of idols — the external condition that provokes an inner response of the spirit, disclosing the person's inner sensitivity to God's honour

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 17:16 Now while Paul was waiting for them at Athens , his spirit was provoked within him as he saw that the city was full of idols .

groups.context_description: Term names idolatry as an inner orientation — the displacement of God by created things as the object of the heart's ultimate allegiance, whether in covetousness, passion, or formal worship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Col 3:5 Put to death therefore what is earthly in you: sexual immorality , impurity , passion , evil desire , and covetousness , which is idolatry .

groups.context_description: Term describes the human person's constitutive dignity as image-bearer — what the person is by divine creation

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 11:7 For a man ought not to cover his head , since he is the image and glory of God , but woman is the glory of man .

groups.context_description: Term describes the ongoing renewal and conformity of the inner person toward the divine image

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 3:18 And we all , with unveiled face , beholding the glory of the Lord , are being transformed into the same image from one degree of glory to another . For this comes from the Lord who is the Spirit .

groups.context_description: Term identifies the object of misplaced worship, revealing the person's inner orientation of false allegiance or faithful refusal

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:23 and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling mortal man and birds and animals and creeping things .

groups.context_description: Term frames a simile that characterises an inner state — self-deception or inner instability

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 1:6 But let him ask in faith , with no doubting , for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea that is driven and tossed by the wind .

groups.context_description: Term names sincerity as an inner quality of unmixed, genuine motivation — the condition of acting from pure inner integrity before God rather than from calculation or performance

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:12 For our boast is this , the testimony of our conscience , that we behaved in the world with simplicity and godly sincerity , not by earthly wisdom but by the grace of God , and supremely so toward you .

groups.context_description: Term names inner purity or sincerity — the quality of being unmixed, genuine, and morally uncontaminated in inner disposition

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Pe 3:1 This is now the second letter that I am writing to you , beloved . In both of them I am stirring up your sincere mind by way of reminder ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition and active practice of relational peace — the will directed toward harmony with others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 12:18 If possible , so far as it depends on you , live peaceably with all .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-being peace given by God through Christ — the reconciled state before God and the interior experience of divine peace guarding the heart and mind

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 14:27 Peace I leave with you ; my peace I give to you . Not as the world gives do I give to you . Let not your hearts be troubled , neither let them be afraid .
Phili 4:7 And the peace of God , which surpasses all understanding , will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus .

groups.context_description: Term names peace as apostolic blessing and greeting — the invocation of divine shalom upon persons in the community of Christ

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:7 To all those in Rome who are loved by God and called to be saints : Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ .

groups.context_description: Term names peace as the eschatological and relational fruit of the kingdom — achieved by Christ, to be pursued in community and received as the Spirit's gift

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gal 5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love , joy , peace , patience , kindness , goodness , faithfulness ,
Eph 2:14 For he himself is our peace , who has made us both one and has broken down in his flesh the dividing wall of hostility

groups.context_description: Term names peaceableness as an inner character quality — the disposition of the trained person or the wisdom from above, producing righteous fruit

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 3:17 But the wisdom from above is first pure , then peaceable , gentle , open to reason , full of mercy and good fruits , impartial and sincere .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of making peace — Christ's reconciling work that restores the broken relationship between humanity and God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Col 1:20 and through him to reconcile to himself all things , whether on earth or in heaven , making peace by the blood of his cross .

groups.context_description: Term introduces the inner source of action, identity, or righteousness — from the heart, from faith, from God, from grace — disclosing the inner-being origin of obedience, spiritual life, and right standing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 6:17 But thanks be to God , that you who were once slaves of sin have become obedient from the heart to the standard of teaching to which you were committed ,

groups.context_description: Term introduces source in the context of resurrection and new life — out of death, from among the dead — signalling the inner transformation of the person through divine power

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 6:4 We were buried therefore with him by baptism into death , in order that , just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father , we too might walk in newness of life .

groups.context_description: Term names a signal or banner that orients the inner life — the object to which hope, fear, or trust is directed, or from which the inner life flees in panic

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 60:4 You have set up a banner for those who fear you, that they may flee to it from the bow . Selah
Isa 11:10 In that day the root of Jesse , who shall stand as a signal for the peoples —of him shall the nations inquire , and his resting place shall be glorious .

groups.context_description: Term names the relational orientation of Israel toward God — the shift from servile master to intimate husband naming the transformation of inner relationship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 2:16 “And in that day , declares the Lord , you will call me ‘My Husband ,’ and no longer will you call me ‘ My Baal .’

groups.context_description: Term names the jealousy of God as a defining inner characteristic — God's exclusive passionate claim on his people's worship and loyalty

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 34:14 ( for you shall worship no other god , for the Lord , whose name is Jealous , is a jealous God ),

groups.context_description: Term names God as the object of direct personal address, petition, trust, and relational orientation — the God toward whom the inner being is turned

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 12:13 And Moses cried to the Lord , “O God , please heal her — please .”
Job 5:8 “As for me , I would seek God , and to God would I commit my cause ,

groups.context_description: Term names God's character or nature as an attribute that calls forth or grounds inner response — what God is like that evokes fear, trust, wonder, or moral conviction

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 23:19 God is not man , that he should lie , or a son of man , that he should change his mind . Has he said , and will he not do it? Or has he spoken , and will he not fulfill it ?
Mic 7:18 Who is a God like you, pardoning iniquity and passing over transgression for the remnant of his inheritance ? He does not retain his anger forever , because he delights in steadfast love .

groups.context_description: Term names God's identity in theological declaration — assertions of his singularity, incomparability, and saving nature that shape belief and knowledge

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 12:2 “ Behold , God is my salvation ; I will trust , and will not be afraid ; for the Lord God is my strength and my song , and he has become my salvation .”
Isa 45:22 “ Turn to me and be saved , all the ends of the earth ! For I am God , and there is no other .

groups.context_description: Term names mercifulness as an inner character quality — the blessed disposition of those who show mercy and the defining character of Christ as high priest

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 5:7 “ Blessed are the merciful , for they shall receive mercy .

Heb 2:17 Therefore he had to be made like his brothers in every respect, so that he might become a merciful and faithful high priest in the service of God , to make propitiation for the sins of the people .

groups.context_description: Term names mercilessness as an inner character failure — one of the marks of those whose inner moral fabric has collapsed

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:31 foolish , faithless , heartless , ruthless .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of divine mercy — God having mercy on whom he wills as the ground of salvation and sovereign grace

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 9:15 For he says to Moses , “I will have mercy on whom I have mercy , and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion .”
1Pe 2:10 Once you were not a people , but now you are God’s people ; once you had not received mercy , but now you have received mercy .

groups.context_description: Term names the cry for mercy — the supplicant's inner anguish and hope directed toward Jesus as healer and Saviour

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 10:48 And many rebuked him , telling him to be silent . But he cried out all the more , “ Son of David , have mercy on me !”
Luk 18:38 And he cried out , “ Jesus , Son of David , have mercy on me !”

groups.context_description: Term names the human act of mercy as the inner disposition that receives mercy in return

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 5:7 “ Blessed are the merciful , for they shall receive mercy .
Mat 18:33 And should not you have had mercy on your fellow servant , as I had mercy on you ?’

groups.context_description: Term names God's mercy as his defining inner attribute — the ground of salvation, the content of covenant faithfulness, and the wellspring of new life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 1:78 because of the tender mercy of our God , whereby the sunrise shall visit us from on high
Eph 2:4 But God , being rich in mercy , because of the great love with which he loved us ,

groups.context_description: Term names mercy as the inner-being virtue and judicial reality — what God desires above ritual, what the wise show, what triumphs over judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 23:23 “ Woe to you , scribes and Pharisees , hypocrites ! For you tithe mint and dill and cumin , and have neglected the weightier matters of the law : justice and mercy and faithfulness . These you ought to have done , without neglecting the others .
Jam 3:17 But the wisdom from above is first pure , then peaceable , gentle , open to reason , full of mercy and good fruits , impartial and sincere .

groups.context_description: Term names the pitiable inner condition — the state of the person who is spiritually destitute, whether through false confidence or loss of hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 3:17 For you say , “I am rich , I have prospered , and I need nothing ,” not realizing that you are wretched , pitiable , poor , blind , and naked .

groups.context_description: Term names almsgiving as the outward expression of an inward disposition of mercy and generosity — the inner orientation of compassion made visible in action

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 6:2 “ Thus , when you give to the needy , sound no trumpet before you , as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets , that they may be praised by others . Truly , I say to you , they have received their reward .
Luk 11:41 But give as alms those things that are within , and behold , everything is clean for you .

Rom 7:17 So now it is no longer I who do it , but sin that dwells within me .

groups.context_description: Term names sin as the universal human condition and objective state of all persons apart from Christ — the condition that defines, condemns, and is overcome only by the righteousness of God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 16:8 And when he comes , he will convict the world concerning sin and righteousness and judgment :
Rom 3:9 What then ? Are we Jews any better off? No , not at all . For we have already charged that all, both Jews and Greeks , are under sin ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner quality of soundness and wholeness of orientation — the undivided inner focus that allows the whole person to be full of light

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 6:22 “The eye is the lamp of the body . So , if your eye is healthy , your whole body will be full of light ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of unreserved giving — generosity without holding back or finding fault; the quality of openhanded-ness as inner character

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 1:5 If any of you lacks wisdom , let him ask God , who gives generously to all without reproach , and it will be given him .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner quality of singleness and sincerity — the undivided, transparent orientation of the heart toward God and others; the opposite of duplicity or divided allegiance

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:3 But I am afraid that as the serpent deceived Eve by his cunning , your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of generosity — the openhanded orientation of the person that overflows in giving; giving as the expression of inner singleness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 12:8 the one who exhorts , in his exhortation ; the one who contributes , in generosity ; the one who leads , with zeal ; the one who does acts of mercy , with cheerfulness .

groups.context_description: Dream-visions and inner fancies — the mental images and fantasies produced in the mind during sleep, which alarm and disturb the inner being

groups.context_description: Term names the commanded inner silence — the hush of reverence, awe, or holy restraint before God or in the face of his coming

Zep 1:7 Be silent before the Lord God ! For the day of the Lord is near ; the Lord has prepared a sacrifice and consecrated his guests .

groups.context_description: H1934 predicates an inner character quality or spiritual state as present within a person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 6:3 Then this Daniel became distinguished above all the other high officials and satraps , because an excellent spirit was in him. And the king planned to set him over the whole kingdom .

groups.context_description: H1934 as the verbal form carrying an act of inner contemplation or reflective observation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 7:8 I considered the horns , and behold , there came up among them another horn , a little one, before which three of the first horns were plucked up by the roots . And behold , in this horn were eyes like the eyes of a man , and a mouth speaking great things .

Mic 7:3 Their hands are on what is evil , to do it well; the prince and the judge ask for a bribe , and the great man utters the evil desire of his soul; thus they weave it together.

groups.context_description: Cha.dah as inner rejoicing in Gods saving acts and presence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 21:6 For you make him most blessed forever ; you make him glad with the joy of your presence .

groups.context_description: Term names the heart being glad or merry — the inner state of lightness and well-being expressed in festive eating, drinking, and communal celebration

Pro 17:22 A joyful heart is good medicine , but a crushed spirit dries up the bones .

groups.context_description: Ya.tav as moral goodness and doing good: inner moral orientation

groups.context_description: Term names the inner state of being pleased or finding something good — the approval, satisfaction, or favour that the inner person extends toward a person, course of action, or situation

Est 2:9 And the young woman pleased him and won his favor . And he quickly provided her with her cosmetics and her portion of food , and with seven chosen young women from the king’s palace , and advanced her and her young women to the best place in the harem .

groups.context_description: Te.ev as exceedingly glad: inner gladness at deliverance through trust in God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 6:23 Then the king was exceedingly glad , and commanded that Daniel be taken up out of the den . So Daniel was taken up out of the den , and no kind of harm was found on him, because he had trusted in his God .

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 16:10 And joy and gladness are taken away from the fruitful field , and in the vineyards no songs are sung , no cheers are raised ; no treader treads out wine in the presses ; I have put an end to the shouting .

Jer 25:30 “ You , therefore, shall prophesy against them all these words , and say to them: “‘The Lord will roar from on high , and from his holy habitation utter his voice ; he will roar mightily against his fold , and shout , like those who tread grapes, against all the inhabitants of the earth .

groups.context_description: Term names praise as the defining inner orientation of the person before God — the mouth and heart continually directed toward declaring God's worth

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 22:3 Yet you are holy , enthroned on the praises of Israel .
Of David , when he changed his behavior before Abimelech , so that he drove him out , and he went away . Psa 34:1 I will bless the Lord at all times ; his praise shall continually be in my mouth .

groups.context_description: Term names God himself as the content and source of praise — what the inner life is oriented toward and what enables praise to rise

Isa 43:21 the people whom I formed for myself that they might declare my praise .

Isa 61:3 to grant to those who mourn in Zion — to give them a beautiful headdress instead of ashes , the oil of gladness instead of mourning , the garment of praise instead of a faint spirit ; that they may be called oaks of righteousness , the planting of the Lord , that he may be glorified .

groups.context_description: Term names the shining of divine light upon the person — God's illuminating presence as the condition of the inner life's flourishing and orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 29:3 when his lamp shone upon my head , and by his light I walked through darkness ,

groups.context_description: Term names praise-celebration as an inner-being act of devotion — whether rightly directed toward God or misdirected toward false gods

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 146:1 Praise the Lord ! Praise the Lord , O my soul !

Whom have I oppressed ? Or from whose hand have I taken a bribe to blind my eyes with it? Testify against me and I will restore it to you .”
Isa 6:8 And I heard the voice of the Lord saying , “ Whom shall I send , and who will go for us?” Then I said , “ Here I am! Send me .”

Pro 5:9 lest you give your honor to others and your years to the merciless ,

Lam 5:15 The joy of our hearts has ceased ; our dancing has been turned to mourning .

groups.context_description: Term qualifies, grounds, and intensifies inner-being orientations — the particle marks how inner states are to be reckoned, how they arise from theological realities, and how they shape conduct; including self-examination, faith, conscience, apostasy, love, forgiveness, submission, ambition, and the reckoning of death to sin and life to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 2:12 saying , “I will tell of your name to my brothers ; in the midst of the congregation I will sing your praise .”

groups.context_description: Term names obedience as the inner disposition of faith — the volitional compliance of the whole person with God's word, resulting in righteousness and purified souls

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 10:5 We destroy arguments and every lofty opinion raised against the knowledge of God , and take every thought captive to obey Christ ,
1Pe 1:22 Having purified your souls by your obedience to the truth for a sincere brotherly love, love one another earnestly from a pure heart ,

groups.context_description: Term names Christ's obedience as the ground of righteousness for many — the inner-being act of the Son that reverses Adam's disobedience

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 5:19 For as by the one man’s disobedience the many were made sinners , so by the one man’s obedience the many will be made righteous .

groups.context_description: Term names pride as an inner-being condition arising from the heart — one of the evils that proceed from within the person and defile them

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 7:22 coveting , wickedness , deceit , sensuality , envy , slander , pride , foolishness .

groups.context_description: Term names the arrogant person — whose inner-being orientation of pride draws divine opposition and whose heart devises proud thoughts

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 1:51 He has shown strength with his arm ; he has scattered the proud in the thoughts of their hearts ;
Jam 4:6 But he gives more grace . Therefore it says , “ God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble .”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of thinking too highly of oneself — self-inflated assessment contrasted with sober self-knowledge

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 12:3 For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think , but to think with sober judgment , each according to the measure of faith that God has assigned .

groups.context_description: Term names persons of high authority or eminence — the inner being engaged through prayer for, humility before, or renunciation of lofty rhetorical power

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 2:2 for kings and all who are in high positions , that we may lead a peaceful and quiet life , godly and dignified in every way.

groups.context_description: Term names steadfast endurance under trial — the inner disposition that holds firm under pressure, suffering, or persecution

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 12:2 looking to Jesus , the founder and perfecter of our faith , who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross , despising the shame , and is seated at the right hand of the throne of God .
Jam 1:12 Blessed is the man who remains steadfast under trial , for when he has stood the test he will receive the crown of life , which God has promised to those who love him .

groups.context_description: Term names perseverance as the sustained inner disposition of steadfast endurance under trial — producing character, hope, and ultimately eternal life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 5:3 Not only that , but we rejoice in our sufferings , knowing that suffering produces endurance ,
Rev 14:12 Here is a call for the endurance of the saints , those who keep the commandments of God and their faith in Jesus .

groups.context_description: Term names the hypocrite as a person whose inner life is defined by disconnection between outward performance and inward reality — performing religious acts to be seen rather than from inner transformation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 23:27 “ Woe to you , scribes and Pharisees , hypocrites ! For you are like whitewashed tombs , which outwardly appear beautiful , but within are full of dead people’s bones and all uncleanness .

groups.context_description: Term names the hypocrite as one who reads external signs but fails to discern the inner realities of God's action in history

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 12:56 You hypocrites ! You know how to interpret the appearance of earth and sky , but why do you not know how to interpret the present time ?

groups.context_description: Term names genuineness as an inner quality — the absence of outward performance disconnected from inner reality; love, faith, and wisdom being truly what they appear

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 1:5 The aim of our charge is love that issues from a pure heart and a good conscience and a sincere faith .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of joining in hypocrisy — the communal spread of a pretence that disconnects outward behaviour from inner reality

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gal 2:13 And the rest of the Jews acted hypocritically along with him , so that even Barnabas was led astray by their hypocrisy .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of pretending — the deliberate inner intention to perform a quality one does not possess, with a hidden contrary purpose

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 20:20 So they watched him and sent spies , who pretended to be sincere , that they might catch him in something he said , so as to deliver him up to the authority and jurisdiction of the governor .

groups.context_description: Term names hypocrisy as an inner condition — the state of inner pretence in which outward performance is disconnected from actual inner reality, particularly in relation to God and others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 23:28 So you also outwardly appear righteous to others , but within you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness .

groups.context_description: Term names submission as an inner-relational orientation — the disposition of yielding to God, the gospel, or legitimate authority, arising from inner conviction and expressed in conduct

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 9:13 By their approval of this service , they will glorify God because of your submission that comes from your confession of the gospel of Christ , and the generosity of your contribution for them and for all others,

groups.context_description: Term names voluntary submission as the fundamental inner-relational orientation of the person before God and within human community — placing oneself under God's authority, arising from conscience, reverence, and faith

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 12:9 Besides this , we have had earthly fathers who disciplined us and we respected them. Shall we not much more be subject to the Father of spirits and live ?
Jam 4:7 Submit yourselves therefore to God . Resist the devil , and he will flee from you .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of arrogance — the haughtiness of the wealthy person who sets themselves above others and above God

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 6:17 As for the rich in this present age , charge them not to be haughty , nor to set their hopes on the uncertainty of riches , but on God , who richly provides us with everything to enjoy .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of haughtiness — self-elevation and valuing what is exalted among people, warned against in favour of humility and fear

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 12:16 Live in harmony with one another . Do not be haughty , but associate with the lowly . Never be wise in your own sight.

groups.context_description: Pleasure as the competing inner orientation — desires that war within and displace the good

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 4:1 What causes quarrels and what causes fights among you ? Is it not this , that your passions are at war within you ?

groups.context_description: Gladly receiving — the inner posture of willing, pleased reception

groups.context_description: Extreme gladness — inner joy that delights in weakness and self-giving

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 12:15 I will most gladly spend and be spent for your souls . If I love you more , am I to be loved less ?

groups.context_description: Delight in the law of God in the inner being

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 7:22 For I delight in the law of God , in my inner being ,

groups.context_description: Inner estimation and revaluation — the act of counting, considering, and esteeming as inner cognitive and moral acts; including the radical revaluation of loss and gain, the counting of others as more significant, and the assessment of one's own condition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 2:3 Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit , but in humility count others more significant than yourselves .
Phili 3:7 But whatever gain I had , I counted as loss for the sake of Christ .

groups.context_description: Term names a disposition of quiet attentiveness or ordered restraint — the inner quality of settled quiet that enables learning, work, or worship

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 2:11 Let a woman learn quietly with all submissiveness .

groups.context_description: Term names a gentle, quiet spirit as an inner-being quality — the disposition of settled peace within, precious in God's sight

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of settling into quiet — the ceasing of noise, argument, or resistance, whether in reverence, acceptance, or godly aspiration

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 11:18 When they heard these things they fell silent . And they glorified God , saying , “ Then to the Gentiles also God has granted repentance that leads to life .”
1Th 4:11 and to aspire to live quietly , and to mind your own affairs, and to work with your hands , as we instructed you ,

groups.context_description: Term names just and unjust measurement as a moral category — the ephah as the standard by which inner integrity is expressed in commerce

anchor_verses.verse_text: Amo 8:5 saying , “ When will the new moon be over , that we may sell grain ? And the Sabbath , that we may offer wheat for sale, that we may make the ephah small and the shekel great and deal deceitfully with false balances ,
Zec 5:8 And he said , “ This is Wickedness .” And he thrust her back into the basket , and thrust down the leaden weight on its opening .

groups.context_description: Term directs inquiry toward inner-being capacities as its subject — framing the question of where wisdom, understanding, or divine help originates

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 28:12 “But where shall wisdom be found ? And where is the place of understanding ?

1Pe 4:11 whoever speaks , as one who speaks oracles of God ; whoever serves , as one who serves by the strength that God supplies —in order that in everything God may be glorified through Jesus Christ . To him belong glory and dominion forever and ever . Amen .

Luk 11:22 but when one stronger than he attacks him and overcomes him , he takes away his armor in which he trusted and divides his spoil .

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 11:34 quenched the power of fire , escaped the edge of the sword , were made strong out of weakness , became mighty in war , put foreign armies to flight .

1Jo 2:14 I write to you , fathers , because you know him who is from the beginning . I write to you , young men , because you are strong , and the word of God abides in you , and you have overcome the evil one .

groups.context_description: Katischuō as power that prevails over the church or through inner conviction

anchor_verses.verse_text: The helpless are crushed, sink down,and fall by his might.

groups.context_description: Term names the withering of inner vigour — the fading of inner strength or courage under pressure or judgment

Kaph Psa 119:81 My soul longs for your salvation ; I hope in your word .

Psa 139:22 I hate them with complete hatred ; I count them my enemies .

groups.context_description: Term names shame as an inward condition of dishonour and disgrace, covering the person before God and before the community

Lam 3:41 Let us lift up our hearts and hands to God in heaven :

groups.context_description: Term names the hands as the moral expression of the inner life — clean or defiled hands as the outer form of inner integrity, guilt, or wickedness

Psa 24:4 He who has clean hands and a pure heart , who does not lift up his soul to what is false and does not swear deceitfully .
