groups.context_description: lev names the seat of emotional/affective experience — the inner place where joy, grief, fear, desire, courage, longing, and all emotional states arise and are felt

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 14:10 The heart knows its own bitterness , and no stranger shares its joy .
Pro 15:13 A glad heart makes a cheerful face , but by sorrow of heart the spirit is crushed .

groups.context_description: lev names the centre of the will — the inner organ of volitional direction, commitment, willing, and resistance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 7:13 Still Pharaoh’s heart was hardened , and he would not listen to them, as the Lord had said .

Exo 35:22 So they came , both men and women . All who were of a willing heart brought brooches and earrings and signet rings and armlets , all sorts of gold objects , every man dedicating an offering of gold to the Lord .

groups.context_description: lev names the organ of God-ward orientation — the inner faculty through which a person prays, seeks God, turns toward or away from God, and is known and shaped by God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 27:8 You have said, “ Seek my face .” My heart says to you, “Your face , Lord , do I seek .”
Isa 29:13 And the Lord said : “ Because this people draw near with their mouth and honor me with their lips , while their hearts are far from me, and their fear of me is a commandment taught by men ,

groups.context_description: lev names the locus of conscience — the inner moral witness that reacts to one's own conduct, accuses or vindicates, and knows the self before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 24:5 And afterward David’s heart struck him, because he had cut off a corner of Saul’s robe.

2Sa 24:10 But David’s heart struck him after he had numbered the people . And David said to the Lord , “I have sinned greatly in what I have done . But now , O Lord , please take away the iniquity of your servant , for I have done very foolishly .”

groups.context_description: lev names the organ of relational address and concern — the heart as the subject and object of intimate relational speech, disclosure, and regard

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 16:17 And he told her all his heart , and said to her, “A razor has never come upon my head , for I have been a Nazirite to God from my mother’s womb . If my head is shaved , then my strength will leave me, and I shall become weak and be like any other man .”
Hos 2:14 “ Therefore , behold , I will allure her, and bring her into the wilderness , and speak tenderly to her.

groups.context_description: Term names the heart as the essential inner person — the Aramaic heart designating the seat of thought and moral life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 7:28 “ Here is the end of the matter . As for me , Daniel , my thoughts greatly alarmed me, and my color changed , but I kept the matter in my heart .”

groups.context_description: Term names the directing of the inner mind toward a purpose — the act of setting one's mental attention and will upon an object of concern

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 6:14 Then the king , when he heard these words , was much distressed and set his mind to deliver Daniel . And he labored till the sun went down to rescue him .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of captivating or encouraging the heart — the inner person stirred through intimate encounter

anchor_verses.verse_text: So Amnon lay down and pretended to be ill. And when the king came to see him, Amnon said to the king, “Please let my sister Tamar come and make a couple of cakes in my sight, that I may eat from her hand.”

groups.context_description: Term names the negation of worship and allegiance — the inner refusal to serve foreign gods; exclusive inner devotion to God expressed through the sustained refusal to bow, serve, or comply

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 3:18 But if not , be it known to you, O king , that we will not serve your gods or worship the golden image that you have set up .”

groups.context_description: Term names the negation that attests inner moral integrity — the person in whom no fault, error, or dishonour can be found; blamelessness as the character of the inner life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 6:4 Then the high officials and the satraps sought to find a ground for complaint against Daniel with regard to the kingdom , but they could find no ground for complaint or any fault , because he was faithful , and no error or fault was found in him .

groups.context_description: Term names the negation of inner humility and honour toward God — the heart that has not humbled itself; pride as an inner orientation asserted through the negation of what was owed to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 5:22 And you his son , Belshazzar , have not humbled your heart , though you knew all this ,

groups.context_description: Term names the negation of self before God — inner humility expressed by denying personal merit, wisdom, or standing; creaturely nothingness before divine sovereignty; spiritual capacity and trust vindicated through divine action

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 2:30 But as for me , this mystery has been revealed to me, not because of any wisdom that I have more than all the living , but in order that the interpretation may be made known to the king , and that you may know the thoughts of your mind .

groups.context_description: Term names the negation of inner rest and composure — inner disturbance and distress disclosed through the absence of sleep, diversion, or peace

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 6:18 Then the king went to his palace and spent the night fasting ; no diversions were brought to him, and sleep fled from him .

groups.context_description: Term names the covenantal negation of belonging — the condition of estrangement in which identity as God's people is withdrawn; the inner-being experience of being declared not-mine

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 1:9 And the Lord said , “ Call his name Not My People , for you are not my people , and I am not your God.”

groups.context_description: Term names the negation of belief or trust — the refusal or failure of the inner act of faith toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 14:11 And the Lord said to Moses , “ How long will this people despise me? And how long will they not believe in me, in spite of all the signs that I have done among them?

groups.context_description: Term names the inner state of anguish, despair, and self-loathing — Job's negations of inner peace, rest, confidence, and desire to live

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 7:11 “ Therefore I will not restrain my mouth ; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit ; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul .

groups.context_description: Term names God's negated inner states — his inner dispositions withheld, withdrawn, or denied: love, delight, mercy, anger restrained

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 11:9 I will not execute my burning anger ; I will not again destroy Ephraim ; for I am God and not a man , the Holy One in your midst , and I will not come in wrath .

groups.context_description: Term names spiritual blindness or unresponsiveness — the inner failure to know, perceive, return to, or cry from the heart to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 5:4 Their deeds do not permit them to return to their God . For the spirit of whoredom is within them, and they know not the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names inner moral integrity affirmed through negation — the person who does not sin, does not fear, acts from the fear of God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 1:22 In all this Job did not sin or charge God with wrong .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner grief or distress disclosed through negation — the person who conceals or expresses inner sadness, relational rupture, or covenantal estrangement

anchor_verses.verse_text: Neh 2:3 I said to the king , “Let the king live forever ! Why should not my face be sad , when the city , the place of my fathers ’ graves , lies in ruins , and its gates have been destroyed by fire ?”

groups.context_description: Term names the prophetic withdrawal of divine compassion — the name embodies God's inner-being relational stance of withheld mercy toward a covenant-breaking people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 1:6 She conceived again and bore a daughter . And the Lord said to him, “ Call her name No Mercy , for I will no more have mercy on the house of Israel , to forgive them at all .

groups.context_description: The word as inner-dwelling and conscience-addressing — the word of Christ taking up residence within, addressing the conscience, or expressing/revealing the inner being of the person

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 4:2 But we have renounced disgraceful , underhanded ways. We refuse to practice cunning or to tamper with God’s word , but by the open statement of the truth we would commend ourselves to everyone’s conscience in the sight of God .
Col 3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly , teaching and admonishing one another in all wisdom , singing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs , with thankfulness in your hearts to God .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of reasoning or questioning — the mind deliberating with itself or the heart raising doubts and questions

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 2:8 And immediately Jesus , perceiving in his spirit that they thus questioned within themselves , said to them , “ Why do you question these things in your hearts ?

groups.context_description: Term names deliberation and discussion among persons — reasoning together about a matter

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 21:25 The baptism of John , from where did it come ? From heaven or from man ?” And they discussed it among themselves , saying , “ If we say , ‘ From heaven ,’ he will say to us , ‘ Why then did you not believe him ?’

groups.context_description: Inner thoughts as site of accusation and spiritual warfare — conflicting inner thoughts that accuse or excuse the conscience; and the capturing of every thought to the obedience of Christ

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 2:15 They show that the work of the law is written on their hearts , while their conscience also bears witness , and their conflicting thoughts accuse or even excuse them
2Cor 10:5 We destroy arguments and every lofty opinion raised against the knowledge of God , and take every thought captive to obey Christ ,

groups.context_description: Inner reasoning and reckoning — the cognitive inner act of considering, reasoning, concluding, and the reckoning of faith, covering both practical inner determinations and the deep inner work of theological conviction

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 6:11 So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus .
1Cor 13:5 or rude . It does not insist on its own way ; it is not irritable or resentful ;

Phili 4:8 Finally , brothers , whatever is true , whatever is honorable , whatever is just , whatever is pure , whatever is lovely , whatever is commendable , if there is any excellence , if there is anything worthy of praise , think about these things .

groups.context_description: Term qualifies worship as spiritual or rational — arising from the inner person rather than being merely external ritual

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 12:1 I appeal to you therefore , brothers , by the mercies of God , to present your bodies as a living sacrifice , holy and acceptable to God , which is your spiritual worship .

groups.context_description: Term names reviling as an act of verbal abuse — the inner-relational act of attacking another with contemptuous speech, and the tested inner response of non-retaliation

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Pe 3:9 Do not repay evil for evil or reviling for reviling , but on the contrary , bless , for to this you were called , that you may obtain a blessing .

groups.context_description: Inner condition of relief from anxiety — reduction of grief-laden concern through wellbeing of a beloved person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 2:28 I am the more eager to send him , therefore , that you may rejoice at seeing him again , and that I may be less anxious .

groups.context_description: Grief and sorrow as spontaneous inner affective response

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 21:17 He said to him the third time, “ Simon , son of John , do you love me ?” Peter was grieved because he said to him the third time, “Do you love me ?” and he said to him , “ Lord , you know everything ; you know that I love you .” Jesus said to him , “ Feed my sheep .

groups.context_description: Grief caused by another — interpersonal pain

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 2:4 For I wrote to you out of much affliction and anguish of heart and with many tears , not to cause you pain but to let you know the abundant love that I have for you .

groups.context_description: Godly grief producing inner transformation and repentance

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 7:9 As it is , I rejoice , not because you were grieved , but because you were grieved into repenting . For you felt a godly grief , so that you suffered no loss through us .

groups.context_description: Grief and sorrow as spontaneous inner affective response

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 21:17 He said to him the third time, “ Simon , son of John , do you love me ?” Peter was grieved because he said to him the third time, “Do you love me ?” and he said to him , “ Lord , you know everything ; you know that I love you .” Jesus said to him , “ Feed my sheep .

groups.context_description: Grief caused by another — interpersonal pain

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 2:4 For I wrote to you out of much affliction and anguish of heart and with many tears , not to cause you pain but to let you know the abundant love that I have for you .

groups.context_description: Godly grief producing inner transformation and repentance

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 7:9 As it is , I rejoice , not because you were grieved , but because you were grieved into repenting . For you felt a godly grief , so that you suffered no loss through us .

groups.context_description: Term names an overwhelming inner condition of sorrow — grief that penetrates and consumes the whole person, to the point of death

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 26:38 Then he said to them , “ My soul is very sorrowful , even to death ; remain here , and watch with me .”

groups.context_description: Term names the experience of being grieved — an inner state of sorrow arising from the moral-spiritual failure of others, particularly their hardness of heart

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 3:5 And he looked around at them with anger , grieved at their hardness of heart , and said to the man , “ Stretch out your hand .” He stretched it out , and his hand was restored .

groups.context_description: Term names the devious inner character — the crooked orientation of the person whose ways depart from righteousness, expressing contempt for God or wisdom

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 3:32 for the devious person is an abomination to the Lord , but the upright are in his confidence .
Pro 14:2 Whoever walks in uprightness fears the Lord , but he who is devious in his ways despises him .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of turning away from wisdom — the inner movement of deviating from sound judgement and heart-orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 4:21 Let them not escape from your sight ; keep them within your heart .

groups.context_description: Term names perversity of speech as an expression of inner moral deviation — the crooked orientation from which crooked talk flows

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 4:24 Put away from you crooked speech , and put devious talk far from you .

groups.context_description: Term names the interrogative "what" as a window into divine knowledge and sovereignty — what God knows in darkness, what he reveals, and what no one can question

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 2:22 he reveals deep and hidden things ; he knows what is in the darkness , and the light dwells with him .

Dan 4:35 all the inhabitants of the earth are accounted as nothing , and he does according to his will among the host of heaven and among the inhabitants of the earth ; and none can stay his hand or say to him, “ What have you done ?”

groups.context_description: Term names the interrogative 'who' as a challenge to or affirmation of divine sovereignty — who can refuse God's commands, who receives authority as God wills

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 3:15 Now if you are ready when you hear the sound of the horn , pipe , lyre , trigon , harp , bagpipe , and every kind of music , to fall down and worship the image that I have made , well and good. But if you do not worship , you shall immediately be cast into a burning fiery furnace . And who is the god who will deliver you out of my hands ?”

Dan 4:17 The sentence is by the decree of the watchers , the decision by the word of the holy ones , to the end that the living may know that the Most High rules the kingdom of men and gives it to whom he will and sets over it the lowliest of men .’

groups.context_description: Term describes the inner dissolution of heart and spirit under overwhelming fear, dread, or threatening circumstance — the collapse of resolve and courage before a superior force or divine presence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jos 2:11 And as soon as we heard it, our hearts melted , and there was no spirit left in any man because of you, for the Lord your God , he is God in the heavens above and on the earth beneath .

Eze 21:7 And when they say to you, ‘ Why do you groan ?’ you shall say , ‘Because of the news that it is coming . Every heart will melt , and all hands will be feeble ; every spirit will faint , and all knees will be weak as water . Behold , it is coming , and it will be fulfilled ,’” declares the Lord God .

groups.context_description: Term describes the pouring out of the innermost self — the soul poured to death, the Spirit poured upon the people — the act of ultimate inner giving

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:12 Therefore I will divide him a portion with the many , and he shall divide the spoil with the strong , because he poured out his soul to death and was numbered with the transgressors ; yet he bore the sin of many , and makes intercession for the transgressors .

groups.context_description: Term describes the divine act of uncovering and exposing the person in judgment, bringing shame and humiliation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 3:17 therefore the Lord will strike with a scab the heads of the daughters of Zion , and the Lord will lay bare their secret parts .

groups.context_description: Term names the violation of intimate covering — the uncovering of sexual boundaries that carries moral consequence and iniquity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 20:18 If a man lies with a woman during her menstrual period and uncovers her nakedness , he has made naked her fountain , and she has uncovered the fountain of her blood . Both of them shall be cut off from among their people .

groups.context_description: Term expresses the person's inner vulnerability and the prayer not to be left defenseless before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 141:8 But my eyes are toward you, O God , my Lord ; in you I seek refuge ; leave me not defenseless !

groups.context_description: Term names the intimate dignity of the person — the nakedness that the law protects from sexual violation and that defines moral and relational boundaries

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 18:6 “ None of you shall approach any one of his close relatives to uncover nakedness . I am the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names nakedness as the person's vulnerability that God covers in covenantal love — the act of covering as the foundation of belonging

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 16:8 “When I passed by you again and saw you, behold , you were at the age for love , and I spread the corner of my garment over you and covered your nakedness ; I made my vow to you and entered into a covenant with you, declares the Lord God , and you became mine .

groups.context_description: Term names nakedness as the shame and exposure that follows spiritual unfaithfulness — judgment as uncovering

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 47:3 Your nakedness shall be uncovered , and your disgrace shall be seen . I will take vengeance , and I will spare no one .

groups.context_description: Term names the covering required for dignity in the divine presence — nakedness that must not be exposed before the holy God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 20:26 And you shall not go up by steps to my altar , that your nakedness be not exposed on it .’

groups.context_description: Term names the razor as the instrument of divine shaming — used to strip the honour and dignity of the person in judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 7:20 In that day the Lord will shave with a razor that is hired beyond the River —with the king of Assyria —the head and the hair of the feet , and it will sweep away the beard also.

groups.context_description: Term names the razor as what the consecrated person avoids — its avoidance marking the person's inner dedication to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 6:5 “ All the days of his vow of separation , no razor shall touch his head . Until the time is completed for which he separates himself to the Lord , he shall be holy . He shall let the locks of hair of his head grow long .

groups.context_description: Term frames a comparison that characterises the inner disposition of deceit — the tongue like a razor, plotting destruction

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 52:2 Your tongue plots destruction , like a sharp razor , you worker of deceit .

groups.context_description: Term names the originary nakedness and bareness of the person — utter vulnerability before God's care intervenes

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 16:7 I made you flourish like a plant of the field . And you grew up and became tall and arrived at full adornment . Your breasts were formed , and your hair had grown ; yet you were naked and bare .

groups.context_description: Term names nakedness as the shame and exposure that judgment brings upon spiritual unfaithfulness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mic 1:11 Pass on your way , inhabitants of Shaphir , in nakedness and shame ; the inhabitants of Zaanan do not come out ; the lamentation of Beth-ezel shall take away from you its standing place.

groups.context_description: Term names nakedness exposed in divine judgment — the public shame and disgrace of the person laid bare before others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Nah 3:5 Behold , I am against you, declares the Lord of hosts , and will lift up your skirts over your face ; and I will make nations look at your nakedness and kingdoms at your shame .

groups.context_description: Term names blessedness as the inner state of the person who receives God's freely given righteousness — and the loss of that blessedness when the inner person turns back to works

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 4:6 just as David also speaks of the blessing of the one to whom God counts righteousness apart from works :
Gal 4:15 What then has become of your blessedness ? For I testify to you that , if possible , you would have gouged out your eyes and given them to me .

groups.context_description: Term names patient forbearance as an inner disposition — the willingness to wait, endure, or bear with others without giving way to frustration

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 13:4 Love is patient and kind ; love does not envy or boast ; it is not arrogant

Jam 5:7 Be patient , therefore , brothers , until the coming of the Lord . See how the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth , being patient about it , until it receives the early and the late rains .

groups.context_description: Term names divine patience — God's inner disposition of forbearance toward human disobedience

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Pe 3:9 The Lord is not slow to fulfill his promise as some count slowness , but is patient toward you , not wishing that any should perish , but that all should reach repentance .

groups.context_description: Term names longsuffering/patience as an inner character quality and fruit of the Spirit — the sustained inner disposition of forbearance toward others and circumstances

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gal 5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love , joy , peace , patience , kindness , goodness , faithfulness ,
Col 3:12 Put on then , as God’s chosen ones , holy and beloved , compassionate hearts , kindness , humility , meekness , and patience ,

groups.context_description: Term names divine longsuffering — God's patient endurance of human sin, restraining judgment to extend the opportunity for repentance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 9:22 What if God , desiring to show his wrath and to make known his power , has endured with much patience vessels of wrath prepared for destruction ,
1Ti 1:16 But I received mercy for this reason , that in me , as the foremost , Jesus Christ might display his perfect patience as an example to those who were to believe in him for eternal life .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner posture of patient attentiveness — the disposition of measured, unhurried receptiveness toward another's speech

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 26:3 especially because you are familiar with all the customs and controversies of the Jews . Therefore I beg you to listen to me patiently .

groups.context_description: Term names the queen as one whose inner will, courage, and intercession move history — inner resistance, love for her people, and courageous self-offering

anchor_verses.verse_text: Est 7:3 Then Queen Esther answered , “ If I have found favor in your sight , O king , and if it please the king , let my life be granted me for my wish , and my people for my request .

groups.context_description: Term names the queen as the one whose incomparable worth evokes praise — even rivals are moved to honour what they recognise

anchor_verses.verse_text: Song 6:9 My dove , my perfect one , is the only one , the only one of her mother , pure to her who bore her. The young women saw her and called her blessed ; the queens and concubines also, and they praised her .

groups.context_description: Term names the kingdom as what is given or withdrawn in response to the moral character of its holder

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 15:28 And Samuel said to him, “The Lord has torn the kingdom of Israel from you this day and has given it to a neighbor of yours, who is better than you .

groups.context_description: Term names the queen as one who seeks wisdom with deep inner desire and receptivity — coming with questions, disclosing her heart, and responding with generous wonder

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 10:1 Now when the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon concerning the name of the Lord , she came to test him with hard questions .

2Ch 9:1 Now when the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon , she came to Jerusalem to test him with hard questions , having a very great retinue and camels bearing spices and very much gold and precious stones . And when she came to Solomon , she told him all that was on her mind .

groups.context_description: Term names manna as the instrument of God's inner-forming provision — humbling, testing, and teaching dependence on every word of God rather than material bread

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 8:3 And he humbled you and let you hunger and fed you with manna , which you did not know , nor did your fathers know , that he might make you know that man does not live by bread alone , but man lives by every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord .
Psa 78:24 and he rained down on them manna to eat and gave them the grain of heaven .

groups.context_description: Term names the bitterness of soul in deep distress — the inner taste-quality of grief, anguish, and sorrow experienced inwardly

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 1:10 She was deeply distressed and prayed to the Lord and wept bitterly .

groups.context_description: Term names the bitter intensity of anger — the fierce, biting quality of wrath in action and in character

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 17:8 Hushai said , “ You know that your father and his men are mighty men , and that they are enraged , like a bear robbed of her cubs in the field . Besides, your father is expert in war ; he will not spend the night with the people .

groups.context_description: Term names the bitterness of the human condition — the taste of life as toil, futility, and moral poison under the sun

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:26 And I find something more bitter than death : the woman whose heart is snares and nets , and whose hands are fetters . He who pleases God escapes her, but the sinner is taken by her .

groups.context_description: Term names the bitterness of inner experience caused by another — the grief inflicted on the inner being through relational or domestic conflict

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 26:35 and they made life bitter for Isaac and Rebekah .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-being experience of bitterness — the grief that makes life bitter, provoked by loss and divine action, and that the person names and brings before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rut 1:20 She said to them, “Do not call me Naomi ; call me Mara , for the Almighty has dealt very bitterly with me.
Job 27:2 “As God lives , who has taken away my right , and the Almighty , who has made my soul bitter ,

groups.context_description: Term names bitter fury as an inner-being state — the rage-bitterness that drives violent action, the inner temperature of a person consumed by enraged opposition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 8:7 I saw him come close to the ram , and he was enraged against him and struck the ram and broke his two horns . And the ram had no power to stand before him, but he cast him down to the ground and trampled on him. And there was no one who could rescue the ram from his power .

groups.context_description: Term names God's transcendent position on high — from which he acts, rescues, and pours out his Spirit on the lowly; the height of God as the origin of his inner-being engagement with the world

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 32:15 until the Spirit is poured upon us from on high , and the wilderness becomes a fruitful field , and the fruitful field is deemed a forest .

Isa 57:15 For thus says the One who is high and lifted up, who inhabits eternity , whose name is Holy : “I dwell in the high and holy place, and also with him who is of a contrite and lowly spirit , to revive the spirit of the lowly , and to revive the heart of the contrite .

groups.context_description: Term names human pride expressed as height — the arrogant inner orientation that reaches upward against God or others, and is brought low

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 26:5 For he has humbled the inhabitants of the height , the lofty city . He lays it low , lays it low to the ground , casts it to the dust .
Isa 37:23 “‘ Whom have you mocked and reviled ? Against whom have you raised your voice and lifted your eyes to the heights ? Against the Holy One of Israel !

groups.context_description: Term names dominion as the sovereign scope of God's reign or the authority entrusted to persons — the inner being engaged through worship, obedience, or the hope of restoration

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 145:13 Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom , and your dominion endures throughout all generations . [The Lord is faithful in all his words and kind in all his works . ]

Isa 22:21 and I will clothe him with your robe , and will bind your sash on him, and will commit your authority to his hand . And he shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem and to the house of Judah .

groups.context_description: Term names dominion as a capacity to rule — the inner being engaged through the character, scope, and will of those who exercise it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 11:3 Then a mighty king shall arise , who shall rule with great dominion and do as he wills .

groups.context_description: Term names messianic dominion extending to universal peace — the inner being engaged through eschatological hope

groups.context_description: Term names the act of using a proverb or parable — the inner being engaged through the wisdom tradition's address to the human heart and the shaping of communal self-understanding

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 12:23 Tell them therefore , ‘ Thus says the Lord God : I will put an end to this proverb , and they shall no more use it as a proverb in Israel .’ But say to them, The days are near , and the fulfillment of every vision .
Eze 18:2 “ What do you mean by repeating this proverb concerning the land of Israel , ‘The fathers have eaten sour grapes , and the children’s teeth are set on edge ’?

groups.context_description: Term names the act of comparison or likening — the inner being engaged through self-recognition, theological discernment, or the declaration of incomparability

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 49:12 Man in his pomp will not remain ; he is like the beasts that perish .

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 9:25 Know therefore and understand that from the going out of the word to restore and build Jerusalem to the coming of an anointed one , a prince , there shall be seven weeks . And for sixty-two weeks it shall be built again with squares and moat , but in a troubled time .

groups.context_description: Term names anointing in anticipation of death — the act of love that prepares the body of Christ for burial, performed in advance through devotion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 14:8 She has done what she could ; she has anointed my body beforehand for burial .

groups.context_description: Death as consequence of inner moral failure — dying as outcome of folly, wickedness, sin

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat , for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die .”
Job 5:2 Surely vexation kills the fool , and jealousy slays the simple .

groups.context_description: Death as object of inner-being orientation — fear, longing, resolve, wisdom on mortality

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 21:25 Another dies in bitterness of soul , never having tasted of prosperity .

Jon 4:8 When the sun rose , God appointed a scorching east wind , and the sun beat down on the head of Jonah so that he was faint . And he asked that he might die and said , “It is better for me to die than to live .”

groups.context_description: God's sovereignty over death as ground of inner-being trust and confidence

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 2:6 The Lord kills and brings to life ; he brings down to Sheol and raises up .
Hab 1:12 Are you not from everlasting , O Lord my God , my Holy One? We shall not die . O Lord , you have ordained them as a judgment , and you, O Rock , have established them for reproof .

groups.context_description: The dead in relation to God — cut off from his hand; loss of praise capacity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 88:5 like one set loose among the dead , like the slain that lie in the grave , like those whom you remember no more , for they are cut off from your hand .
Psa 88:10 Do you work wonders for the dead ? Do the departed rise up to praise you? Selah

groups.context_description: Death as end/consequence of a moral path — wickedness leads to death; choosing life vs death

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 30:19 I call heaven and earth to witness against you today , that I have set before you life and death , blessing and curse . Therefore choose life , that you and your offspring may live ,
Pro 8:36 but he who fails to find me injures himself ; all who hate me love death .”

groups.context_description: Death as power/threat/reality the person faces, fears, chooses, or is delivered from

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 20:3 But David vowed again , saying , “Your father knows well that I have found favor in your eyes , and he thinks , ‘Do not let Jonathan know this , lest he be grieved .’ But truly , as the Lord lives and as your soul lives , there is but a step between me and death .”
Psa 55:4 My heart is in anguish within me; the terrors of death have fallen upon me .

groups.context_description: God's sovereignty over death as ground of redemption and inner-being hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 25:8 He will swallow up death forever ; and the Lord God will wipe away tears from all faces , and the reproach of his people he will take away from all the earth , for the Lord has spoken .
Hos 13:14 I shall ransom them from the power of Sheol ; I shall redeem them from Death . O Death , where are your plagues ? O Sheol , where is your sting ? Compassion is hidden from my eyes .

groups.context_description: The dead and inner-being capacities — those who no longer praise, remember, or are remembered

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 6:5 For in death there is no remembrance of you; in Sheol who will give you praise ?
Psa 116:15 Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his saints .

groups.context_description: "By no means!" — strong inner-being moral assertion against misapplication of grace

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 6:2 By no means ! How can we who died to sin still live in it ?

groups.context_description: "Do not say in your heart" and other inner-being directives — mē shaping the inner life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 10:6 But the righteousness based on faith says , “Do not say in your heart , ‘ Who will ascend into heaven ?’” (that is , to bring Christ down )

groups.context_description: Inner-being absence or incapacity named through negation — no fear of God, cannot see, did not seek

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 11:8 as it is written , “ God gave them a spirit of stupor , eyes that would not see and ears that would not hear , down to this very day .”

groups.context_description: Term as the particle of inner-being warning — it marks the feared spiritual failure that vigilance must prevent: drifting, an unbelieving heart, falling short of rest

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 3:12 Take care , brothers , lest there be in any of you an evil , unbelieving heart , leading you to fall away from the living God .

groups.context_description: Term describes the inner transformation into folly — the process by which a person or their thinking becomes foolish before God through suppression of truth or divine action

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:22 Claiming to be wise , they became fools ,

groups.context_description: Term describes the loss of distinctive inner character — the becoming flat or flavourless through failure to maintain one's orientation as God's people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 5:13 “ You are the salt of the earth , but if salt has lost its taste , how shall its saltiness be restored? It is no longer good for anything except to be thrown out and trampled under people’s feet.

groups.context_description: Term names the inner epistemic verdict of foolishness — how the word of the cross or spiritual truth is assessed by the unrenewed inner being, and how God assesses the wisdom of the world

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:14 The natural person does not accept the things of the Spirit of God , for they are folly to him , and he is not able to understand them because they are spiritually discerned .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of foolishness as unpreparedness and failure to think ahead — the disposition that hears but does not act, or acquires without foresight

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 25:3 For when the foolish took their lamps , they took no oil with them ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-epistemic reversal through which God chooses the world's foolish and calls the wise to embrace apparent folly — the paradox of wisdom in Christ

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:18 Let no one deceive himself . If anyone among you thinks that he is wise in this age , let him become a fool that he may become wise .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of moral/spiritual blindness or contemptuous judgment — the foolishness that fails to discern what is sacred, or that breeds controversy

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 23:17 You blind fools ! For which is greater , the gold or the temple that has made the gold sacred ?

groups.context_description: Term marks a plea addressed directly to God, giving voice to an inner state of need, urgency, fear, or penitence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 32:11 Please deliver me from the hand of my brother , from the hand of Esau , for I fear him, that he may come and attack me , the mothers with the children .
Num 12:13 And Moses cried to the Lord , “O God , please heal her — please .”

groups.context_description: Term marks an urgent entreaty between persons where the inner state of the speaker — fear, desperation, longing, moral distress — gives the request its force

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 34:8 But Hamor spoke with them, saying , “The soul of my son Shechem longs for your daughter . Please give her to him to be his wife .

Gen 44:18 Then Judah went up to him and said , “Oh, my lord , please let your servant speak a word in my lord’s ears , and let not your anger burn against your servant , for you are like Pharaoh himself.

groups.context_description: Term names what is fitting, lovely, or becoming — expressing the inner sense of rightness and beauty that governs aesthetic delight and moral appropriateness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 33:1 Shout for joy in the Lord , O you righteous ! Praise befits the upright .
Song 2:14 O my dove , in the clefts of the rock , in the crannies of the cliff , let me see your face , let me hear your voice , for your voice is sweet , and your face is lovely .

groups.context_description: Term names the groaning of the person in extremis — the soul's cry under mortal wounding, heard or unheard, expressing the ultimate inner-being limit of suffering

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 24:12 From out of the city the dying groan , and the soul of the wounded cries for help; yet God charges no one with wrong .

groups.context_description: Term names the groaning of those in bondage and extremity — the inner-being cry that God hears, that awakens his covenantal memory, and that moves him to compassionate intervention

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 2:24 And God heard their groaning , and God remembered his covenant with Abraham , with Isaac , and with Jacob .

groups.context_description: Term names inheritance as a moral-spiritual outcome — what a person receives as the result of their inner character and relationship with God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:111 Your testimonies are my heritage forever , for they are the joy of my heart .
Pro 3:35 The wise will inherit honor , but fools get disgrace .

groups.context_description: Term names God as the inheritance of the person — the inner-being orientation of those who possess God himself as their portion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 18:20 And the Lord said to Aaron , “You shall have no inheritance in their land , neither shall you have any portion among them. I am your portion and your inheritance among the people of Israel .

groups.context_description: Term names God himself or divine presence as the inheritance of the faithful person — the ultimate inner-being possession

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 18:20 And the Lord said to Aaron , “You shall have no inheritance in their land , neither shall you have any portion among them. I am your portion and your inheritance among the people of Israel .
Psa 16:6 The lines have fallen for me in pleasant places ; indeed , I have a beautiful inheritance .

groups.context_description: Term names the inheritance of the righteous as a moral-spiritual promise — what the upright receive as the outcome of their inner character

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 37:18 The Lord knows the days of the blameless , and their heritage will remain forever ;

groups.context_description: Term names what is received as an inner-being treasure — the law, children, or wisdom as inheritance of the heart

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 28:9 Oh, save your people and bless your heritage ! Be their shepherd and carry them forever .

groups.context_description: Term names the withholding of divine compassion — the inner-relational disposition of God toward a people under judgment, where compassion is deliberately hidden

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 13:14 I shall ransom them from the power of Sheol ; I shall redeem them from Death . O Death , where are your plagues ? O Sheol , where is your sting ? Compassion is hidden from my eyes .

groups.context_description: Term names divine comfort — God actively bringing relief and restoration to the inner grief of his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 40:1 Comfort , comfort my people , says your God .
Isa 66:13 As one whom his mother comforts , so I will comfort you; you shall be comforted in Jerusalem .

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 42:6 therefore I despise myself, and repent in dust and ashes .”

groups.context_description: Term names divine relenting — God's inner-being disposition changing in response to human repentance or intercession, turning from intended judgment

groups.context_description: Term names wandering or fleeing as an inner experience — restlessness of sleep fleeing, the anguish of spiritual straying, or the inner alienation expressed through wandering

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 55:7 yes , I would wander far away ; I would lodge in the wilderness ; Selah

groups.context_description: Term names the freewill inner disposition — voluntary offering or willingness expressed in dedication to God's house

Psa 51:12 Restore to me the joy of your salvation , and uphold me with a willing spirit .

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 30:15 Terrors are turned upon me; my honor is pursued as by the wind , and my prosperity has passed away like a cloud .

Jer 22:17 But you have eyes and heart only for your dishonest gain , for shedding innocent blood , and for practicing oppression and violence .”

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 24:41 Then you will be free from my oath , when you come to my clan . And if they will not give her to you, you will be free from my oath .’

Jos 2:19 Then if anyone goes out of the doors of your house into the street , his blood shall be on his own head , and we shall be guiltless . But if a hand is laid on anyone who is with you in the house , his blood shall be on our head .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-being quality of innocence — the condition of the heart and hands being free from moral guilt before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 20:5 Did he not himself say to me, ‘ She is my sister ’? And she herself said , ‘He is my brother .’ In the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands I have done this .”
Psa 73:13 All in vain have I kept my heart clean and washed my hands in innocence .

anchor_verses.verse_text: Of David . Psa 25:1 To you, O Lord , I lift up my soul .

Job 38:36 Who has put wisdom in the inward parts or given understanding to the mind ?

groups.context_description: Distinguished inner excellence — the excellent spirit as the ground of outward distinction

groups.context_description: Lament over God's apparent perpetual absence — inner anguish of "how long?"

anchor_verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster . A Psalm of David . Psa 13:1 How long , O Lord ? Will you forget me forever ? How long will you hide your face from me ?

groups.context_description: Perpetual inner suffering and unceasing pain — anguish that will not relent

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 15:18 Why is my pain unceasing , my wound incurable , refusing to be healed ? Will you be to me like a deceitful brook , like waters that fail ?

groups.context_description: Everlasting joy, hope and divine faithfulness — inner orientation toward what endures

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 16:11 You make known to me the path of life ; in your presence there is fullness of joy ; at your right hand are pleasures forevermore .

groups.context_description: Term names the prophetic word as a communication that engages or targets the inner life — heard prophecy moves to courage and action; false prophecy targets and manipulates inner disposition

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 15:8 As soon as Asa heard these words , the prophecy of Azariah the son of Oded , he took courage and put away the detestable idols from all the land of Judah and Benjamin and from the cities that he had taken in the hill country of Ephraim , and he repaired the altar of the Lord that was in front of the vestibule of the house of the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term identifies the act of prophesying as a Spirit-empowered inner event — the Spirit of God resting on or rushing upon a person activates an inward responsive capacity that issues in prophetic expression

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 11:25 Then the Lord came down in the cloud and spoke to him, and took some of the Spirit that was on him and put it on the seventy elders . And as soon as the Spirit rested on them, they prophesied . But they did not continue doing it.
1Sa 10:6 Then the Spirit of the Lord will rush upon you, and you will prophesy with them and be turned into another man .

groups.context_description: Term identifies prophesying as expression of a true inner commission and orientation toward God — the genuine prophet speaks from inward faithfulness to divine word

anchor_verses.verse_text: Amo 3:8 The lion has roared ; who will not fear ? The Lord God has spoken ; who can but prophesy ?”

groups.context_description: Term identifies prophesying as expression of inner deceit and corrupt orientation — false prophets speak from their own hearts, conceiving and uttering from an inward disposition of falsehood

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 23:26 How long shall there be lies in the heart of the prophets who prophesy lies , and who prophesy the deceit of their own heart ,

groups.context_description: Term identifies the prophetic act as obedient expression of divine commission — the prophet's inner responsiveness to God's word issues in directed speech against persons, places, or nations

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 37:4 Then he said to me, “ Prophesy over these bones , and say to them, O dry bones , hear the word of the Lord .
Eze 37:7 So I prophesied as I was commanded . And as I prophesied , there was a sound , and behold , a rattling , and the bones came together , bone to its bone .

groups.context_description: Title identifies a person whose identity and function is the mediation of divine inner-being communication — expressed in prophetic guidance, worship leadership, and prophetic manipulation

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 22:14 So Hilkiah the priest , and Ahikam , and Achbor , and Shaphan , and Asaiah went to Huldah the prophetess , the wife of Shallum the son of Tikvah , son of Harhas , keeper of the wardrobe (now she lived in Jerusalem in the Second Quarter ), and they talked with her .

groups.context_description: Term identifies prophesying as an inner-originating expression that sustains and motivates the inner disposition of hearers toward covenantal action

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ezr 6:14 And the elders of the Jews built and prospered through the prophesying of Haggai the prophet and Zechariah the son of Iddo . They finished their building by decree of the God of Israel and by decree of Cyrus and Darius and Artaxerxes king of Persia ;

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-being act of beautifying God through praise — the person's deliberate orientation toward adorning God with worship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 15:2 The Lord is my strength and my song , and he has become my salvation ; this is my God , and I will praise him , my father’s God , and I will exalt him .

groups.context_description: Term names expectation as an inner orientation — the forward-looking hope directed at a specific source, here misplaced and turned to shame at its collapse

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 20:6 And the inhabitants of this coastland will say in that day , ‘ Behold , this is what has happened to those in whom we hoped and to whom we fled for help to be delivered from the king of Assyria ! And we, how shall we escape ?’”

groups.context_description: The prophet as vessel of the divine word — inner-being vocation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 1:5 “ Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, and before you were born I consecrated you; I appointed you a prophet to the nations .”

groups.context_description: The false prophet — inner-being failure of self-generated speech, greed, and deception

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 23:26 How long shall there be lies in the heart of the prophets who prophesy lies , and who prophesy the deceit of their own heart ,

groups.context_description: Rejection of the prophet — Israel's inner-being resistance to God's claim through prophetic calling

anchor_verses.verse_text: Neh 9:26 “Nevertheless, they were disobedient and rebelled against you and cast your law behind their back and killed your prophets , who had warned them in order to turn them back to you, and they committed great blasphemies .

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 34:7 The sons of Jacob had come in from the field as soon as they heard of it, and the men were indignant and very angry , because he had done an outrageous thing in Israel by lying with Jacob’s daughter , for such a thing must not be done .

Isa 32:6 For the fool speaks folly , and his heart is busy with iniquity , to practice ungodliness , to utter error concerning the Lord , to leave the craving of the hungry unsatisfied , and to deprive the thirsty of drink .

groups.context_description: Term names lewdness as the inner condition of spiritual adultery — exposed by God before the lovers it sought

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 2:10 Now I will uncover her lewdness in the sight of her lovers , and no one shall rescue her out of my hand .

Hos 9:10 Like grapes in the wilderness , I found Israel . Like the first fruit on the fig tree in its first season , I saw your fathers . But they came to Baal-peor and consecrated themselves to the thing of shame , and became detestable like the thing they loved .

Num 6:5 “ All the days of his vow of separation , no razor shall touch his head . Until the time is completed for which he separates himself to the Lord , he shall be holy . He shall let the locks of hair of his head grow long .

groups.context_description: Term names the state of consecration itself — the dedicated condition of the person who has set themselves apart to God, whose inner life is wholly oriented toward this commitment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 6:7 Not even for his father or for his mother , for brother or sister , if they die , shall he make himself unclean , because his separation to God is on his head .
Num 6:8 All the days of his separation he is holy to the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names bronze as a metaphor for hardened moral character, inescapable bondage, or the inner resolve of divine strengthening — the material pointing to an inner state

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 6:28 They are all stubbornly rebellious , going about with slanders ; they are bronze and iron ; all of them act corruptly .
Lam 3:7 He has walled me about so that I cannot escape ; he has made my chains heavy ;

groups.context_description: Term names an inner faculty of the person — the deep inner life searched and assessed by Christ

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 2:23 and I will strike her children dead . And all the churches will know that I am he who searches mind and heart , and I will give to each of you according to your works .

groups.context_description: Spiritual deadness as the person's condition — dead in sin, dead to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 23:27 “ Woe to you , scribes and Pharisees , hypocrites ! For you are like whitewashed tombs , which outwardly appear beautiful , but within are full of dead people’s bones and all uncleanness .
Eph 2:1 And you were dead in the trespasses and sins

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 6:11 So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus .

Rom 7:4 Likewise , my brothers , you also have died to the law through the body of Christ , so that you may belong to another , to him who has been raised from the dead , in order that we may bear fruit for God .

Heb 9:14 how much more will the blood of Christ , who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without blemish to God , purify our conscience from dead works to serve the living God .

groups.context_description: Dead faith — faith as a dead inner-being capacity without works

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 2:17 So also faith by itself , if it does not have works , is dead .

groups.context_description: Resurrection of the dead as ground for inner-being hope, faith, and aspiration

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 3:11 that by any means possible I may attain the resurrection from the dead .

1Pe 1:3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ ! According to his great mercy , he has caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead ,

groups.context_description: Deliberate mortification of inner-being capacities — putting to death what is earthly

anchor_verses.verse_text: Col 3:5 Put to death therefore what is earthly in you: sexual immorality , impurity , passion , evil desire , and covetousness , which is idolatry .

Hos 11:8 How can I give you up, O Ephraim ? How can I hand you over, O Israel ? How can I make you like Admah ? How can I treat you like Zeboiim ? My heart recoils within me; my compassion grows warm and tender .

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 14:3 “ Son of man , these men have taken their idols into their hearts , and set the stumbling block of their iniquity before their faces . Should I indeed let myself be consulted by them ?

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 4:25 Let your eyes look directly forward , and your gaze be straight before you .

groups.context_description: Term names the sick/morbid craving of the inner person — the unhealthy inner obsession with controversy that produces envy, dissension, and evil suspicions

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 6:4 he is puffed up with conceit and understands nothing . He has an unhealthy craving for controversy and for quarrels about words , which produce envy , dissension , slander , evil suspicions ,

Luk 9:1 And he called the twelve together and gave them power and authority over all demons and to cure diseases ,

groups.context_description: Term names the disruption or perceived disruption of rational inner faculties — being out of one's mind, whether genuinely or in others' assessment

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of forgetting or neglecting — the failure to retain in active consciousness what matters morally or relationally

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 3:13 Brothers , I do not consider that I have made it my own . But one thing I do: forgetting what lies behind and straining forward to what lies ahead ,

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 10:5 We destroy arguments and every lofty opinion raised against the knowledge of God , and take every thought captive to obey Christ ,

Eze 20:41 As a pleasing aroma I will accept you, when I bring you out from the peoples and gather you out of the countries where you have been scattered . And I will manifest my holiness among you in the sight of the nations .

Hab 3:16 I hear , and my body trembles ; my lips quiver at the sound ; rottenness enters into my bones ; my legs tremble beneath me. Yet I will quietly wait for the day of trouble to come upon people who invade us .

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 6:10 For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evils . It is through this craving that some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pangs .

groups.context_description: Settled inner conviction orienting moral, spiritual, and relational life toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 14:14 I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself , but it is unclean for anyone who thinks it unclean .
2Cor 5:1 For we know that if the tent that is our earthly home is destroyed , we have a building from God , a house not made with hands , eternal in the heavens .

groups.context_description: Self-knowledge of inner conflict — awareness of flesh, sin, and spiritual limitation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 7:18 For I know that nothing good dwells in me , that is , in my flesh . For I have the desire to do what is right , but not the ability to carry it out.
1Cor 2:11 For who knows a person’s thoughts except the spirit of that person , which is in him ? So also no one comprehends the thoughts of God except the Spirit of God .

groups.context_description: Identity-shaping knowledge — who the person is in Christ, transforming inner orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:16 Do you not know that you are God’s temple and that God’s Spirit dwells in you ?
Eph 1:18 having the eyes of your hearts enlightened , that you may know what is the hope to which he has called you, what are the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints ,

groups.context_description: God's or another's knowledge of the inner person — hearts, thoughts, sincerity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 8:27 And he who searches hearts knows what is the mind of the Spirit , because the Spirit intercedes for the saints according to the will of God .
2Cor 11:11 And why ? Because I do not love you ? God knows I do!

groups.context_description: Knowledge divorced from love — spiritually empty inner-life capacity

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:1 Now concerning food offered to idols : we know that “ all of us possess knowledge .” This “ knowledge ” puffs up , but love builds up .
1Cor 13:2 And if I have prophetic powers , and understand all mysteries and all knowledge , and if I have all faith , so as to remove mountains , but have not love , I am nothing .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine compassion as an inner disposition of sovereign mercy — God's free inclination toward the objects of his compassion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 9:15 For he says to Moses , “I will have mercy on whom I have mercy , and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion .”

groups.context_description: Term names the compassions of God as his defining inner character — the mercies that motivate holy living and compassionate hearts as the inner garment of the new humanity

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ , the Father of mercies and God of all comfort ,
Col 3:12 Put on then , as God’s chosen ones , holy and beloved , compassionate hearts , kindness , humility , meekness , and patience ,

groups.context_description: Term names compassion as the inner character of God and the standard for human inner disposition — to be merciful as God is merciful

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 6:36 Be merciful , even as your Father is merciful .

Jam 5:11 Behold , we consider those blessed who remained steadfast . You have heard of the steadfastness of Job , and you have seen the purpose of the Lord , how the Lord is compassionate and merciful .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of laziness and reluctance — contrasted with zealous, fervent inner engagement

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 12:11 Do not be slothful in zeal , be fervent in spirit , serve the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of delay and hesitation — the reluctance to act that must be overcome in urgent obedience

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 9:38 Since Lydda was near Joppa , the disciples , hearing that Peter was there , sent two men to him , urging him, “Please come to us without delay .”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner affirmation of truth — the "truly" that anchors a moral assertion, a confession, or a conviction about God's character

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 9:2 “Truly I know that it is so: But how can a man be in the right before God ?
Job 34:12 Of a truth , God will not do wickedly , and the Almighty will not pervert justice .

groups.context_description: Term names the affliction of the person or people as it is seen and heard by God — the inner-being suffering that draws the divine eye and ear, and that becomes the ground of God's redemptive intervention

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 3:7 Then the Lord said , “I have surely seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt and have heard their cry because of their taskmasters . I know their sufferings ,

groups.context_description: Term names affliction as the inner experience of the person — the furnace through which God refines and teaches, the condition remembered before God, and the ground of trust in his word

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:50 This is my comfort in my affliction , that your promise gives me life .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner refreshment and benefit that one derives from another in the Lord — joy as relational nourishment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phile 20 Yes , brother , I want some benefit from you in the Lord . Refresh my heart in Christ .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine name as the embodied self-disclosure of God's character, nature, and presence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 2:9 Therefore God has highly exalted him and bestowed on him the name that is above every name ,
1Ti 6:1 Let all who are under a yoke as bondservants regard their own masters as worthy of all honor , so that the name of God and the teaching may not be reviled .

groups.context_description: Term is used in expressions of human inner orientation toward Christ/God — calling upon, trusting in, praising, fearing, enduring for, or working in the divine name

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 6:9 Pray then like this : “ Our Father in heaven , hallowed be your name .
1Jo 3:23 And this is his commandment , that we believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ and love one another , just as he has commanded us .

groups.context_description: Term designates a person's name as their identity, moral standing, and belonging — including names written in the book of life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 10:20 Nevertheless , do not rejoice in this , that the spirits are subject to you , but rejoice that your names are written in heaven .”

Rev 3:1 “ And to the angel of the church in Sardis write : ‘ The words of him who has the seven spirits of God and the seven stars . “‘I know your works . You have the reputation of being alive , but you are dead .

groups.context_description: Term appears in a theologically weighted naming act — the giving of a name expresses divine declaration or identity conferral

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 1:21 She will bear a son , and you shall call his name Jesus , for he will save his people from their sins .”

Rev 2:17 He who has an ear , let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches . To the one who conquers I will give some of the hidden manna , and I will give him a white stone , with a new name written on the stone that no one knows except the one who receives it.’

groups.context_description: Term expresses renown or known standing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 6:14 King Herod heard of it, for Jesus ’ name had become known . Some said , “ John the Baptist has been raised from the dead . That is why these miraculous powers are at work in him .”

groups.context_description: Term is used of the divine name as supreme over all named powers

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 1:21 far above all rule and authority and power and dominion , and above every name that is named , not only in this age but also in the one to come .

groups.context_description: Term denotes the act of invoking or naming the Lord's name, expressing an inner orientation of faith or moral commitment

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ti 2:19 But God’s firm foundation stands , bearing this seal : “The Lord knows those who are his ,” and , “Let everyone who names the name of the Lord depart from iniquity .”

groups.context_description: Term is used of bearing or naming an identity, with the name revealing or contrasting with the inner moral state of the person

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 5:11 But now I am writing to you not to associate with anyone who bears the name of brother if he is guilty of sexual immorality or greed , or is an idolater , reviler , drunkard , or swindler —not even to eat with such a one .

groups.context_description: Term denotes a theologically weighted naming act — Jesus names individuals or groups, conferring identity and role

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 6:13 And when day came , he called his disciples and chose from them twelve , whom he named apostles :
Eph 3:15 from whom every family in heaven and on earth is named ,

groups.context_description: On as vital strength: firstborn as firstfruits of vital energy

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 49:3 “ Reuben , you are my firstborn , my might , and the firstfruits of my strength , preeminent in dignity and preeminent in power .
Hos 12:3 In the womb he took his brother by the heel , and in his manhood he strove with God .

groups.context_description: On as strength given or withheld by God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 40:26 Lift up your eyes on high and see : who created these ? He who brings out their host by number , calling them all by name ; by the greatness of his might and because he is strong in power , not one is missing .
Isa 40:29 He gives power to the faint , and to him who has no might he increases strength .

groups.context_description: The eye as inner moral organ — healthy and bad eye as inner orientation of the whole person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 6:22 “The eye is the lamp of the body . So , if your eye is healthy , your whole body will be full of light ,

groups.context_description: Spiritual blindness and the closed eye — inner failure of perception and understanding

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 13:15 For this people’s heart has grown dull , and with their ears they can barely hear , and their eyes they have closed , lest they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears and understand with their heart and turn , and I would heal them .’

groups.context_description: Eyes opened to spiritual reality — the inner moment of recognition and illumination

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 1:18 having the eyes of your hearts enlightened , that you may know what is the hope to which he has called you, what are the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints ,

groups.context_description: Desires of the eyes and envy — the eye as instrument of inner covetousness

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Jo 2:16 For all that is in the world — the desires of the flesh and the desires of the eyes and pride of life — is not from the Father but is from the world .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner aspiration toward the heavenly — the deep desire that orients the inner person beyond the earthly toward what God has prepared

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 11:16 But as it is , they desire a better country, that is , a heavenly one . Therefore God is not ashamed to be called their God , for he has prepared for them a city .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner aspiration toward the heavenly — the deep desire that orients the inner person beyond the earthly toward what God has prepared

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 11:16 But as it is , they desire a better country, that is , a heavenly one . Therefore God is not ashamed to be called their God , for he has prepared for them a city .

groups.context_description: Term names consuming inner appetite or passion driving disordered conduct — desire as an overpowering inner force

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:27 and the men likewise gave up natural relations with women and were consumed with passion for one another , men committing shameless acts with men and receiving in themselves the due penalty for their error .

groups.context_description: Term names divine wrath as the revealed inner response to human unrighteousness and ungodliness — anger as the consistent inner orientation of God toward sin

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:18 For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men , who by their unrighteousness suppress the truth .

groups.context_description: Term names human anger as requiring governance — the inner state that must be put away, not nursed, as incompatible with righteous living

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 1:20 for the anger of man does not produce the righteousness of God .

groups.context_description: Term names righteous anger co-existing with grief — the simultaneous presence of anger and sorrow as the inner response to hardness of heart

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 3:5 And he looked around at them with anger , grieved at their hardness of heart , and said to the man , “ Stretch out your hand .” He stretched it out , and his hand was restored .

groups.context_description: Term names morally accountable anger — the inner state of anger that exists under divine command and requires governance before it becomes sin

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 4:26 Be angry and do not sin ; do not let the sun go down on your anger ,

groups.context_description: Term names anger as an inner action-driver — the fury that compels the person to respond, act, or enforce consequences

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 15:28 But he was angry and refused to go in . His father came out and entreated him ,

groups.context_description: Term names anger as cosmic inner rebellion against God — the hostility of nations and powers in ultimate opposition to divine rule

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 11:18 The nations raged , but your wrath came , and the time for the dead to be judged , and for rewarding your servants , the prophets and saints , and those who fear your name , both small and great , and for destroying the destroyers of the earth .”

groups.context_description: Term names anger as a provoked inner response to injustice or disobedience — the arousal of wrath in another as consequence of conduct

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 6:4 Fathers , do not provoke your children to anger , but bring them up in the discipline and instruction of the Lord .

groups.context_description: Signs as the direct conditions of inner belief — miraculous signs that function to produce or test faith, with belief or unbelief as the inner-being response explicitly tied to the sign

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 4:8 “If they will not believe you,” God said, “ or listen to the first sign , they may believe the latter sign .

Exo 4:9 If they will not believe even these two signs or listen to your voice , you shall take some water from the Nile and pour it on the dry ground , and the water that you shall take from the Nile will become blood on the dry ground .”

groups.context_description: Inner attentiveness called for — enōtizomai as the summons to receptive inner attention to proclaimed word

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 2:14 But Peter , standing with the eleven , lifted up his voice and addressed them : “ Men of Judea and all who dwell in Jerusalem , let this be known to you , and give ear to my words .

groups.context_description: Spiritual receptivity — ous as the inner organ of capacity to hear and respond to divine word (the hearing formula)

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 11:15 He who has ears to hear , let him hear .

groups.context_description: Inner hardness and resistance — ous as the organ of spiritual receptivity closed, dulled or uncircumcised by inner rebellion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 28:27 For this people’s heart has grown dull , and with their ears they can barely hear , and their eyes they have closed ; lest they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears and understand with their heart and turn , and I would heal them .’

groups.context_description: Blessed receptivity and deep reception — ous as the open inner organ through which grace and divine direction enter

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 13:16 But blessed are your eyes , for they see , and your ears , for they hear .

groups.context_description: Term names the medium as a false spiritual access point — the inner prohibition against seeking guidance through forbidden channels

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 19:31 “Do not turn to mediums or necromancers ; do not seek them out, and so make yourselves unclean by them: I am the Lord your God .

groups.context_description: Term names the desperate consultation of a medium — inner act of seeking forbidden spiritual guidance driven by despair

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 23:24 Moreover , Josiah put away the mediums and the necromancers and the household gods and the idols and all the abominations that were seen in the land of Judah and in Jerusalem , that he might establish the words of the law that were written in the book that Hilkiah the priest found in the house of the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of being strong or prevailing — in its inner-being dimension: the Spirit-given strength that prevails, the brazen boldness of the wicked, the misplaced strengthening in riches, and the wisdom-given strength that surpasses raw power

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 3:10 The Spirit of the Lord was upon him, and he judged Israel . He went out to war , and the Lord gave Cushan-rishathaim king of Mesopotamia into his hand . And his hand prevailed over Cushan-rishathaim .
Pro 21:29 A wicked man puts on a bold face , but the upright gives thought to his ways .

groups.context_description: Term names God as the inner refuge and stronghold of the person — the secure centre of the self, the source of strength and salvation, to whom the inner person turns in trust

anchor_verses.verse_text: Of David . Psa 27:1 The Lord is my light and my salvation ; whom shall I fear ? The Lord is the stronghold of my life ; of whom shall I be afraid ?
Nah 1:7 The Lord is good , a stronghold in the day of trouble ; he knows those who take refuge in him .

groups.context_description: Term names the false refuge — the misplaced trust in human or material security in place of God — and its exposure as shame

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 52:7 “ See the man who would not make God his refuge , but trusted in the abundance of his riches and sought refuge in his own destruction !”
Isa 30:2 who set out to go down to Egypt , without asking for my direction , to take refuge in the protection of Pharaoh and to seek shelter in the shadow of Egypt !

groups.context_description: Term names strength as the intensity of inner character — fierce anger, passionate love, or the vigour of committed action — and as the quality of personal moral force

Eze 7:24 I will bring the worst of the nations to take possession of their houses . I will put an end to the pride of the strong , and their holy places shall be profaned .
