anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 15:2 The Lord is my strength and my song , and he has become my salvation ; this is my God , and I will praise him , my father’s God , and I will exalt him .
Psa 28:7 The Lord is my strength and my shield ; in him my heart trusts , and I am helped ; my heart exults , and with my song I give thanks to him .

groups.context_description: Term names God's strength as the object of communal worship — the strength ascribed to God in the sanctuary as the expression of corporate inner orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ch 16:28 Ascribe to the Lord , O families of the peoples , ascribe to the Lord glory and strength !
Psa 68:35 Awesome is God from his sanctuary ; the God of Israel — he is the one who gives power and strength to his people . Blessed be God !

groups.context_description: Term names God's strength as the inner refuge and security of those who trust him — the safe place to which the person returns in times of threat

anchor_verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster . Of the Sons of Korah . According to Alamoth . A Song . Psa 46:1 God is our refuge and strength , a very present help in trouble .
Psa 59:9 O my Strength , I will watch for you, for you, O God , are my fortress .

groups.context_description: Term names strength as the attribute tied to moral character — wisdom-linked strength that exceeds raw force, and the ethical quality of the strong person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 84:5 Blessed are those whose strength is in you, in whose heart are the highways to Zion.
Pro 31:25 Strength and dignity are her clothing , and she laughs at the time to come .

groups.context_description: Term names human strength as the object of God's judgment — the pride of power brought low, the collapse of the strong under divine claim

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 15:13 “You have led in your steadfast love the people whom you have redeemed ; you have guided them by your strength to your holy abode .
Lev 26:19 and I will break the pride of your power , and I will make your heavens like iron and your earth like bronze .

groups.context_description: Term names the disposition of reverential fear toward God that is owed but absent — the inner orientation Israel has forfeited through apostasy

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 2:19 Your evil will chastise you, and your apostasy will reprove you. Know and see that it is evil and bitter for you to forsake the Lord your God ; the fear of me is not in you, declares the Lord God of hosts .

groups.context_description: Term describes the inner experience of dread or terror under threat — a fear that destabilizes, paralyzes, or consumes the person inwardly

Job 4:14 dread came upon me, and trembling , which made all my bones shake .

groups.context_description: Term describes the inner orientation of reverential fear and awe toward God — the fear that is the proper and blessed response to divine majesty, word, and goodness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 28:14 Blessed is the one who fears the Lord always , but whoever hardens his heart will fall into calamity .

groups.context_description: Term describes the absence or displacement of fear — inner peace, confidence, and security that replaces dread through trust in God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Of David . Psa 27:1 The Lord is my light and my salvation ; whom shall I fear ? The Lord is the stronghold of my life ; of whom shall I be afraid ?

groups.context_description: Dread of God — the inner terror and trembling before divine majesty

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:120 My flesh trembles for fear of you, and I am afraid of your judgments .

groups.context_description: Dread as anticipated inner terror — the fear that comes upon the heart before disaster

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 3:25 For the thing that I fear comes upon me, and what I dread befalls me .

groups.context_description: Dread falling on enemies — the inner terror of nations before God or his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 15:16 Terror and dread fall upon them; because of the greatness of your arm , they are still as a stone , till your people , O Lord , pass by , till the people pass by whom you have purchased .

groups.context_description: The absence or presence of dread as inner moral condition — the wicked without fear of God; the righteous without terror

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 21:9 Their houses are safe from fear , and no rod of God is upon them .

groups.context_description: Dread of God — the inner terror and trembling before divine majesty

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:120 My flesh trembles for fear of you, and I am afraid of your judgments .

groups.context_description: Dread as anticipated inner terror — the fear that comes upon the heart before disaster

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 3:25 For the thing that I fear comes upon me, and what I dread befalls me .

groups.context_description: Dread falling on enemies — the inner terror of nations before God or his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 15:16 Terror and dread fall upon them; because of the greatness of your arm , they are still as a stone , till your people , O Lord , pass by , till the people pass by whom you have purchased .

groups.context_description: The absence or presence of dread as inner moral condition — the wicked without fear of God; the righteous without terror

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 21:9 Their houses are safe from fear , and no rod of God is upon them .

groups.context_description: Term names reckless instability of character — the inner disposition that acts impulsively without regard for moral boundaries

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 49:4 Unstable as water , you shall not have preeminence , because you went up to your father’s bed ; then you defiled it—he went up to my couch !

groups.context_description: The remembered redemption — gratitude, obligation and trust grounded in God's ransoming act

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 31:5 Into your hand I commit my spirit ; you have redeemed me, O Lord , faithful God .

groups.context_description: The appeal for redemption — inner longing and cry for God to ransom the soul

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 69:18 Draw near to my soul , redeem me; ransom me because of my enemies !

groups.context_description: The costliness of ransom — the impossibility of self-redemption and inner insufficiency before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 49:8 for the ransom of their life is costly and can never suffice ,

groups.context_description: Redemption as divine provision and ground of inner hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 130:7 O Israel , hope in the Lord ! For with the Lord there is steadfast love , and with him is plentiful redemption .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of intercession — entreaty or pleading on behalf of another before God, or the absence of such intercession as a moral absence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:12 Therefore I will divide him a portion with the many , and he shall divide the spoil with the strong , because he poured out his soul to death and was numbered with the transgressors ; yet he bore the sin of many , and makes intercession for the transgressors .
Jer 7:16 “As for you , do not pray for this people , or lift up a cry or prayer for them, and do not intercede with me, for I will not hear you .

groups.context_description: Term names the substitutionary laying of iniquity on another — the moral burden of the many placed on the one who bears it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:6 All we like sheep have gone astray ; we have turned —every one —to his own way ; and the Lord has laid on him the iniquity of us all .

groups.context_description: Term names the wondrous/miraculous as a category of divine self-disclosure — God's identity as wonder-worker and the quality of incomprehensible excellence that is his name

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pe Psa 119:129 Your testimonies are wonderful ; therefore my soul keeps them.

Isa 9:6 For to us a child is born , to us a son is given ; and the government shall be upon his shoulder , and his name shall be called Wonderful Counselor , Mighty God , Everlasting Father , Prince of Peace .

groups.context_description: Term names the wondrous works and deeds of God — acts beyond human comprehension that evoke wonder, awe, and praise

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:18 Open my eyes , that I may behold wondrous things out of your law .
Psa 139:14 I praise you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made . Wonderful are your works ; my soul knows it very well .

groups.context_description: Term marks things that surpass human capacity or comprehension — naming an inner-being condition of being exceeded or overwhelmed by reality

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 30:11 “ For this commandment that I command you today is not too hard for you, neither is it far off .
Job 42:3 ‘ Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge ?’ Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand , things too wonderful for me, which I did not know .

groups.context_description: Term names the incomprehensible as a quality of divine identity or knowledge that surpasses human inner capacity — evoking awe at what exceeds the person's grasp

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 139:6 Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high ; I cannot attain it .

groups.context_description: Term designates the wondrous works of God as incomprehensible acts testifying to divine inner perfection — works designed to evoke wonder in the human inner being

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 37:16 Do you know the balancings of the clouds , the wondrous works of him who is perfect in knowledge ,

groups.context_description: Term intensifies inner-being certainty, conviction, and commitment — the surely/certainly that names the firmness of moral judgment, apostolic conviction, and wholehearted resolve

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 4:23 And he said to them , “ Doubtless you will quote to me this proverb , ‘ Physician , heal yourself .’ What we have heard you did at Capernaum , do here in your hometown as well.”
1Cor 9:22 To the weak I became weak , that I might win the weak . I have become all things to all people, that by all means I might save some .

groups.context_description: Term names the constancy of the inner-being orientation — always praying, always giving thanks, always rejoicing, always abounding — as the mark of a life wholly directed toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 18:1 And he told them a parable to the effect that they ought always to pray and not lose heart .
Phili 4:4 Rejoice in the Lord always ; again I will say , rejoice .

groups.context_description: Term names God as the Almighty — the All-Sovereign whose absolute power is the object of worship, the ground of covenant relationship, and the authority over all history and judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 6:18 and I will be a father to you , and you shall be sons and daughters to me , says the Lord Almighty .”
Rev 1:8 “ I am the Alpha and the Omega ,” says the Lord God , “ who is and who was and who is to come , the Almighty .”

groups.context_description: Term names the universal scope of the gospel's reach — preached, commanded, and received everywhere — disclosing the inner-being mandate that has no geographic boundary

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 16:20 And they went out and preached everywhere , while the Lord worked with them and confirmed the message by accompanying signs .]]
Act 17:30 The times of ignorance God overlooked , but now he commands all people everywhere to repent ,

groups.context_description: Term names completeness in its inner-being dimension — the complete inability of the bound, and the complete sufficiency of Christ's saving power

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 13:11 And behold, there was a woman who had had a disabling spirit for eighteen years . She was bent over and could not fully straighten herself.
Heb 7:25 Consequently , he is able to save to the uttermost those who draw near to God through him , since he always lives to make intercession for them .

groups.context_description: Term names the complete encirclement — being hemmed in on every side — as the inner-being experience of inescapable judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 19:43 For the days will come upon you , when your enemies will set up a barricade around you and surround you and hem you in on every side

groups.context_description: Term names the universal drawing of people toward Jesus from every quarter — the inner orientation of seeking that comes from all sides

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 1:45 But he went out and began to talk freely about it, and to spread the news , so that Jesus could no longer openly enter a town , but was out in desolate places , and people were coming to him from every quarter .

groups.context_description: Term names appointment to oversight or charge — the inner being engaged through the trust, responsibility, and accountability that comes with being set over persons, property, or a work

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 34:13 Who gave him charge over the earth , and who laid on him the whole world ?
Psa 31:5 Into your hand I commit my spirit ; you have redeemed me, O Lord , faithful God .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of contemplative attention — the focused consideration of what is perceived in a revelatory vision

groups.context_description: Term designates a skilled or instructive song as an act of worship — the inner devotional origin implied in the call to praise God as King of all the earth (Psa 47:7); an expression of the inward orientation of worship through outward skill.

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 47:7 For God is the King of all the earth ; sing praises with a psalm !

groups.context_description: Term names prudent wisdom as an inner capacity — understanding, considering, and perceiving wisely as an orientation toward God and truth

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 14:2 The Lord looks down from heaven on the children of man , to see if there are any who understand , who seek after God .

Jer 9:24 but let him who boasts boast in this , that he understands and knows me, that I am the Lord who practices steadfast love , justice , and righteousness in the earth . For in these things I delight , declares the Lord .”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner wisdom that produces wise conduct and success — the capacity for prudent discernment in action that governs how the person acts (Isa 52:13); wisdom as the inner quality expressed in competent, God-aligned conduct.

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 52:13 Behold , my servant shall act wisely ; he shall be high and lifted up , and shall be exalted .

groups.context_description: Term names understanding as an inner-being capacity — good sense and discernment that God gives or withholds, shaping how a person relates and conducts themselves

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 16:22 Good sense is a fountain of life to him who has it, but the instruction of fools is folly .
Pro 19:11 Good sense makes one slow to anger , and it is his glory to overlook an offense .

groups.context_description: Term names God's act of forgiving sin as the outcome of atonement — the cultic and covenantal restoration of the person's standing before God through sacrifice and priestly mediation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 4:20 Thus shall he do with the bull . As he did with the bull of the sin offering , so shall he do with this. And the priest shall make atonement for them , and they shall be forgiven .

groups.context_description: Term names the intercession for divine forgiveness — the act of seeking God's pardon on behalf of oneself or others, and God's response of pardoning or withholding

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 9:19 O Lord , hear ; O Lord , forgive . O Lord , pay attention and act . Delay not , for your own sake , O my God , because your city and your people are called by your name .”

groups.context_description: Term names God's promised and proclaimed forgiveness in the new covenant context — the unconditional covenantal act by which God forgives iniquity and remembers sin no more

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 31:34 And no longer shall each one teach his neighbor and each his brother , saying , ‘ Know the Lord ,’ for they shall all know me, from the least of them to the greatest , declares the Lord . For I will forgive their iniquity , and I will remember their sin no more .”

groups.context_description: Term names forgiveness as a characteristic attribute of God's inner being — the disposition of readiness to pardon that belongs to his essential goodness and steadfast love

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 86:5 For you , O Lord , are good and forgiving , abounding in steadfast love to all who call upon you .

groups.context_description: Term names forgiveness as a divine attribute and possession — a quality that belongs to God's character, that grounds the possibility of the human-divine relationship, and that evokes reverential fear

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 130:4 But with you there is forgiveness , that you may be feared .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of defiant exultation in suffering — the joy that arises from moral integrity before God even in the midst of pain

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 6:10 This would be my comfort ; I would even exult in pain unsparing , for I have not denied the words of the Holy One .

groups.context_description: Term names the crookedness or perverseness that destroys — the inner moral distortion that fractures both the self and others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 11:3 The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them .
Pro 15:4 A gentle tongue is a tree of life , but perverseness in it breaks the spirit .

groups.context_description: Term names the perversion of moral discernment — the subverting of inner judgement and the cause of the righteous through corrupt influence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 16:19 You shall not pervert justice . You shall not show partiality , and you shall not accept a bribe , for a bribe blinds the eyes of the wise and subverts the cause of the righteous .

groups.context_description: Term names the ruin brought by sin or folly upon the inner life — the self-destruction of the wicked through their own perversion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 19:3 When a man’s folly brings his way to ruin , his heart rages against the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the state of being disfavoured or unloved — an inner relational orientation of lesser love or active disfavour that carries legal and relational consequence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 21:15 “ If a man has two wives , the one loved and the other unloved , and both the loved and the unloved have borne him children , and if the firstborn son belongs to the unloved ,

groups.context_description: Term names hatred as an inner hostile disposition directed toward another person — a settled enmity that motivates action, can be concealed, and stands in direct contrast to love

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 13:15 Then Amnon hated her with very great hatred , so that the hatred with which he hated her was greater than the love with which he had loved her. And Amnon said to her, “Get up ! Go !”
Pro 10:18 The one who conceals hatred has lying lips , and whoever utters slander is a fool .

groups.context_description: Term names hatred as an attributed inner disposition of God toward a people — projected or perceived divine enmity used to interpret suffering or covenant relationship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 1:27 And you murmured in your tents and said , ‘Because the Lord hated us he has brought us out of the land of Egypt , to give us into the hand of the Amorites , to destroy us .

groups.context_description: Term names hatred as a fundamental inner human capacity — listed alongside love and envy as constitutive orientations of the person that define relational existence and cease at death

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:6 Their love and their hate and their envy have already perished , and forever they have no more share in all that is done under the sun .

groups.context_description: Term names governance as the outward expression of the ruler's inner character — wisdom, justice, righteousness, and peace embodied in dominion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 9:6 For to us a child is born , to us a son is given ; and the government shall be upon his shoulder , and his name shall be called Wonderful Counselor , Mighty God , Everlasting Father , Prince of Peace .

groups.context_description: Term names striving with God as the defining inner act — tenacious, costly engagement with the divine that transforms identity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 32:28 Then he said , “Your name shall no longer be called Jacob , but Israel , for you have striven with God and with men , and have prevailed .”

groups.context_description: Sarx as governing principle of fallen human desire, passions, and moral incapacity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 8:7 For the mind that is set on the flesh is hostile to God , for it does not submit to God’s law ; indeed , it cannot .

Gal 5:17 For the desires of the flesh are against the Spirit , and the desires of the Spirit are against the flesh , for these are opposed to each other , to keep you from doing the things you want to do.

groups.context_description: Sarx as creaturely mortal condition of embodied human existence

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 15:50 I tell you this , brothers : flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God , nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable .
If I am to live in the flesh, that means fruitful labor for me. Yet which I shall choose I cannot tell.

groups.context_description: Sarx in Christological confession: Christs incarnate flesh

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 1:14 And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us , and we have seen his glory , glory as of the only Son from the Father , full of grace and truth .
By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God,

groups.context_description: Sarx in covenantal union: one flesh as marriage bond

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 19:5 and said , ‘ Therefore a man shall leave his father and his mother and hold fast to his wife , and the two shall become one flesh ’?

groups.context_description: Term names the fleshly inner condition — the person dominated by the flesh, manifesting in jealousy, strife, and spiritual immaturity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 7:14 For we know that the law is spiritual , but I am of the flesh , sold under sin .

groups.context_description: Term names the fleshly inner orientation in conflict with the Spirit — fleshly wisdom, weapons, passions warring against the soul

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:3 for you are still of the flesh . For while there is jealousy and strife among you , are you not of the flesh and behaving only in a human way ?

groups.context_description: Term names rejoicing as the active inner and communal expression of joy — the gladness that characterises a people, a place, or a relationship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 60:15 Whereas you have been forsaken and hated , with no one passing through , I will make you majestic forever , a joy from age to age .
Isa 62:5 For as a young man marries a young woman , so shall your sons marry you, and as the bridegroom rejoices over the bride , so shall your God rejoice over you.

groups.context_description: Term names rejoicing as what is taken away in judgment — the inner gladness silenced by divine discipline or catastrophe

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 24:11 There is an outcry in the streets for lack of wine ; all joy has grown dark ; the gladness of the earth is banished .
Lam 5:15 The joy of our hearts has ceased ; our dancing has been turned to mourning .

groups.context_description: Term names exultation as the inner quality characterising the life of the righteous in God's presence and word

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 45:7 you have loved righteousness and hated wickedness . Therefore God , your God , has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond your companions ;
Psa 119:111 Your testimonies are my heritage forever , for they are the joy of my heart .

groups.context_description: Term names the restoration of inner joy as the sign of divine renewal — joy returned to those who were desolate

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:12 Restore to me the joy of your salvation , and uphold me with a willing spirit .
Isa 12:3 With joy you will draw water from the wells of salvation .

groups.context_description: Term names the deep inner exultation of the person before God — the soul's leap of joy in salvation, in God's word, and in his presence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 35:9 Then my soul will rejoice in the Lord , exulting in his salvation .

Isa 61:10 I will greatly rejoice in the Lord ; my soul shall exult in my God , for he has clothed me with the garments of salvation ; he has covered me with the robe of righteousness , as a bridegroom decks himself like a priest with a beautiful headdress , and as a bride adorns herself with her jewels .

groups.context_description: Term names divine exultation over his people — God's inner delight and rejoicing that he brings to bear upon his restored community

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 32:41 I will rejoice in doing them good , and I will plant them in this land in faithfulness , with all my heart and all my soul .
Zep 3:17 The Lord your God is in your midst , a mighty one who will save ; he will rejoice over you with gladness ; he will quiet you by his love ; he will exult over you with loud singing .

groups.context_description: Term names a settled inner hostile disposition — persistent hatred or grudge-bearing directed toward another person, driving external aggression or threatening intent

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 27:41 Now Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father had blessed him, and Esau said to himself , “The days of mourning for my father are approaching ; then I will kill my brother Jacob .”
Psa 55:3 because of the noise of the enemy , because of the oppression of the wicked . For they drop trouble upon me, and in anger they bear a grudge against me .

groups.context_description: Term names hatred as an inner disposition — an established hostile attitude toward God or others that characterises the person's orientation and draws divine judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 9:7 The days of punishment have come ; the days of recompense have come ; Israel shall know it. The prophet is a fool ; the man of the spirit is mad , because of your great iniquity and great hatred .

groups.context_description: Term names hope in God — the inner orientation of trust and expectation toward God as the source of blessing and protection

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 146:5 Blessed is he whose help is the God of Jacob , whose hope is in the Lord his God ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of looking to or hoping in God — the expectant orientation of persons or creation toward God as provider and savior

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:166 I hope for your salvation , O Lord , and I do your commandments .
Psa 145:15 The eyes of all look to you, and you give them their food in due season .

groups.context_description: Division of a community — relational fracture producing inner-social split

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 14:4 But the people of the city were divided ; some sided with the Jews and some with the apostles .

groups.context_description: Division within a community — inner relational fracture over allegiance, identity, and unity of mind

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:10 I appeal to you , brothers , by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ , that all of you agree , and that there be no divisions among you , but that you be united in the same mind and the same judgment .

groups.context_description: Divine knowledge of human thought — God declaring to man what his thought is, as part of the divine creative and revelatory act

anchor_verses.verse_text: Amo 4:13 For behold , he who forms the mountains and creates the wind , and declares to man what is his thought , who makes the morning darkness , and treads on the heights of the earth — the Lord , the God of hosts , is his name !

groups.context_description: Term names the inner orientation of reverent devotion — the disposition that characterises the God-fearer and true worshiper, producing faithful attendance to the divine

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 16:14 One who heard us was a woman named Lydia , from the city of Thyatira , a seller of purple goods , who was a worshiper of God . The Lord opened her heart to pay attention to what was said by Paul .
Act 17:4 And some of them were persuaded and joined Paul and Silas , as did a great many of the devout Greeks and not a few of the leading women .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of worship directed at God or idols — reverent devotion that can be genuine or misdirected, fruitful or vain

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 15:9 in vain do they worship me , teaching as doctrines the commandments of men .’”

groups.context_description: Term names the God-fearer as one whose inner orientation of reverence and obedience opens divine attentiveness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 9:31 We know that God does not listen to sinners , but if anyone is a worshiper of God and does his will , God listens to him .

groups.context_description: Term names troubled, disquieting thoughts — the inner agitation of the mind under pressure or in visionary states

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 20:2 “ Therefore my thoughts answer me, because of my haste within me .

groups.context_description: Term names inner division — the split allegiance of the person who cannot commit fully to God's law

anchor_verses.verse_text: Samekh Psa 119:113 I hate the double-minded , but I love your law .

groups.context_description: Closing the heart against compassion — the inner act of hardening whereby the inner being shuts itself off from pity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 17:10 They close their hearts to pity ; with their mouths they speak arrogantly .

groups.context_description: Term names folly as the observed inner-opposite of wisdom — a condition the searching mind investigates, compares, and recognises as corrupting and self-defeating

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 10:13 The beginning of the words of his mouth is foolishness , and the end of his talk is evil madness .

groups.context_description: Term names the fool's inner condition of lacking sense and understanding — a pervasive inner blindness evident in speech, behaviour, and spiritual perception

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 5:21 “ Hear this , O foolish and senseless people , who have eyes , but see not , who have ears , but hear not .

groups.context_description: Term names folly as an inner condition of those in positions of leadership — the inversion where those lacking wisdom are placed in authority

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 10:6 folly is set in many high places, and the rich sit in a low place.

groups.context_description: Term names folly as an object of the searching inner mind — a condition placed alongside wisdom as something the person investigates and must understand

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 1:17 And I applied my heart to know wisdom and to know madness and folly . I perceived that this also is but a striving after wind .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner quality of gravity and honourableness — the settled inner character that commands respect; moral dignity as a disposition of the whole person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 4:8 Finally , brothers , whatever is true , whatever is honorable , whatever is just , whatever is pure , whatever is lovely , whatever is commendable , if there is any excellence , if there is anything worthy of praise , think about these things .

groups.context_description: Term names dignity as an inner quality of character — the gravitas and moral seriousness that shapes the person's life, conduct, and teaching

anchor_verses.verse_text: Tit 2:7 Show yourself in all respects to be a model of good works , and in your teaching show integrity , dignity ,

groups.context_description: Term names worship-prostration as an inner devotional response to divine revelation — recognition of God's presence and truth

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 2:46 Then King Nebuchadnezzar fell upon his face and paid homage to Daniel , and commanded that an offering and incense be offered up to him .

groups.context_description: Term names commanded or coerced prostration before a human image — the false worship system that demands inner-being allegiance, which the faithful refuse

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 3:28 Nebuchadnezzar answered and said , “ Blessed be the God of Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego , who has sent his angel and delivered his servants , who trusted in him, and set aside the king’s command , and yielded up their bodies rather than serve and worship any god except their own God .

groups.context_description: Term names prostration in worship misdirected toward idols — the physical expression of inner devotion wrongly oriented

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 44:17 And the rest of it he makes into a god , his idol , and falls down to it and worships it. He prays to it and says , “ Deliver me, for you are my god !”

groups.context_description: Term names deliberate inner intention or scheming — the inner act of purposing to change or overturn what is established

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 7:25 He shall speak words against the Most High , and shall wear out the saints of the Most High , and shall think to change the times and the law ; and they shall be given into his hand for a time , times , and half a time .

groups.context_description: Term names the desires of the heart as petitions before God — what the inner being asks for and hopes to receive

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 37:4 Delight yourself in the Lord , and he will give you the desires of your heart .

groups.context_description: Term names inquiring of God — the inner orientation of seeking divine guidance, oracle, or provision through direct petition or prayer

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 32:29 Then Jacob asked him, “ Please tell me your name .” But he said , “ Why is it that you ask my name ?” And there he blessed him.

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 4:12 My people inquire of a piece of wood , and their walking staff gives them oracles . For a spirit of whoredom has led them astray , and they have left their God to play the whore .

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 38:3 Dress for action like a man ; I will question you, and you make it known to me .

groups.context_description: Term names asking as the expression of personal desire or inner appetite — the person's own craving or longing framed as a request

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:10 And whatever my eyes desired I did not keep from them. I kept my heart from no pleasure , for my heart found pleasure in all my toil , and this was my reward for all my toil .

groups.context_description: Inner condition of ease and security — freedom from dread as positive state, often fruit of trust or divine promise

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 1:33 but whoever listens to me will dwell secure and will be at ease , without dread of disaster .”

groups.context_description: Complacent ease — inner condition of undisturbed settledness that has become spiritual stagnation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 48:11 “ Moab has been at ease from his youth and has settled on his dregs ; he has not been emptied from vessel to vessel , nor has he gone into exile ; so his taste remains in him, and his scent is not changed .

groups.context_description: Term names intense panting desire — the inner being straining urgently, gasping, or panting toward its object; longing expressed as bodily urgency

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:131 I open my mouth and pant , because I long for your commandments .

groups.context_description: Delighting in God's word and finding consolation — inner-being rest in God's statutes

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:47 for I find my delight in your commandments , which I love .

groups.context_description: Deep delight as a settled inner orientation — the law as delight and God's heartfelt attachment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 31:20 Is Ephraim my dear son ? Is he my darling child ? For as often as I speak against him, I do remember him still . Therefore my heart yearns for him; I will surely have mercy on him, declares the Lord .

groups.context_description: Blinding of inner perception — the hardening of heart and eye against understanding

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 6:10 Make the heart of this people dull , and their ears heavy , and blind their eyes ; lest they see with their eyes , and hear with their ears , and understand with their hearts , and turn and be healed .”

groups.context_description: Term names the divine roaring that demands inner response — the lion-voice of God that produces unavoidable fear, trembling, and return in the inner being of those who hear it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Amo 3:8 The lion has roared ; who will not fear ? The Lord God has spoken ; who can but prophesy ?”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner groaning and roaring of anguish — the tumult of the heart expressed as an uncontainable inner sound under suffering and threat

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 38:8 I am feeble and crushed ; I groan because of the tumult of my heart .

groups.context_description: Term names the roaring groan as inner-being expression — the cry of dereliction, the groan of unconfessed guilt, and the wail of loss that pours out of the person at the extremity of inner suffering

anchor_verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster : according to The Doe of the Dawn . A Psalm of David . Psa 22:1 My God , my God , why have you forsaken me? Why are you so far from saving me, from the words of my groaning ?

groups.context_description: Inner reliance on God as the proper object of trust

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 13:18 Thus the men of Israel were subdued at that time , and the men of Judah prevailed , because they relied on the Lord , the God of their fathers .

groups.context_description: Misplaced or hypocritical inner reliance on wrong objects — people, military, self

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 3:5 Trust in the Lord with all your heart , and do not lean on your own understanding .

groups.context_description: Divine support as inner-being ground of confidence in calamity

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 22:19 They confronted me in the day of my calamity , but the Lord was my support .

groups.context_description: Divine support as inner-being ground of confidence in calamity

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 22:19 They confronted me in the day of my calamity , but the Lord was my support .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of ease and security — the state of the person wholly undisturbed, here in the context of unjust prosperity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 21:23 One dies in his full vigor , being wholly at ease and secure ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inner condition of complacent ease — the self-secure, untroubled disposition that produces contempt, moral blindness, or judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 12:5 In the thought of one who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune ; it is ready for those whose feet slip .
Amo 6:1 “ Woe to those who are at ease in Zion , and to those who feel secure on the mountain of Samaria , the notable men of the first of the nations , to whom the house of Israel comes !

groups.context_description: Term names the divinely intended condition of secure quietness — the ease of God's people living in peace and safety as a condition of shalom

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 32:18 My people will abide in a peaceful habitation , in secure dwellings , and in quiet resting places .

groups.context_description: Term names worship as the act of prostration and bowing before God — the complete physical and inner-being orientation of the person in submission and reverence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 34:8 And Moses quickly bowed his head toward the earth and worshiped .
Psa 95:6 Oh come , let us worship and bow down ; let us kneel before the Lord , our Maker !

groups.context_description: Term names idolatrous prostration — bowing to other gods or images as the inner-being act of misplaced worship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 5:9 You shall not bow down to them or serve them, for I the Lord your God am a jealous God , visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children to the third and fourth generation of those who hate me ,

groups.context_description: Term names social prostration as an act of relational deference — the physical expression of honour, petition, or submission before persons of higher standing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 18:2 He lifted up his eyes and looked , and behold , three men were standing in front of him. When he saw them, he ran from the tent door to meet them and bowed himself to the earth

2Sa 14:22 And Joab fell on his face to the ground and paid homage and blessed the king . And Joab said , “ Today your servant knows that I have found favor in your sight , my lord the king , in that the king has granted the request of his servant .”

groups.context_description: Term names the pit of destruction as existential peril — the state of being trapped or facing annihilation, from which God delivers

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 107:20 He sent out his word and healed them, and delivered them from their destruction .

groups.context_description: Term names the pit as a metaphor for moral self-entrapment — the consequence of misleading others returns upon the one who set it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 28:10 Whoever misleads the upright into an evil way will fall into his own pit , but the blameless will have a goodly inheritance .

groups.context_description: Term names earnest inner-directed seeking — the soul and spirit yearning and seeking God, particularly in distress or need, expressing deep inner orientation toward divine encounter

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 26:9 My soul yearns for you in the night ; my spirit within me earnestly seeks you. For when your judgments are in the earth , the inhabitants of the world learn righteousness .

groups.context_description: Term names the corruption of the inner moral life — acting corruptly before God as the expression of the person's inward turn away from his ways

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 6:12 And God saw the earth , and behold , it was corrupt , for all flesh had corrupted their way on the earth .

Deu 4:25 “ When you father children and children’s children , and have grown old in the land , and you act corruptly by making a carved image in the form of anything , and by doing what is evil in the sight of the Lord your God , so as to provoke him to anger ,

groups.context_description: Term names self-destruction as the consequence of inner moral failure — the person who corrupts themselves bringing ruin upon themselves

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 6:32 He who commits adultery lacks sense ; he who does it destroys himself .
Pro 11:9 With his mouth the godless man would destroy his neighbor , but by knowledge the righteous are delivered .

groups.context_description: Term names the divine act of destruction as the response to inner moral corruption — God's judgment that mirrors the people's own corrupted way

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 6:13 And God said to Noah , “I have determined to make an end of all flesh , for the earth is filled with violence through them . Behold , I will destroy them with the earth .

Deu 9:26 And I prayed to the Lord , ‘O Lord God , do not destroy your people and your heritage , whom you have redeemed through your greatness , whom you have brought out of Egypt with a mighty hand .

groups.context_description: Term names the physical marring of the Servant as the outer expression of the inner-being cost of vicarious suffering — the corruption of appearance as the vehicle of redemptive identification

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 52:14 As many were astonished at you— his appearance was so marred , beyond human semblance, and his form beyond that of the children of mankind —

groups.context_description: Term names apparent or feigned madness — the inner state or outer presentation that leads others to perceive a person as mad

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 21:14 Then Achish said to his servants , “ Behold , you see the man is mad . Why then have you brought him to me ?

groups.context_description: Term names the dismissal of prophetic intensity as madness, or the inner disintegration produced by divine judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 9:7 The days of punishment have come ; the days of recompense have come ; Israel shall know it. The prophet is a fool ; the man of the spirit is mad , because of your great iniquity and great hatred .

groups.context_description: Term names madness as a condition of the inner being — whether reckless furious intensity or the madness of divine judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 28:28 The Lord will strike you with madness and blindness and confusion of mind ,

groups.context_description: Term names divine rule — God's sovereign dominion over nations, nature, and creation — engaging the inner being through awe, acknowledgement, trust, and the recognition of divine sovereignty

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 8:6 You have given him dominion over the works of your hands ; you have put all things under his feet ,

He was fed grass like an ox , and his body was wet with the dew of heaven , until he knew that the Most High God rules the kingdom of mankind and sets over it whom he will .

groups.context_description: Term names derision — the condition of being an object of mocking contempt among enemies, arising from moral-spiritual failure within the community

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 32:25 And when Moses saw that the people had broken loose (for Aaron had let them break loose , to the derision of their enemies ),

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of setting attention — directing the mind to consider, take to heart, or attend carefully to a matter or person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 7:23 Pharaoh turned and went into his house , and he did not take even this to heart .

groups.context_description: Term names the everlasting dominion of God as the object that grounds inner praise, fear, and obedience — and human dominion as the precarious condition that reveals inner pride or humility

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 4:3 How great are his signs , how mighty his wonders ! His kingdom is an everlasting kingdom , and his dominion endures from generation to generation .

Dan 4:34 At the end of the days I , Nebuchadnezzar , lifted my eyes to heaven , and my reason returned to me, and I blessed the Most High , and praised and honored him who lives forever , for his dominion is an everlasting dominion , and his kingdom endures from generation to generation ;

groups.context_description: Term names the inner orientation of heart, soul, and eyes toward detestable objects — gaze, desire, and delight directed at idolatrous things

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 11:21 But as for those whose heart goes after their detestable things and their abominations , I will bring their deeds upon their own heads , declares the Lord God .”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner character conformation that results from attachment to detestable things — the person becoming like what they love

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 9:10 Like grapes in the wilderness , I found Israel . Like the first fruit on the fig tree in its first season , I saw your fathers . But they came to Baal-peor and consecrated themselves to the thing of shame , and became detestable like the thing they loved .

groups.context_description: Term names the commanded inner revulsion — the active rejection of what is unclean as an expression of obedience and moral self-preservation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 20:25 You shall therefore separate the clean beast from the unclean , and the unclean bird from the clean . You shall not make yourselves detestable by beast or by bird or by anything with which the ground crawls , which I have set apart for you to hold unclean .

groups.context_description: Term names the reflexive self-defilement — the inner corruption that results from transgression, making the person themselves detestable

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 11:43 You shall not make yourselves detestable with any swarming thing that swarms , and you shall not defile yourselves with them, and become unclean through them .

groups.context_description: Term names divine non-revulsion toward the afflicted — God does not abhor those who suffer; affliction does not make a person detestable to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 22:24 For he has not despised or abhorred the affliction of the afflicted , and he has not hidden his face from him, but has heard , when he cried to him.

groups.context_description: Term names the objects of idolatrous inner devotion — the detestable things toward which misdirected worship is oriented

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 8:10 So I went in and saw . And there , engraved on the wall all around , was every form of creeping things and loathsome beasts , and all the idols of the house of Israel .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of recalling and turning to mind — deliberate inner movement of memory and reflection that leads to repentance, discernment, or hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lam 3:21 But this I call to mind , and therefore I have hope :

groups.context_description: Term names repentance as the inner act of turning toward God — the deliberate inner reorientation of the whole person from evil ways back to covenantal faithfulness, with all the heart and soul

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 33:11 Say to them, As I live , declares the Lord God , I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked , but that the wicked turn from his way and live ; turn back , turn back from your evil ways , for why will you die , O house of Israel ?

groups.context_description: Term names God's relenting as a divine inner-relational response to human repentance — God turning from judgment when his people turn, expressing the inner disposition of divine compassion and the withholding of wrath

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jon 3:10 When God saw what they did , how they turned from their evil way , God relented of the disaster that he had said he would do to them, and he did not do it.

groups.context_description: Meditation and inner pondering — reflective contemplation of God's works and word

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 77:3 When I remember God , I moan ; when I meditate , my spirit faints . Selah

groups.context_description: Complaint and lament as inner expression of distress

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 7:11 “ Therefore I will not restrain my mouth ; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit ; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul .

groups.context_description: Term names meditation as the sustained inner engagement with God's law and presence — the all-day practice of the devoted heart that produces understanding

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 15:4 But you are doing away with the fear of God and hindering meditation before God .
Mem Psa 119:97 Oh how I love your law ! It is my meditation all the day .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner complaint or meditation poured out before God — the sustained outpouring of anguish, petition, and reflective address that constitutes the deepest inner communication with God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 10:1 “I loathe my life ; I will give free utterance to my complaint ; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul .
Psa 142:2 I pour out my complaint before him; I tell my trouble before him.

groups.context_description: Term names profound silence as an inner-being atmosphere — the hush of awe before a significant moment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 8:1 When the Lamb opened the seventh seal , there was silence in heaven for about half an hour .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner state and act of silence — holding the voice still in the presence of power, shame, or reverence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 26:63 But Jesus remained silent . And the high priest said to him , “I adjure you by the living God , tell us if you are the Christ , the Son of God .”
Mar 4:39 And he awoke and rebuked the wind and said to the sea , “ Peace ! Be still !” And the wind ceased , and there was a great calm .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner discipline of silence — the restraint of voice in reverence, receptiveness, or ordered worship

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:28 But if there is no one to interpret , let each of them keep silent in church and speak to himself and to God .

groups.context_description: Term characterises the person as inwardly glad — the quality of heart that rejoices in God, salvation, or relational flourishing

Psa 126:3 The Lord has done great things for us; we are glad .

groups.context_description: Term characterises morally negative gladness — the inner glee of the wicked, gladness at another's shame, or perverse delight

anchor_verses.verse_text: Est 5:9 And Haman went out that day joyful and glad of heart . But when Haman saw Mordecai in the king’s gate , that he neither rose nor trembled before him, he was filled with wrath against Mordecai .
Pro 2:14 who rejoice in doing evil and delight in the perverseness of evil ,

groups.context_description: Term names joy as the defining inner quality of life before God — the gladness of heart that characterises covenant faithfulness and divine presence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 28:47 Because you did not serve the Lord your God with joyfulness and gladness of heart , because of the abundance of all things ,
Psa 4:7 You have put more joy in my heart than they have when their grain and wine abound .

Joe 1:16 Is not the food cut off before our eyes , joy and gladness from the house of our God ?

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 12:7 And there you shall eat before the Lord your God , and you shall rejoice , you and your households , in all that you undertake , in which the Lord your God has blessed you.
To the choirmaster . A Psalm of David . Psa 21:1 O Lord , in your strength the king rejoices , and in your salvation how greatly he exults !

groups.context_description: Term names morally negative rejoicing — the inner glee at another's fall, the gladness of the wicked, or the perverse delight in evil

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 31:29 “ If I have rejoiced at the ruin of him who hated me, or exulted when evil overtook him
Psa 58:10 The righteous will rejoice when he sees the vengeance ; he will bathe his feet in the blood of the wicked .

groups.context_description: Term names divine rejoicing — God's own inner delight and exultation over his people and his works

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 104:31 May the glory of the Lord endure forever ; may the Lord rejoice in his works ,

groups.context_description: Term names the sifting of the inner being by a hostile spiritual agent — the adversarial trial to which faith is subjected

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 22:31 “ Simon , Simon , behold , Satan demanded to have you , that he might sift you like wheat ,

groups.context_description: Term names the act of causing another to stumble morally — the inner-relational damage done when one person's conduct triggers moral failure in another

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:13 Therefore , if food makes my brother stumble , I will never eat meat , lest I make my brother stumble .

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 12:20 Then David arose from the earth and washed and anointed himself and changed his clothes . And he went into the house of the Lord and worshiped . He then went to his own house . And when he asked , they set food before him, and he ate .

groups.context_description: Term names the anointing of restoration — God anointing the person as the act of claiming and beautifying what had been degraded

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 16:9 Then I bathed you with water and washed off your blood from you and anointed you with oil .

groups.context_description: Term describes the plunging of the person/domain into darkness as divine judgment, producing inner anguish

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 6:23 but if your eye is bad , your whole body will be full of darkness . If then the light in you is darkness , how great is the darkness !

groups.context_description: Divine intimate friendship and confident disclosure

groups.context_description: Membership in or access to the divine council/assembly

groups.context_description: Trustworthiness in keeping intimate confidence

groups.context_description: Insight as a Spirit-endowed inner capacity — divine gift producing interpretive and cognitive power

In the days of your father , light and understanding and wisdom like the wisdom of the gods were found in him, and King Nebuchadnezzar , your father —your father the king — made him chief of the magicians , enchanters , Chaldeans , and astrologers ,

Luk 2:52 And Jesus increased in wisdom and in stature and in favor with God and man .

Zec 12:10 “And I will pour out on the house of David and the inhabitants of Jerusalem a spirit of grace and pleas for mercy , so that, when they look on me, on him whom they have pierced , they shall mourn for him, as one mourns for an only child , and weep bitterly over him, as one weeps over a firstborn .

Luk 7:13 And when the Lord saw her , he had compassion on her and said to her , “Do not weep .”

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 6:12 You are not restricted by us , but you are restricted in your own affections .

groups.context_description: Distress as existential suffering condition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 8:35 Who shall separate us from the love of Christ ? Shall tribulation , or distress , or persecution , or famine , or nakedness , or danger , or sword ?

groups.context_description: Stereoō as strengthening through faith

Act 16:5 So the churches were strengthened in the faith , and they increased in numbers daily .

groups.context_description: Weakness as the inner-being condition of limitation, vulnerability, and human frailty — the site of divine power

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 8:26 Likewise the Spirit helps us in our weakness . For we do not know what to pray for as we ought , but the Spirit himself intercedes for us with groanings too deep for words .

groups.context_description: Weak in conscience — vulnerability of the inner moral faculty

groups.context_description: Weakness in faith and conscience — the inner-being condition of limited conviction or vulnerable moral formation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 3:10 that I may know him and the power of his resurrection , and may share his sufferings , becoming like him in his death ,

groups.context_description: Term describes the person's conformity to the image and likeness of Christ — the inner and eschatological telos of the person's transformation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 8:29 For those whom he foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son , in order that he might be the firstborn among many brothers .

groups.context_description: Term names understanding as a faculty of love and spiritual orientation — the inner capacity to comprehend God's will, directed toward him in knowledge and devotion

groups.context_description: Term names understanding as the inner reception and grasp of revealed truth — the capacity to perceive insight into mystery, to receive what God gives, and to resist what God thwarts

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 3:4 When you read this , you can perceive my insight into the mystery of Christ ,

groups.context_description: Intelligence/discernment as an inner capacity — and its paradoxical limits before divine revelation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 11:25 At that time Jesus declared , “I thank you , Father , Lord of heaven and earth , that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children ;

groups.context_description: Senselessness as an inner condition — failure of understanding as a heart-state

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 1:21 For although they knew God , they did not honor him as God or give thanks to him, but they became futile in their thinking , and their foolish hearts were darkened .

groups.context_description: Understanding as an inner receptive act — the mind/heart grasping revealed truth

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 13:15 For this people’s heart has grown dull , and with their ears they can barely hear , and their eyes they have closed , lest they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears and understand with their heart and turn , and I would heal them .’

groups.context_description: Lack of understanding as an inner condition — hearts hardened against perception

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 6:52 for they did not understand about the loaves , but their hearts were hardened .

groups.context_description: Understanding as a practical inner act — discerning the will of God and one's situation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 5:17 Therefore do not be foolish , but understand what the will of the Lord is.

groups.context_description: Term names the anguish of heart that accompanies deep pastoral care — inner compression of spirit expressed in tears and written in love
