groups.context_description: Term names the collective anguish of peoples at cosmic upheaval — inner perplexity and distress as the familiar order dissolves

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 21:25 “ And there will be signs in sun and moon and stars , and on the earth distress of nations in perplexity because of the roaring of the sea and the waves ,

groups.context_description: Inner compulsion, constraint, and distress as motivating or anguishing force

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:14 For the love of Christ controls us , because we have concluded this : that one has died for all , therefore all have died ;

groups.context_description: Being seized with fear as inner affective state

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 8:37 Then all the people of the surrounding country of the Gerasenes asked him to depart from them , for they were seized with great fear . So he got into the boat and returned .

groups.context_description: Term names the conscience as inner moral witness — it bears testimony, accuses, or excuses the person regarding their conduct before God and others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 23:1 And looking intently at the council , Paul said , “ Brothers , I have lived my life before God in all good conscience up to this day .”
Rom 2:15 They show that the work of the law is written on their hearts , while their conscience also bears witness , and their conflicting thoughts accuse or even excuse them

groups.context_description: Term names the conscience as morally vulnerable — susceptible to defilement, weakening through wrong associations, or cauterisation by persistent sin

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:7 However , not all possess this knowledge . But some , through former association with idols , eat food as really offered to an idol , and their conscience , being weak , is defiled .
1Ti 4:2 through the insincerity of liars whose consciences are seared ,

groups.context_description: Term names the conscience as the interior moral foundation of godly living — holding faith and conduct with a clear or good conscience

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 13:5 Therefore one must be in subjection , not only to avoid God’s wrath but also for the sake of conscience .
1Ti 1:5 The aim of our charge is love that issues from a pure heart and a good conscience and a sincere faith .

groups.context_description: Term names the conscience as the object of Christ's redemptive purification — what the old sacrificial system could not cleanse, his blood can

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 9:14 how much more will the blood of Christ , who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without blemish to God , purify our conscience from dead works to serve the living God .
Heb 10:22 let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith , with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water .

groups.context_description: Term names a conscious inner awareness — moral complicity or cognitive recognition — that informs volitional response

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 5:2 and with his wife’s knowledge he kept back for himself some of the proceeds and brought only a part of it and laid it at the apostles ’ feet .
Act 12:12 When he realized this, he went to the house of Mary , the mother of John whose other name was Mark , where many were gathered together and were praying .

groups.context_description: Term names revolt as an inner disposition of rebellion against God — a deep turning away expressed in false teaching, corrupted speech, and covenantal betrayal

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 31:6 Turn to him from whom people have deeply revolted , O children of Israel .

groups.context_description: Term names the removal of idols and objects of false worship as an inner act of reorientation — putting away foreign gods is an expression of turning the heart back to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jos 24:14 “ Now therefore fear the Lord and serve him in sincerity and in faithfulness . Put away the gods that your fathers served beyond the River and in Egypt , and serve the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the removal or loss of inner-being realities — God's steadfast love withdrawn, the heart of stone removed, guilt and iniquity taken away — describing divine action on or loss of inner-being blessings

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 11:19 And I will give them one heart , and a new spirit I will put within them. I will remove the heart of stone from their flesh and give them a heart of flesh ,

groups.context_description: Term names the moral-volitional act of putting away inner dispositions and corrupting elements — vexation, false ways, crooked speech, evil, and perversity actively removed from the inner person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 11:10 Remove vexation from your heart , and put away pain from your body , for youth and the dawn of life are vanity .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of turning aside from God's way — the volitional departure from covenantal faithfulness expressed as apostasy, persistent sinfulness, or rebellion of the heart

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 32:8 They have turned aside quickly out of the way that I commanded them. They have made for themselves a golden calf and have worshiped it and sacrificed to it and said , ‘ These are your gods , O Israel , who brought you up out of the land of Egypt !’”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner disposition of faithfulness expressed by not turning aside — remaining on the path of God's commands as a sustained inner commitment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jos 1:7 Only be strong and very courageous , being careful to do according to all the law that Moses my servant commanded you. Do not turn from it to the right hand or to the left , that you may have good success wherever you go .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-responsive act of turning aside toward God or his revelation — directing attention and presence toward divine encounter

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 3:3 And Moses said , “I will turn aside to see this great sight , why the bush is not burned .”

groups.context_description: Term names the departure or withdrawal of God's Spirit, presence, face, or steadfast love — the inner-relational consequence of spiritual abandonment or divine withdrawal

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 16:14 Now the Spirit of the Lord departed from Saul , and a harmful spirit from the Lord tormented him.

groups.context_description: Term names the departure of inner strength or spiritual endowment — the loss of an inward empowerment that sustained the person's identity and capacity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 16:17 And he told her all his heart , and said to her, “A razor has never come upon my head , for I have been a Nazirite to God from my mother’s womb . If my head is shaved , then my strength will leave me, and I shall become weak and be like any other man .”

groups.context_description: Term describes inner moral degeneration — the corruption of a people's inner nature manifesting as turning away from God and spiritual fruitlessness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 2:21 Yet I planted you a choice vine , wholly of pure seed . How then have you turned degenerate and become a wild vine ?

groups.context_description: Term names the contempt of surrounding nations toward Israel — and God's judgment that removes and avenges that contempt

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 28:24 “And for the house of Israel there shall be no more a brier to prick or a thorn to hurt them among all their neighbors who have treated them with contempt . Then they will know that I am the Lord God .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of swerving toward the proud and the lie — the misdirection of trust that the blessed person avoids

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 40:4 Blessed is the man who makes the Lord his trust , who does not turn to the proud , to those who go astray after a lie !

groups.context_description: Term names self-discipline as an inner gift of the Spirit — the quality of a sound and controlled inner life given by God, in contrast to a spirit of fear

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ti 1:7 for God gave us a spirit not of fear but of power and love and self-control .

groups.context_description: Term names sound-mindedness as an inner quality — the rational, morally disciplined disposition of the person, whether in speech or conduct

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 26:25 But Paul said , “I am not out of my mind , most excellent Festus , but I am speaking true and rational words .

groups.context_description: Salvation of the inner person — saved by grace through faith

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 2:8 For by grace you have been saved through faith . And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God ,

groups.context_description: Healing through faith — the inner instrument of salvation in physical need

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 7:50 And he said to the woman , “ Your faith has saved you ; go in peace .”

groups.context_description: Cry for rescue — inner-being posture of dependent appeal

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 14:30 But when he saw the wind , he was afraid , and beginning to sink he cried out, “ Lord , save me .”

groups.context_description: The Savior as the object of inner-being trust, joy, and hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 1:47 and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior ,

groups.context_description: The Savior as Jesus — his identity and the inner-being transformation he brings

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 5:31 God exalted him at his right hand as Leader and Savior , to give repentance to Israel and forgiveness of sins .

groups.context_description: Salvation as God's inner-being transformative gift seen and received

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 6:17 and take the helmet of salvation , and the sword of the Spirit , which is the word of God ,

groups.context_description: Reckless inner orientation of self-squandering

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 15:13 Not many days later , the younger son gathered all he had and took a journey into a far country , and there he squandered his property in reckless living .

groups.context_description: Debauchery as the inner orientation of dissolute self-indulgence, contrasted with Spirit-filling

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 5:18 And do not get drunk with wine , for that is debauchery , but be filled with the Spirit ,

groups.context_description: Term names taste as a figure for inner discernment — the palate's capacity to test and evaluate food as the analogy for the inner faculty of testing words, truth, and the goodness of God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 34:8 Oh, taste and see that the Lord is good ! Blessed is the man who takes refuge in him !

groups.context_description: Term names the abstaining from taste as an expression of inner grief or penitence — the voluntary refusal to eat as the outward form of an inward state of mourning or contrition

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Sa 3:35 Then all the people came to persuade David to eat bread while it was yet day . But David swore , saying , “ God do so to me and more also , if I taste bread or anything else till the sun goes down !”

groups.context_description: Term names animal-like instinctual lust that cannot be restrained — used as a metaphor for Israel's insatiable craving for foreign gods

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 2:24 a wild donkey used to the wilderness , in her heat sniffing the wind ! Who can restrain her lust ? None who seek her need weary themselves ; in her month they will find her .

groups.context_description: Term names intense communal mourning — lamentation heaped upon a people under divine distress or judgment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lam 2:5 The Lord has become like an enemy ; he has swallowed up Israel ; he has swallowed up all its palaces ; he has laid in ruins its strongholds , and he has multiplied in the daughter of Judah mourning and lamentation .

groups.context_description: Term names the power that God bestows on his people — a capacity imparted to the inner being

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 68:35 Awesome is God from his sanctuary ; the God of Israel — he is the one who gives power and strength to his people . Blessed be God !

groups.context_description: Term names divine inner loathing directed specifically at pride — God abhors the arrogance of Jacob

anchor_verses.verse_text: Amo 6:8 The Lord God has sworn by himself , declares the Lord , the God of hosts : “I abhor the pride of Jacob and hate his strongholds , and I will deliver up the city and all that is in it.”

groups.context_description: Term names divine moral assessment of inner character as detestable — the wicked person's thoughts, heart, and dispositions as abomination before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 11:20 Those of crooked heart are an abomination to the Lord , but those of blameless ways are his delight .
Pro 15:26 The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the Lord , but gracious words are pure .

groups.context_description: Term names worship as abominable when it arises from a corrupted inner moral condition — the sacrifices of the wicked are detestable

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 15:8 The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord , but the prayer of the upright is acceptable to him .

groups.context_description: Term names the failure of inner shame — the conscience that can no longer feel appropriate disgrace over its own abomination

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 6:15 Were they ashamed when they committed abomination ? No , they were not at all ashamed ; they did not know how to blush . Therefore they shall fall among those who fall ; at the time that I punish them, they shall be overthrown ,” says the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names inner grief and self-loathing as response to abomination — the inner movement of sorrow over one's own detestable conduct as the beginning of restoration

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 36:31 Then you will remember your evil ways , and your deeds that were not good , and you will loathe yourselves for your iniquities and your abominations .

groups.context_description: Term names the categories of conduct that God finds abominable — violations of holiness that offend divine character and defile the person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 18:22 You shall not lie with a male as with a woman ; it is an abomination .

groups.context_description: Term names the deep inner disposition of abhorrence toward falsehood and evil — the morally alert person's visceral revulsion paired with love of what is true

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:163 I hate and abhor falsehood , but I love your law .

groups.context_description: Term names the inverted disposition of abhorrence — the capacity for moral revulsion directed against justice and truth rather than evil

anchor_verses.verse_text: Amo 5:10 They hate him who reproves in the gate , and they abhor him who speaks the truth .

groups.context_description: Term names divine sustained inner abhorrence toward those who have persistently corrupted themselves or violated covenant

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 106:40 Then the anger of the Lord was kindled against his people , and he abhorred his heritage ;

groups.context_description: All-consuming longing of the soul for God's word and rules — inner being entirely taken up with desire for the divine

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:20 My soul is consumed with longing for your rules at all times .

groups.context_description: Term describes the purity of the heart and inner person as moral and spiritual integrity before God — an inner life free from corruption and oriented toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:10 Create in me a clean heart , O God , and renew a right spirit within me.

groups.context_description: Term describes purity attributed to God's word and the fear of the Lord — the quality of inner spiritual integrity that characterises divine revelation and right orientation toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 19:9 the fear of the Lord is clean , enduring forever ; the rules of the Lord are true , and righteous altogether .

groups.context_description: Term describes the covenantal and spiritual restoration of the inner person — being made clean before God through divine action, removing inner defilement and renewing covenantal relationship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 36:25 I will sprinkle clean water on you, and you shall be clean from all your uncleannesses , and from all your idols I will cleanse you .

groups.context_description: Term describes ritual/levitical cleanness as a covenantal condition governing access to God and sacred space — the state of being clean as prerequisite for worship and community life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 10:10 You are to distinguish between the holy and the common , and between the unclean and the clean ,

groups.context_description: Term describes the purification of the inner person from sin and moral defilement — the heart and soul made pure through divine cleansing, repentance, and spiritual renewal

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:2 Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity , and cleanse me from my sin !

groups.context_description: Term describes ritual cleansing as a covenantal act of transition — the person made ritually clean through prescribed acts, enabling restored participation in sacred community

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 5:10 And Elisha sent a messenger to him, saying , “ Go and wash in the Jordan seven times , and your flesh shall be restored , and you shall be clean .”

groups.context_description: Term describes God's purifying action upon priests, Levites, and people — cleansing for restored covenantal service and communal life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mal 3:3 He will sit as a refiner and purifier of silver , and he will purify the sons of Levi and refine them like gold and silver , and they will bring offerings in righteousness to the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the prescribed purification procedure as the covenantal pathway governing a person's return to community and sacred participation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 14:2 “This shall be the law of the leprous person for the day of his cleansing . He shall be brought to the priest ,

groups.context_description: Term names purification as a covenantal service and inner-oriented commitment — the person setting their heart toward God even where external cleanness requirements are imperfectly met

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 30:19 who sets his heart to seek God , the Lord , the God of his fathers , even though not according to the sanctuary’s rules of cleanness .”

groups.context_description: Term names the ritual period of purification following childbirth — the covenantal state of the post-partum woman and her transition back to full community participation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 12:4 Then she shall continue for thirty-three days in the blood of her purifying . She shall not touch anything holy , nor come into the sanctuary , until the days of her purifying are completed .

groups.context_description: Term describes the absolute moral purity of God's character — an inner quality of divine nature that is incompatible with evil or wrongdoing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hab 1:13 You who are of purer eyes than to see evil and cannot look at wrong , why do you idly look at traitors and remain silent when the wicked swallows up the man more righteous than he ?

groups.context_description: Term names the ritual purification period as a state of cleansing — the post-partum woman's covenantal condition during and after the prescribed period of purification

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 12:4 Then she shall continue for thirty-three days in the blood of her purifying . She shall not touch anything holy , nor come into the sanctuary , until the days of her purifying are completed .

groups.context_description: Term characterizes the person as inwardly blameless and whole before God — the quality of heart and conduct that constitutes right standing and walking in God's presence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 17:1 When Abram was ninety-nine years old the Lord appeared to Abram and said to him, “ I am God Almighty ; walk before me, and be blameless ,
Psa 15:2 He who walks blamelessly and does what is right and speaks truth in his heart ;

groups.context_description: Term characterizes the person as inwardly blameless and morally whole — the quality of inner character that defines one who fears God and turns from evil

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job , and that man was blameless and upright , one who feared God and turned away from evil .
Psa 37:37 Mark the blameless and behold the upright , for there is a future for the man of peace .

groups.context_description: Term characterizes the beloved as wholly complete and perfect — the wholeness that evokes delight and singular devotion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Song 5:2 I slept , but my heart was awake . A sound ! My beloved is knocking . “ Open to me, my sister , my love , my dove , my perfect one , for my head is wet with dew , my locks with the drops of the night .”

Song 6:9 My dove , my perfect one , is the only one , the only one of her mother , pure to her who bore her. The young women saw her and called her blessed ; the queens and concubines also, and they praised her .

groups.context_description: Term names the absence of soundness as the condition of the person or people under divine discipline or moral corruption — linking physical and inner brokenness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 38:3 There is no soundness in my flesh because of your indignation ; there is no health in my bones because of my sin .

groups.context_description: Term names the Thummim as the instrument of divine disclosure of inner moral guilt — the means by which God reveals what is hidden in the person or community

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 14:41 Therefore Saul said , “O Lord God of Israel , why have you not answered your servant this day ? If this guilt is in me or in Jonathan my son , O Lord , God of Israel , give Urim . But if this guilt is in your people Israel , give Thummim .” And Jonathan and Saul were taken , but the people escaped .

groups.context_description: Term names integrity as the inner moral condition of the person — the wholeness and blamelessness of heart and conduct that characterizes the one who walks rightly before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 20:5 Did he not himself say to me, ‘ She is my sister ’? And she herself said , ‘He is my brother .’ In the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands I have done this .”
Of David . Psa 26:1 Vindicate me, O Lord , for I have walked in my integrity , and I have trusted in the Lord without wavering .

groups.context_description: Term names moral-spiritual completion or termination — the state of being blameless/complete in integrity, or the eschatological finishing of transgression and sin

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 9:24 “ Seventy weeks are decreed about your people and your holy city , to finish the transgression , to put an end to sin , and to atone for iniquity , to bring in everlasting righteousness , to seal both vision and prophet , and to anoint a most holy place .

groups.context_description: Term names ritual defilement of a person — becoming unclean through contact with death, bodily discharge, or unclean things, requiring cleansing before re-entering community and worship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 11:44 For I am the Lord your God . Consecrate yourselves therefore, and be holy , for I am holy . You shall not defile yourselves with any swarming thing that crawls on the ground .

groups.context_description: Term names moral self-defilement — the active corruption of oneself through idolatry, sexual sin, or unfaithfulness, which defiles the inner person, sacred space, and the land

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 2:23 How can you say , ‘I am not unclean , I have not gone after the Baals ’? Look at your way in the valley ; know what you have done — a restless young camel running here and there ,
Eze 20:43 And there you shall remember your ways and all your deeds with which you have defiled yourselves, and you shall loathe yourselves for all the evils that you have committed .

groups.context_description: Term names uncleanness as a condition separating the person from God and community — whether ritual impurity requiring cleansing or moral/spiritual defilement requiring divine removal

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 36:25 I will sprinkle clean water on you, and you shall be clean from all your uncleannesses , and from all your idols I will cleanse you .

groups.context_description: Term names ritual uncleanness as a condition of the person — the state of being separated from worship and community, calling for recognition and cleansing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 9:7 And those men said to him, “ We are unclean through touching a dead body . Why are we kept from bringing the Lord’s offering at its appointed time among the people of Israel ?”

groups.context_description: Term names moral uncleanness as an inner condition — the comprehensive defilement of the person before God through sin, idolatry, or universal human unworthiness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 6:5 And I said : “ Woe is me! For I am lost ; for I am a man of unclean lips , and I dwell in the midst of a people of unclean lips ; for my eyes have seen the King , the Lord of hosts !”

groups.context_description: Term names integrity as the inner quality that the person holds fast and presents before God — the moral wholeness that defines character and guides conduct under pressure

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 2:3 And the Lord said to Satan , “Have you considered my servant Job , that there is none like him on the earth , a blameless and upright man , who fears God and turns away from evil ? He still holds fast his integrity , although you incited me against him to destroy him without reason .”
Pro 11:3 The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them .

groups.context_description: Term characterises God's way, works, and knowledge as perfect — the inner perfection of divine character and action as the ground of trust

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 32:4 “The Rock , his work is perfect , for all his ways are justice . A God of faithfulness and without iniquity , just and upright is he .
Job 37:16 Do you know the balancings of the clouds , the wondrous works of him who is perfect in knowledge ,

groups.context_description: Term characterises the Torah as perfect — its completeness as the source of inner renewal of the soul

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 19:7 The law of the Lord is perfect , reviving the soul ; the testimony of the Lord is sure , making wise the simple ;

groups.context_description: Term names comfort as the inner state grounded in faithfulness to God's word and promise — the comfort that comes not from ease but from covenant integrity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 6:10 This would be my comfort ; I would even exult in pain unsparing , for I have not denied the words of the Holy One .
Psa 119:50 This is my comfort in my affliction , that your promise gives me life .

groups.context_description: Term names consolation as God's gift that cheers the soul amid inner anxiety — the divine presence that offers what no human comfort can adequately supply

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 15:11 Are the comforts of God too small for you, or the word that deals gently with you ?
Psa 94:19 When the cares of my heart are many , your consolations cheer my soul .

groups.context_description: Term names lowliness/humility as an inner quality of the person — a settled inner disposition of not elevating oneself, which God values and responds to with grace and exaltation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 11:29 Take my yoke upon you , and learn from me , for I am gentle and lowly in heart , and you will find rest for your souls .

groups.context_description: Term names humility as an inner quality of the renewed person — the disposition that re-orients self-estimation, governs communal relations, and characterises service to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility count others more significant than yourselves.

groups.context_description: Term names false or counterfeit humility — the outward performance of self-abasement without genuine inner transformation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Col 2:23 These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting self-made religion and asceticism and severity to the body , but they are of no value in stopping the indulgence of the flesh .

groups.context_description: Term names the insensitive heart — rendered unfeeling like fat, incapable of genuine response to God or his word

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 119:70 their heart is unfeeling like fat , but I delight in your law .

groups.context_description: Term names tearing as the expression of violent inner states — divine wrath, human predatory character, destructive anger — and the experience of being torn as what the inner person suffers under judgment or hostile power

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 7:2 lest like a lion they tear my soul apart, rending it in pieces, with none to deliver .
Hos 6:1 “ Come , let us return to the Lord ; for he has torn us, that he may heal us; he has struck us down, and he will bind us up.

groups.context_description: Term names prey in its inner-being dimension — the predatory inner character of unjust leaders and oppressors, God's fierce resolve to fight for his people, and his covenantal provision for those who fear him

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 111:5 He provides food for those who fear him; he remembers his covenant forever .

Isa 31:4 For thus the Lord said to me, “As a lion or a young lion growls over his prey , and when a band of shepherds is called out against him he is not terrified by their shouting or daunted at their noise , so the Lord of hosts will come down to fight on Mount Zion and on its hill .

groups.context_description: Term names the torn animal as the boundary that tests inner integrity — the person who refuses defilement, bears losses rather than shifting blame, or whose predatory character fills its dens with what it has seized

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 31:39 What was torn by wild beasts I did not bring to you. I bore the loss of it myself. From my hand you required it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night .

Eze 4:14 Then I said , “ Ah , Lord God ! Behold , I have never defiled myself . From my youth up till now I have never eaten what died of itself or was torn by beasts, nor has tainted meat come into my mouth .”

groups.context_description: Term names the fresh-plucked olive leaf as the sign of renewed hope — the communicative act that produces inner recognition of grace and new beginning

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 8:11 And the dove came back to him in the evening , and behold , in her mouth was a freshly plucked olive leaf . So Noah knew that the waters had subsided from the earth .

groups.context_description: Term names a command issued with authority — the inner being engaged through apostolic commission, divine mandate, or the distinction between command and appeal

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 1:1 Paul , an apostle of Christ Jesus by command of God our Savior and of Christ Jesus our hope ,
Tit 2:15 Declare these things ; exhort and rebuke with all authority . Let no one disregard you .

groups.context_description: Term names a commanding act that evokes inner responses of awe, submission, or dread — the authority behind the command engaging the inner being of those who witness or receive it

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 1:27 And they were all amazed , so that they questioned among themselves , saying , “ What is this ? A new teaching with authority ! He commands even the unclean spirits , and they obey him .”
Luk 8:25 He said to them , “ Where is your faith ?” And they were afraid , and they marveled , saying to one another , “Who then is this , that he commands even winds and water , and they obey him ?”

groups.context_description: Term names a directive command that shapes the inner orientation of those under it — calling forth obedience, vocation, or acknowledgement of divine order

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 17:9 Does he thank the servant because he did what was commanded ?
1Cor 7:17 Only let each person lead the life that the Lord has assigned to him , and to which God has called him. This is my rule in all the churches .

groups.context_description: Term names divine appointment or ordering — the inner being engaged through calling, commission, election, and submission to divinely-appointed authority

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 13:48 And when the Gentiles heard this, they began rejoicing and glorifying the word of the Lord , and as many as were appointed to eternal life believed .
Rom 13:1 Let every person be subject to the governing authorities . For there is no authority except from God , and those that exist have been instituted by God .

groups.context_description: Term names a divinely-ordained arrangement or ordinance — the inner being engaged through reception of or resistance to God's appointed order

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 7:53 you who received the law as delivered by angels and did not keep it.”

groups.context_description: Term names a royal edict whose threat is met by faith — the inner being engaged through the choice to fear God rather than the king's command

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 11:23 By faith Moses , when he was born , was hidden for three months by his parents , because they saw that the child was beautiful , and they were not afraid of the king’s edict .

groups.context_description: "How long?" — the inner-being cry of anguish, longing, and tested patience

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 6:11 Then I said , “How long , O Lord ?” And he said : “ Until cities lie waste without inhabitant , and houses without people , and the land is a desolate waste ,

groups.context_description: Term names Israel's spiritual fornication — the inner disposition of disordered desire driving pursuit of foreign gods and alliances, defiling the covenantal relationship with God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 4:30 When you are in tribulation , and all these things come upon you in the latter days , you will return to the Lord your God and obey his voice .

anchor_verses.verse_text: Neh 9:27 Therefore you gave them into the hand of their enemies , who made them suffer . groups.context_description: Term names anguish as a reliable divine refuge — the God who is found present precisely in times of distress

anchor_verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster . Of the Sons of Korah . According to Alamoth . A Song . Psa 46:1 God is our refuge and strength , a very present help in trouble .

groups.context_description: Term names the distress of the righteous as the condition from which God delivers — anguish as the ground of divine rescue

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 7:7 And he made the Hall of the Throne where he was to pronounce judgment, even the Hall of Judgment . It was finished with cedar from floor to rafters .

groups.context_description: Term names the pre-fall state of nakedness without shame — the person's original inner freedom from guilt and fear before God and the other

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 7:11 God is a righteous judge , and a God who feels indignation every day .

groups.context_description: Term names human indignation as responsive inner emotion — the emotional-moral reaction of moral displeasure arising from provocation or perceived injustice

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 24:24 Whoever says to the wicked , “You are in the right ,” will be cursed by peoples , abhorred by nations ,
Pro 25:23 The north wind brings forth rain , and a backbiting tongue , angry looks .

groups.context_description: Term names divine indignation as purposive instrument of judgment — God's inner wrath deployed purposively against wickedness and idolatry

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 10:5 Woe to Assyria , the rod of my anger ; the staff in their hands is my fury !

groups.context_description: Term names the prophet's inner state as divinely imparted indignation — God's own indignation placed within the person called to speak for him

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 15:17 I did not sit in the company of revelers , nor did I rejoice ; I sat alone , because your hand was upon me, for you had filled me with indignation .

groups.context_description: Term names human malicious scheming — inner plotting directed harmfully against another

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 21:27 “ Behold , I know your thoughts and your schemes to wrong me.

groups.context_description: Term names moral corruption as an inner quality expressed outwardly in conduct

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 11:15 What right has my beloved in my house , when she has done many vile deeds ? Can even sacrificial flesh avert your doom ? Can you then exult ?

groups.context_description: Term names divine purposing — resolute inner intention of God, explicitly located in heart and mind

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 42:2 “I know that you can do all things , and that no purpose of yours can be thwarted .
Jer 23:20 The anger of the Lord will not turn back until he has executed and accomplished the intents of his heart . In the latter days you will understand it clearly .

groups.context_description: Term names the formation of purposeful intention in a human subject — what the mind resolves to do

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 11:6 And the Lord said , “Behold, they are one people , and they have all one language , and this is only the beginning of what they will do . And nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.

groups.context_description: Term names resolute divine purposing — intention formed and carried through without relenting

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 4:28 “ For this the earth shall mourn , and the heavens above be dark ; for I have spoken ; I have purposed ; I have not relented , nor will I turn back .”

groups.context_description: Term names the inner purposeful scheming of the wicked — a deliberate inner plan conceived and pursued against others, contrasted with God's protective purpose

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 140:8 Grant not , O Lord , the desires of the wicked ; do not further their evil plot , or they will be exalted ! Selah

groups.context_description: Term names the act of singing praise as a whole-person inner orientation — heart, soul, spirit, and voice directed toward God in musical worship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 57:7 My heart is steadfast , O God , my heart is steadfast ! I will sing and make melody !
A Song . A Psalm of David . Psa 108:1 My heart is steadfast , O God ! I will sing and make melody with all my being !

groups.context_description: Term names song as the inner-being expression of orientation toward God — the statutes, the praises, and the voice from the night all flowing from the inner life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 35:10 But none says , ‘ Where is God my Maker , who gives songs in the night ,
Psa 119:54 Your statutes have been my songs in the house of my sojourning .

groups.context_description: Term names the psalm/song as a designated form of inner-being expression — the compositional act of directing one's whole inner life toward God in structured musical-poetic form

anchor_verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster : according to The Doe of the Dawn . A Psalm of David . Psa 22:1 My God , my God , why have you forsaken me? Why are you so far from saving me, from the words of my groaning ?
A Psalm of David . Psa 23:1 The Lord is my shepherd ; I shall not want .

groups.context_description: Term names music as the instrument of coerced worship — the signal that tests whether the inner being will orient toward the true God or capitulate to false worship under threat

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 3:15 Now if you are ready when you hear the sound of the horn , pipe , lyre , trigon , harp , bagpipe , and every kind of music , to fall down and worship the image that I have made , well and good. But if you do not worship , you shall immediately be cast into a burning fiery furnace . And who is the god who will deliver you out of my hands ?”

groups.context_description: Term names melody as the vehicle of inner-being worship — both its proper expression and the failure of melody when detached from inner integrity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 51:3 For the Lord comforts Zion ; he comforts all her waste places and makes her wilderness like Eden , her desert like the garden of the Lord ; joy and gladness will be found in her, thanksgiving and the voice of song .
Amo 5:23 Take away from me the noise of your songs ; to the melody of your harps I will not listen .

groups.context_description: Term names the Lord himself as the inner song of the redeemed — the person's strength and salvation expressed as song, God being the content of that song

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 15:2 The Lord is my strength and my song , and he has become my salvation ; this is my God , and I will praise him , my father’s God , and I will exalt him .

groups.context_description: Whoredom as a spiritual condition — the spirit of whoredom within, leading Israel astray

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 5:4 Their deeds do not permit them to return to their God . For the spirit of whoredom is within them, and they know not the Lord .

groups.context_description: Spiritual whoredom — Israel/Judah "whoring after" foreign gods, forsaking the Lord; covenantal heart-departure

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 1:2 When the Lord first spoke through Hosea , the Lord said to Hosea , “ Go , take to yourself a wife of whoredom and have children of whoredom , for the land commits great whoredom by forsaking the Lord .”

groups.context_description: "A spirit of whoredom" — whoredom as a driving inner disposition and inner-being faculty

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 4:12 My people inquire of a piece of wood , and their walking staff gives them oracles . For a spirit of whoredom has led them astray , and they have left their God to play the whore .

groups.context_description: "Your heart and your own eyes, which you are inclined to whore after" — the inner mechanism of desire as the root of whoredom

anchor_verses.verse_text: Num 15:39 And it shall be a tassel for you to look at and remember all the commandments of the Lord , to do them, not to follow after your own heart and your own eyes , which you are inclined to whore after .

groups.context_description: Literal fornication as inner-being act — the will and desire engaged in sexual unfaithfulness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 7:10 And behold , the woman meets him, dressed as a prostitute , wily of heart .

groups.context_description: Ezekiel's "whoring heart" allegory — inner-being characterisation of Jerusalem/Israel's spiritual condition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 6:9 then those of you who escape will remember me among the nations where they are carried captive , how I have been broken over their whoring heart that has departed from me and over their eyes that go whoring after their idols . And they will be loathsome in their own sight for the evils that they have committed , for all their abominations .

groups.context_description: Whoredom as inner-being corruption — defilement of the relational space with God; the act that takes away understanding

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hos 4:11 whoredom , wine , and new wine , which take away the understanding .

groups.context_description: Term used in moral sowing metaphors — sowing righteousness or injustice as inner character dispositions that determine what is reaped

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 11:18 The wicked earns deceptive wages , but one who sows righteousness gets a sure reward .

groups.context_description: Term used of God's sowing of inner states — light, joy, restoration, re-planting as covenantal love

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 97:11 Light is sown for the righteous , and joy for the upright in heart .

groups.context_description: Term names sowing in tears — grief-laden faithful labour as an inner experience anticipating joyful harvest

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 126:5 Those who sow in tears shall reap with shouts of joy !

groups.context_description: Term names the offspring as bearer of covenantal enmity — primordial hostility between lines establishing inner orientation of antagonism

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 3:15 I will put enmity between you and the woman , and between your offspring and her offspring ; he shall bruise your head , and you shall bruise his heel .”

groups.context_description: Term names inner longing for offspring — the grief of childlessness as a deep inner yearning

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 15:3 And Abram said , “ Behold , you have given me no offspring , and a member of my household will be my heir .”

groups.context_description: Term names offspring defined by inner moral character — godly, holy, or corrupt seed whose spiritual quality is their defining mark

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mal 2:15 Did he not make them one , with a portion of the Spirit in their union? And what was the one God seeking ? Godly offspring . So guard yourselves in your spirit , and let none of you be faithless to the wife of your youth .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner transformation of offspring — the Spirit poured on descendants; heart of offspring circumcised to love God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 30:6 And the Lord your God will circumcise your heart and the heart of your offspring , so that you will love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul , that you may live .

groups.context_description: Term names seed as emblem of hope carried through grief — the weeping sower who shall return with joy

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 126:6 He who goes out weeping , bearing the seed for sowing , shall come home with shouts of joy , bringing his sheaves with him.

groups.context_description: Term names seed as metaphor for inner moral character and peace sown as inner-community state

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 2:21 Yet I planted you a choice vine , wholly of pure seed . How then have you turned degenerate and become a wild vine ?

groups.context_description: Term used in metaphor of divine sowing — God causing righteousness and praise to sprout; naming inner character qualities he cultivates

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 11:37 And if any part of their carcass falls upon any seed grain that is to be sown , it is clean ,

groups.context_description: Term names vegetables chosen as alternative to defilement — inner moral conviction of refusing to compromise faithfulness to God through dietary integrity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 1:12 “ Test your servants for ten days ; let us be given vegetables to eat and water to drink .

groups.context_description: Term names provision given in response to faithfulness — the honouring of moral integrity through the granting of the chosen diet

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 1:16 So the steward took away their food and the wine they were to drink , and gave them vegetables .

groups.context_description: Earnest desire and eager zeal — the inner longing for what is good and spiritual

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 12:31 But earnestly desire the higher gifts . And I will show you a still more excellent way .

groups.context_description: Jealousy and covetous desire — inner hostility and consuming want

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 4:2 You desire and do not have , so you murder . You covet and cannot obtain , so you fight and quarrel . You do not have , because you do not ask .

groups.context_description: Zeal as passionate inner devotion — consuming fervour for God and his cause

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 2:17 His disciples remembered that it was written , “ Zeal for your house will consume me .”

groups.context_description: Jealousy as inner hostility — envy, rivalry, and divisive passion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 3:14 But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your hearts , do not boast and be false to the truth .

groups.context_description: Divine jealousy — God's passionate inner claim on his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:2 For I feel a divine jealousy for you , since I betrothed you to one husband , to present you as a pure virgin to Christ .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner orientation of the will and desire directed toward God, his kingdom, or righteousness — the commanded posture of the disciple

anchor_verses.verse_text: Col 3:1 If then you have been raised with Christ , seek the things that are above , where Christ is , seated at the right hand of God .

groups.context_description: Term exposes the inward misdirection of desire toward self-interest, self-glory, or human approval in contrast to God-oriented orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 7:18 The one who speaks on his own authority seeks his own glory ; but the one who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true , and in him there is no falsehood .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner movement of the person toward God himself — in longing, worship, encounter, or relational desire

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 4:23 But the hour is coming , and is now here, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth , for the Father is seeking such people to worship him .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner demand or desire for external confirmation — seeking a sign, proof, or wisdom as a substitute for trust

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:22 For Jews demand signs and Greeks seek wisdom ,

groups.context_description: Term names the inward intention of hostility, betrayal, or lethal opposition expressed through purposeful directed action

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 26:16 And from that moment he sought an opportunity to betray him .

groups.context_description: Term names the paradox of inner self-preservation instinct and its inversion — the desire to preserve life as the condition of losing it, or to seek death when life is unbearable

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 17:33 Whoever seeks to preserve his life will lose it , but whoever loses his life will keep it .

groups.context_description: Seeking after earthly or invalid things — misdirected inner orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 8:12 And he sighed deeply in his spirit and said , “ Why does this generation seek a sign ? Truly , I say to you , no sign will be given to this generation .”

groups.context_description: Seeking what is truly good — inner-being orientation toward the word and the heavenly

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 11:14 For people who speak thus make it clear that they are seeking a homeland .

groups.context_description: Sheer terror as the inner response to understanding divine judgement — the trembling dread that arises from comprehension of the divine message

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 28:19 As often as it passes through it will take you; for morning by morning it will pass through , by day and by night ; and it will be sheer terror to understand the message .

groups.context_description: Term names God's sovereign, active power — expressed through the outstretched arm — by which he delivers, redeems, judges, and rules in history

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 51:9 Awake , awake , put on strength , O arm of the Lord ; awake , as in days of old , the generations of long ago . Was it not you who cut Rahab in pieces, who pierced the dragon ?
Isa 53:1 Who has believed what he has heard from us ? And to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed ?

groups.context_description: Term names the arm as the measure of human or national power and capacity — including its moral dimension: the wicked person's power to harm, and the powerless person's helplessness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 40:9 Have you an arm like God , and can you thunder with a voice like his?
Psa 37:17 For the arms of the wicked shall be broken , but the Lord upholds the righteous .

groups.context_description: Term expresses the inner orientation of trust — where the arm of flesh names the object of misplaced human confidence, contrasted with the arm of God as the right object of trust

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 44:3 for not by their own sword did they win the land , nor did their own arm save them, but your right hand and your arm , and the light of your face , for you delighted in them .
Jer 17:5 Thus says the Lord : “ Cursed is the man who trusts in man and makes flesh his strength , whose heart turns away from the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term expresses God's tender, nurturing arm — gathering, carrying, and sustaining the vulnerable; the arm as the posture of compassionate holding and refuge

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 33:27 The eternal God is your dwelling place , and underneath are the everlasting arms . And he thrust out the enemy before you and said , ‘ Destroy .’
Isa 40:11 He will tend his flock like a shepherd ; he will gather the lambs in his arms ; he will carry them in his bosom , and gently lead those that are with young .

groups.context_description: Term names arrogance/presumption as an inner-being disposition that deceives, produces strife, and leads to downfall — the pride of heart that sets itself above God and others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 11:2 When pride comes , then comes disgrace , but with the humble is wisdom .
Obd 3 The pride of your heart has deceived you, you who live in the clefts of the rock , in your lofty dwelling , who say in your heart , “ Who will bring me down to the ground ?”

groups.context_description: Term names the pride of the hardened heart — the inner condition of self-exaltation that leads directly to divine removal of glory and authority

anchor_verses.verse_text: Dan 5:20 But when his heart was lifted up and his spirit was hardened so that he dealt proudly , he was brought down from his kingly throne , and his glory was taken from him .

terms.transliteration: hhho ōn kai o ēn kai o erchomenos

groups.context_description: Inner-being identity and relational status — "I am," "we are," being named in relation to God, sin, grace, the flesh

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 7:14 For we know that the law is spiritual , but I am of the flesh , sold under sin .

groups.context_description: No one righteous, no one seeks — the universal inner-being condition of humanity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 3:10 as it is written : “None is righteous , no, not one ;

groups.context_description: The word is near, in your mouth and in your heart; hope, conviction, calling — inner-being states named through "being"

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 10:8 But what does it say ? “The word is near you , in your mouth and in your heart ” (that is , the word of faith that we proclaim );
